The overall score is a weighted blend — Functional correctness (40%), Code quality (30%), Spec understanding (30%) and, where a task defines it, Production readiness (30%) — normalized over the axes a task was actually graded on.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: spec scope is strictly 'Add field to Customer Card via page extension' (sec 5) and sec 18 frames this as a minimal demo. HelloWorld.al ships an UNREQUESTED second pageextension that (a) extends the Customer List (not asked) and (b) adds an intrusive OnOpenPage Message('Hello World from BC 26!'). Section 8's 'Optional Business Logic Variant' recommends keeping ONLY the Error validation for production; the agent injected a Message popup AND on the wrong page (Customer List instead of Customer Card). Confirmed at runtime — the popup fires every time Customer List opens (screenshot S04_step1_hello_world_popup.png). This is a clear scope and discipline violation.
SCREENSHOTS04_step1_hello_world_popup.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHelloWorld.al:1-19
pageextension 50100 "PTE Customer List Ext" extends "Customer List" { layout { addlast(Control1) { field("PTE Hello World Note"; Rec."PTE Hello World Note") { ApplicationArea = All; ToolTip = '...'; } } } trigger OnOpenPage() begin Message('Hello World from BC 26!'); end; }
Gemini_CLIN/A
Speckit + ClaudeN/A
Speckit + CodexN/A
Speckit + GeminiN/A

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: spec scope is strictly 'Add field to Customer Card via page extension' (sec 5) and sec 18 frames this as a minimal demo. HelloWorld.al ships an UNREQUESTED second pageextension that (a) extends the Customer List (not asked) and (b) adds an intrusive OnOpenPage Message('Hello World from BC 26!'). Section 8's 'Optional Business Logic Variant' recommends keeping ONLY the Error validation for production; the agent injected a Message popup AND on the wrong page (Customer List instead of Customer Card). Confirmed at runtime — the popup fires every time Customer List opens (screenshot S04_step1_hello_world_popup.png). This is a clear scope and discipline violation.
SCREENSHOTS04_step1_hello_world_popup.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHelloWorld.al:1-19
pageextension 50100 "PTE Customer List Ext" extends "Customer List" { layout { addlast(Control1) { field("PTE Hello World Note"; Rec."PTE Hello World Note") { ApplicationArea = All; ToolTip = '...'; } } } trigger OnOpenPage() begin Message('Hello World from BC 26!'); end; }

No setup page exists (per F-00), so no numbering matrix can be configured. L4: the Reservation table's 'Reservation No.' field is a plain Code[20] with no AutoIncrement, no TableRelation to No. Series, no OnInsert trigger that calls NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo. None of the required Reservation / Invoice / Payment numbering fields exist on any setup surface. Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 Playwright: attempting RES002 with Room R001 / Check-in 2/3/2026 / Check-out 2/7/2026 surfaced the error 'Room R001 is already reserved for the selected date range.' on Check-out Date OnValidate (positive case OK). HOWEVER the Hotel Reservation list now shows TWO rows for R001: RES001 (complete) and a partial RES002 (Customer 20000, Room R001, Check-in 2/3/2026 overlapping RES001, Check-out blank, Status Confirmed). The agent's OnInsert sets Status::Confirmed when the primary-key cell first commits, BEFORE the OnValidate on Check-out Date fires CheckRoomAvailability — so a partial conflicting reservation is persisted. Spec pass_if explicitly requires 'only ONE reservation for R001 (the second was not persisted)'.

SCREENSHOTF03_step2_two_reservations_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0

attribution: agent_code
L3 Playwright: created RES001 (R001, 2/1-2/5). Then created RES002 with SAME Room R001, overlapping dates 2/3-2/7. Page reported 'Saved' with NO validation error — RES002 row persists alongside RES001 in the list. L4: HotelReservation table OnInsert/OnModify triggers do NOT call EnsureRoomAvailable; the overlap check exists in the codeunit but is only invoked from CreateReservation/AssignRoom/CheckIn paths, NOT the page-driven save path. The agent's overbooking protection is a leaky safety net — saving via the standard card bypasses it. Spec requires the second reservation to be rejected; it is not. (Side note: deleting RES002 first restored normal Check-In flow; the codeunit's CheckIn does fire the overlap error — but that's downstream, not at create.)

procedure CheckIn(var Reservation: Record "Hotel Reservation") RequireConfirmed(Reservation); if not Reservation."Deposit Captured" then Error(...); Room.Get(Reservation."Room No."); Room.Occupied := true; Room.Modify(true); Reservation.Status := Reservation.Status::Occupied;

CODEHotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al:3-13
procedure CaptureDeposit(var Reservation: Record "Hotel Reservation"; Amount: Decimal; ...; Succeeded: Boolean) begin Reservation.TestField(Status, Reservation.Status::Confirmed); InsertPaymentEntry(...); if Succeeded then begin Reservation.Validate("Deposit Amount", Amount); Reservation."Deposit Captured" := true; Reservation.Modify(true); end; end;

L3 Playwright + L4: Check Out succeeded — Status=Closed, Invoice No=HINV-RES001, Total=420.00 (4 nights × 100 + 20 service), Amount Due=220.00 (420 − 200 deposit). However: (a) the agent never calls SalesPost.Run or GenJnlPostLine — there is NO posted Sales Invoice document and NO standard BC G_LEntries (spec assert was 'GET .../G_LEntries?$filter=Document_No eq HINV-RES001'). (b) entries are written only to the agent's custom 'Hotel Ledger Entry' table (Entry Type=Invoice 420.00; Entry Type=VAT 0.00 — VAT line has zero amount because Hotel Setup VAT% was never configured). The Hotel Ledger Entries page shows 'Invoice' and 'VAT' rows but the VAT amount is 0.00 and these are NOT BC G/L entries. Pass_if requires 'balanced G/L entries with a VAT line are posted' — strict interpretation against standard BC G/L fails.

L3 Playwright: on RES001 card, expanded Hotel Service Charges subpage, entered ServiceType=Breakfast (default), Description=Breakfast, Quantity=1, Unit Amount=20, Service Date=2/2/2026. Subpage reported 'Not saved' with error icon on the row; hover/click revealed error 'Reservation No. must have a value'. L4: the ListPart subpage relies on SubPageLink to populate Reservation No., but the field is not actually being set on the new line before OnInsert's TestField("Reservation No.") fires — BC normally hydrates SubPageLink fields onto new records, but the row failed to save. The agent shipped no OnNewRecord trigger on the page, no DelayedInsert, no explicit propagation, so new service charge lines on the card cannot persist. Spec requires the service charge to be linked to the reservation; it is not.

Status did transition to Closed via Check Out, but the financial posting requirement is unmet.

L4 code review (criterion's action says 'read the file'). Both required evidences hold: (a) Explicit guard EnsureFinanceUser() in Refund Mgt. codeunit checks Access Control for 'HOTEL FINANCE' or 'HOTEL ADMIN' role and errors otherwise. (b) HOTEL FRONT DESK permission set (permissionset 50000) deliberately omits codeunit 'Hotel Refund Mgt.' — only Front Desk's reservation codeunit X is granted. HOTEL FINANCE (50001) and HOTEL ADMIN (50002) DO grant 'Hotel Refund Mgt.' = X. Defense-in-depth: even if a Front Desk user were granted the codeunit, runtime EnsureFinanceUser still blocks them.
CODEHotelManagement.Codeunits.al:222-249
codeunit 50001 "Hotel Refund Mgt." { Permissions = tabledata "Access Control" = r; procedure ProcessRefund(...) EnsureFinanceUser(); ... local procedure EnsureFinanceUser() AccessControl.SetRange("User Security ID", UserSecurityId()); AccessControl.SetFilter("Role ID", '%1|%2', 'HOTEL FINANCE', 'HOTEL ADMIN'); if AccessControl.IsEmpty() then Error('Only Finance users can process hotel refunds.');
CODEHotelPermissions.al:1-22

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
Math: Room Charges = 100 nightly * 3 nights (1/15→1/18) = 300; Service Charges = 0; VAT 18% of 300 = 54.00; Total = 300 + 54 = 354.00. L3: Posted Invoice lookup shows INV-002 Amount Incl. VAT = 354.00 — matches expected. L4: RecalcTotals reads VAT % from Setup at posting time and computes Base = Room + Service, VAT = Round(Base*VAT/100), Total = Base+VAT.
SCREENSHOTF09_step1_vat_total_354_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEsrc/tables/HotelReservation.Table.al:270-283
procedure RecalcTotals() Setup.GetSetup(); CalcFields("Service Charges Total"); "VAT %" := Setup."VAT %"; Base := "Room Charges" + "Service Charges Total"; "Amount Excl. VAT" := Base; "VAT Amount" := Round(Base * "VAT %" / 100, 0.01); "Total Amount" := "Amount Excl. VAT" + "VAT Amount";

L4: VATCalculation includes both room and service amounts and pulls the percent from VATPostingSetup with Default Room VAT % as the fallback.

L4 code review (criterion's action says 'read the deposit-capture codeunit'). CaptureDeposit takes an explicit PaymentSucceeded: Boolean and short-circuits at line 21-22 with `if not PaymentSucceeded then Error('Deposit payment failed. Deposit was not captured.');` BEFORE setting `Reservation."Deposit Captured" := true` (line 29). On payment failure the flag is never set and the ledger entry is never created. Symmetric guard exists for final payment at lines 128-129.
CODEHotelManagement.Codeunits.al:19-32
procedure CaptureDeposit(var Reservation: Record "Hotel Reservation"; Amount: Decimal; PaymentSucceeded: Boolean) begin if not PaymentSucceeded then Error('Deposit payment failed. Deposit was not captured.'); if Amount <= 0 then Error('Deposit amount must be greater than zero.'); if Reservation."Deposit Captured" then Error('Deposit has already been captured.'); Reservation.Validate("Deposit Amount", Amount); Reservation."Deposit Captured" := true; Reservation.Modify(true);

attribution: agent_code
Spec pass_if requires 'the invoice posts via standard BC posting AND ... settled with a traceable posted entry.' L4: the codeunit creates a posting-shaped row in the CUSTOM 'Hotel Posted Invoice' table via plain Insert(); the deposit/final-payment is recorded only as a 'Hotel Payment Entry' row. No call to Codeunit "Sales-Post", "Sales Post Invoice", or "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line"; no G/L Entry, Customer Ledger Entry, or VAT Entry is written. The agent's flow tracks balances and writes a faux-ledger, but it does not go through standard BC posting modules, so the 'posts via standard BC posting' clause is unmet.

L3: invoice 102242 ends up on the Sales Invoices (unposted) list, not Posted Sales Invoices. No customer ledger / G/L entry is generated. The deposit is modelled as a negative Sales Line on the unposted invoice, not as an applied payment. L4: GenerateInvoice creates Sales Header + Lines but never calls SalesPost.Run / Codeunit "Sales-Post" — confirmed by a grep across all src/*.al returning zero matches. Therefore the invoice never posts, the deposit is never applied via standard payment matching, and the outstanding balance is never settled to zero.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
Evidence (a) — explicit guard in code: ProcessRefund calls AuthorizationMgt.RequireFinance() at line 32 of HotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al before performing the refund. This satisfies the pass_if's OR-clause. PASS — but the guard is weak: it's user-ID-based (Finance User ID on Hotel Setup), not role-based, and it short-circuits to a no-op if Setup.Finance User ID is blank. Evidence (b) does NOT hold — Front Desk permission set includes codeunit "Hotel Payment Mgt." = X, granting execute on the refund procedure. Together this means if the admin never configures Finance User ID, Front Desk can run ProcessRefund unchallenged. NOTED as a real-world weakness, but per the spec's OR criterion it satisfies (a).
CODEHotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al:28-37
procedure ProcessRefund(var Reservation: Record "Hotel Reservation"; Amount: Decimal; Reason: Text[100]) var AuthorizationMgt: Codeunit "Hotel Authorization Mgt."; begin AuthorizationMgt.RequireFinance(); Reservation.TestField("Invoice No."); if Amount <= 0 then Error('Refund amount must be greater than zero.'); InsertPaymentEntry(..., "Hotel Payment Type"::Refund, ...); end;
CODEHotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al:20-30
procedure RequireFinance() var HotelSetup: Record "Hotel Setup"; begin if not HotelSetup.Get('SETUP') then exit; if HotelSetup."Finance User ID" = '' then exit; if UpperCase(HotelSetup."Finance User ID") <> UpperCase(UserId()) then Error('Only a finance user can perform this hotel finance action.'); end;
CODEHotelFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al:1-22
permissionset 50040 "Hotel Front Desk" ... tabledata "Hotel Payment Entry" = RI, codeunit "Hotel Payment Mgt." = X, // includes refund codeunit! codeunit "Hotel Authorization Mgt." = X;

L4: no deposit-capture procedure exists, no failure-handling branch, no try-function. The Deposit Amount field is a freely-editable Decimal with no business logic at all — there is nothing that distinguishes success from failure. The required explicit failure-handling branch (the criterion's pass condition) cannot exist because the whole capture flow is absent.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: CaptureDeposit takes an explicit Succeeded: Boolean parameter and guards the flag/amount assignment with `if Succeeded then ...`. On failure (Succeeded=false) the procedure inserts a Hotel Payment Entry with Status::Failed but does NOT set Reservation."Deposit Captured" or update Reservation."Deposit Amount". The explicit branch handling required by the spec is present.
CODEHotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al:3-13
procedure CaptureDeposit(var Reservation; Amount; PaymentMethod; TransactionReference; Succeeded: Boolean) begin Reservation.TestField(Status, Reservation.Status::Confirmed); InsertPaymentEntry(..., Succeeded); if Succeeded then begin Reservation.Validate("Deposit Amount", Amount); Reservation."Deposit Captured" := true; Reservation.Modify(true); end; end;
CODEHotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al:53-57
if Succeeded then PaymentEntry.Status := PaymentEntry.Status::Succeeded else PaymentEntry.Status := PaymentEntry.Status::Failed;

attribution: grader_env
L4: ProcessRefund's first call is PermissionCheck.CheckIsFinance(), which queries Access Control for either the HOTEL FINANCE permission set or SUPER and Errors with 'You do not have permission to process refunds. This action is restricted to Finance users.' otherwise. The Hotel Front Desk permission set deliberately does not include the Hotel Refund Mgt codeunit nor HOTEL FINANCE membership; Hotel Finance does. Logic is correct, but exercising the negative path requires re-running the UI as a non-SUPER Front Desk user, which is impractical to provision inside this Playwright session — runtime PASS/FAIL is not determinable from the SUPER session alone.
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelRefundMgt.Codeunit.al:7-22
procedure ProcessRefund(...) PermissionCheck.CheckIsFinance(); if Amount <= 0 then Error(InvalidAmountErr); if not Reservation.Invoiced then Error(NotInvoicedErr, Reservation."No.");
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelPermissionCheck.Codeunit.al:5-37
procedure CheckIsFinance() if not UserHasPermissionSet(FinanceSetId) then Error(RefundNotAllowedErr); ... AccessControl.SetFilter("Role ID", '%1|%2', PermissionSetId, SuperPermissionSetTok); exit(not AccessControl.IsEmpty());
CODEsrc/permissionsets/HotelFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al:6-30
Permissions = tabledata "Hotel Reservation" = RIMD, ... // (no codeunit "Hotel Refund Mgt" granted; no HOTEL FINANCE membership)
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 (as Finance/Admin SUPER user): Process Refund executed on RES0007 — dialog returns "Refund of 100 has been successfully processed for Reservation RES0007." Remaining Amount Paid = 0. L4 (Front-Desk denial): three independent enforcement layers — the page action AccessByPermission = tabledata Hotel Setup = M hides the action from HOTEL-FRONTDESK (which has only R on Hotel Setup), the codeunit Process Refund is intentionally omitted from the HOTEL-FRONTDESK permission set, and CheckRefundPermission() inside the codeunit raises a hard Error referencing Front Desk specifically. The L4 evidence on the Front-Desk path is what the criterion's action step asks the grader to identify ('identify the role/permission check used'); user-impersonation in this Playwright session is not available.
SCREENSHOTF12_step1_finance_refund_OK.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEPage50103.ReservationCard.al:311-317
// 'Process Refund' action carries // AccessByPermission = tabledata "Hotel Setup" = M // HOTEL-FRONTDESK has only R on Hotel Setup, so the action is hidden AccessByPermission = tabledata "Hotel Setup" = M;
CODECodeunit50108.ProcessRefund.al:46-48
HotelPermissionMgt.CheckRefundPermission();
CODECodeunit50105.HotelPermissionMgt.al:94-103
procedure CheckRefundPermission() begin if not HasFinancePermission() then Error('Access denied. Processing refunds is restricted to users with HOTEL-FINANCE or HOTEL-ADMIN... Front Desk users are not authorised...'); end;

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Money never reaches BC's standard posting layer. HotelInvoicePosting writes a row to the custom 'Hotel Posted Invoice' table via Insert(); payments/refunds are direct Inserts into the custom 'Hotel Payment Entry' table. No Codeunit "Sales-Post", "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line", or call into any standard posting API. No G/L Entry, Customer Ledger Entry, or VAT Entry is created, so finance has no traceable record outside the extension's own tables. This is the central money-routing miss.
BASHgrep -nE 'Sales-Post|Sales Post|GenJnlPostLine|Gen\. Jnl\.-Post Line|G/L Entry|Cust\. Ledger Entry|VAT Entry' workspace/src/codeunits/*.al
no matches — no standard posting codeunit or ledger table is referenced anywhere

Entry."Entry Type" := Entry."Entry Type"::Refund; Entry.Insert(true); Reservation."Refunded Amount" := Reservation."Refunded Amount" + Amount;

Codex (GPT-5 CLI)N/A
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
Three distinct permission sets: Hotel Front Desk grants operational tables but withholds the refund codeunit; Hotel Finance owns posting/refund codeunits and posted-invoice writes; Hotel Admin holds setup and room master-data control. The HotelPermissionCheck codeunit enforces the Front-Desk vs Finance gate at runtime.
CODEsrc/permissionsets/HotelFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al:6-30
tabledata "Hotel Reservation" = RIMD, tabledata "Hotel Service Charge" = RIMD, codeunit "Hotel Reservation Mgt" = X (no Refund codeunit)
CODEsrc/permissionsets/HotelFinance.PermissionSet.al:6-30
tabledata "Hotel Posted Invoice" = RIM, codeunit "Hotel Refund Mgt" = X, codeunit "Hotel Invoice Posting" = X
CODEsrc/permissionsets/HotelAdmin.PermissionSet.al:6-28
tabledata "Hotel Setup" = RIMD, tabledata "Hotel Room" = RIMD (master data + setup only)
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: three distinct permission sets (HOTEL-ADMIN/FINANCE/FRONTDESK) model the Admin/Finance/Front Desk separation, and code-level guards (CheckFinancePermission, CheckRefundPermission, CheckTaxSetupPermission) plus AccessByPermission on financial actions enforce the operational/financial split.
CODEworkspace/src/PermissionSet5010*.al
HOTEL-ADMIN: full access HOTEL-FINANCE: Hotel Setup = RIMD; codeunits Generate Invoice / Process Refund / VAT Calculation allowed HOTEL-FRONTDESK: Hotel Setup = R only; Process Refund codeunit NOT granted
CODECodeunit50105.HotelPermissionMgt.al:44-103
CheckFinancePermission/CheckRefundPermission/CheckTaxSetupPermission gate financial actions on HotelSetup.WritePermission()

Room.Occupied flag exists; CheckIn flips it true, CheckOut flips it false. Lifecycle wiring is direct and symmetric.
CODEsrc/tables/HotelRoom.Table.al:29-32
field(5; Occupied; Boolean) { Editable = false; }
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al:88-90
Room.Get(Reservation."Room No."); Room.Occupied := true; Room.Modify(true);
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al:140-143
if Room.Get(Reservation."Room No.") then begin Room.Occupied := false; Room.Modify(true); end;
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Room.Occupied flag is the occupancy tracker; Status OnValidate trigger calls UpdateRoomOccupancy which recomputes the flag based on remaining Occupied reservations — check-in sets it true, check-out (Status → Closed) clears it because no other Occupied reservation references the room.
CODETable50100.Room.al:28-32
field(4; "Occupied"; Boolean)
CODECodeunit50100.HotelReservationMgt.al:25-67
UpdateRoomOccupancy + IsRoomCurrentlyOccupied — drives Room.Occupied based on whether any other reservation for this room is in Occupied status
CODECodeunit50102.ReservationCheckOut.al:30-31
ReservationHeader.Validate(Status, "Hotel Reservation Status"::Closed); ReservationHeader.Modify(true); // Status OnValidate → UpdateRoomOccupancy clears Room.Occupied

S-06 · weight 2
VAT is driven by configurable rates and supports recalculation when configuration changes before invoicing.
Axis: Spec understanding · contributes to 30% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)N/A
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
VAT % is a configurable Hotel Setup field. RecalcTotals reads the current VAT % at invoice time, so a Setup change made before checkout is picked up automatically. HotelTax.RecalcReservationTax exposes a manual-recalc entry point that is blocked once the reservation is invoiced.
CODEsrc/tables/HotelSetup.Table.al:12-18
field(10; "VAT %"; Decimal) { MinValue = 0; MaxValue = 100; }
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelTax.Codeunit.al:20-26
procedure RecalcReservationTax(var Reservation: Record "Hotel Reservation") if Reservation.Invoiced then Error(AlreadyInvoicedErr, Reservation."No."); Reservation.RecalcTotals(); Reservation.Modify(true);
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelInvoicePosting.Codeunit.al:23-26
// Recalculate totals using current tax setup at invoice time Reservation.RecalcTotals();
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: VAT % is resolved at runtime from BC's VATPostingSetup (Business × Product group) and falls back to Hotel Setup's Default Room VAT %. HotelSetup.OnModify triggers BulkRecalcUnInvoicedReservations when VAT-relevant fields change — exactly the spec's 'recalc on configuration change before invoicing'. L3: changing Default Room VAT % from 0 → 10 produced the recalc dialog 'VAT has been recalculated on 2 un-invoiced reservation(s).' and the subsequent invoice math used the new rate (450 net + 45 VAT = 495).
CODECodeunit50104.VATCalculation.al:286-298
if (VATBusPostingGroup <> '') and (VATProdPostingGroup <> '') then if VATPostingSetup.Get(VATBusPostingGroup, VATProdPostingGroup) then exit(VATPostingSetup."VAT %"); exit(DefaultVATPct);
CODETable50102.HotelSetup.al:68-110
OnModify: if VATSetupChanged then ... HotelVATCalculation.BulkRecalcUnInvoicedReservations();

All four spec negative cases have explicit handlers: overbooking is blocked by CheckOverbooking, failed payment refuses to mark Deposit Captured, duplicate invoicing fires DuplicateInvoiceErr, and unauthorized refund is rejected by the permission-set gate.
CODEsrc/tables/HotelReservation.Table.al:254-268
Overbooking → CheckOverbooking + OverbookingErr
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al:62-67
Failed payment → Successful=false branch: Deposit Captured stays false, Error(PaymentFailedErr)
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelInvoicePosting.Codeunit.al:12-13
Duplicate invoice → DuplicateInvoiceErr
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelRefundMgt.Codeunit.al:13
Unauthorized refund → PermissionCheck.CheckIsFinance() → RefundNotAllowedErr
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: each of the four negative cases (overbooking, failed payment, duplicate invoice, unauthorized refund) has an explicit handling path that raises a user-readable Error and prevents the bad state from persisting.
CODECodeunit50106.RoomAvailabilityCheck.al:80-88
Overbooking: Error('Overbooking prevented: Room %1 is already reserved by another guest for a period that overlaps %2 to %3...');
CODECodeunit50107.PostDeposit.al:82-94
Failed payment: ReservationHeader.Validate("Payment Status", "Hotel Payment Status"::Failed); Error('Deposit capture FAILED ... Check-In and Invoice Generation are blocked...');
CODECodeunit50103.GenerateInvoice.al:49-54
Duplicate invoice: if ReservationHeader."Invoice No." <> '' then Error('Invoice generation is blocked... Duplicate invoices are not permitted.');
CODECodeunit50105.HotelPermissionMgt.al:94-103
Unauthorized refund: if not HasFinancePermission() then Error('Processing refunds is restricted to ... HOTEL-FINANCE or HOTEL-ADMIN. Front Desk users are not authorised to process refunds.');

CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al:78-79

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: each lifecycle action explicitly guards the current Status before transitioning — Check-In requires Confirmed, Check-Out requires Occupied, Generate Invoice requires Closed, and re-opening a Closed reservation is blocked at the table trigger. Each guard raises a user-readable error naming the current status.
CODECodeunit50101.ReservationCheckIn.al:22-24

CODECodeunit50103.GenerateInvoice.al:57-60
if ReservationHeader.Status <> "Hotel Reservation Status"::Closed then Error('An invoice can only be generated for a Closed reservation. Current status: %1.', ReservationHeader.Status);

trigger OnModify() if (xRec."Status" = xRec."Status"::Closed) and ("Status" <> "Status"::Closed) then Error('You cannot re-open a Closed reservation.');

Financial and posting integrity is preserved — postings are balanced, routed through standard posting, and traceable.

Claude CodeFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Postings never traverse standard BC posting. Invoice creation is a plain Insert on the custom 'Hotel Posted Invoice'. Payments are plain Inserts on 'Hotel Payment Entry'. No G/L Entry, Customer Ledger Entry, VAT Entry, or Sales Invoice Header is produced — the posted invoice/payment is only traceable from the reservation through the extension's own tables, not from standard finance views.
BASHgrep -nE 'Sales-Post|GenJnlPostLine|Sales Post' workspace/src/codeunits/*.al
no matches
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelInvoicePosting.Codeunit.al:36-56
PostedInvoice.Insert(); // Apply the deposit as a payment entry against the invoice if DepositToApply > 0 then LogPayment(...);
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al:159-180
Entry."Entry Type" := Entry."Entry Type"::Payment; Entry.Insert(true); Reservation."Paid Amount" := Reservation."Paid Amount" + Amount;
DartsFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: GenerateInvoice produces an unposted Sales Invoice (Sales Header + Sales Lines) and never invokes Codeunit::"Sales-Post" or any other standard posting codeunit. L3: the resulting invoice 102242 sits on the Sales Invoices (unposted) list — no posted document, no G/L / Customer Ledger entries are produced, and there is no traceable balanced posting from the reservation. Postings simply do not occur.
BASHgrep -nE 'Sales-Post|SalesPost|GenJnlPostLine' workspace/src/*.al
no matches — no standard BC posting codeunit is ever invoked.

Failed payment: CaptureDeposit logs an Entry with Successful=false, explicitly resets Deposit Captured=false, and Errors — no false success. Duplicate invoice: status guard + Invoiced guard both block (verified at runtime). Out-of-sequence: each lifecycle action validates the prior status and Errors with a clear message.
CODEsrc/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al:53-67
Entry.Successful := Successful; ... if not Successful then begin Reservation."Deposit Captured" := false; Reservation.Modify(true); Error(PaymentFailedErr); end; Reservation."Deposit Captured" := true;

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: failed payments never write success (PostDeposit only flips flags inside the success branch); duplicate invoicing is blocked at two layers (action Enabled gate + codeunit guard); out-of-sequence calls are rejected (CheckIn requires Confirmed, CheckOut requires Occupied, GenerateInvoice requires Closed, modifying away from Closed errors).

Duplicate invoice block; status guard (Closed only); payment status Failed blocks invoicing.
CODECodeunit50101.ReservationCheckIn.al:22-57

F-04 · weight 3
Activation requires signatory + locks critical fields after
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 40% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)PASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 1Pass
L3 Playwright: invoked the Activate action on CONT002 (no signatory assigned, no template). BC raised dialog 'Contract Template Code must have a value in Lease Contract: No.=CONT002. It cannot be zero or empty.' — activation is blocked by ValidateContractReady's TestField chain (Template is checked before Signatory). L4 confirms the same procedure explicitly TestFields 'Signatory 1 Code' (and 'Signatory 2 Code' if Require Two Signatories) at line 1228-1230 — signatory requirement is enforced. Critical fields (No., Project No., Tenant, Property, Start/End Date, Base Rent, Deposit) all carry OnValidate triggers that call CheckEditableCriticalField, which errors once Signed=true or Status in [Active, Cancellation, Closed]. Both halves of the spec (signatory required + field locking) are implemented.
SCREENSHOTF04_step1_activate_no_signatory_error_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 1
CODERentalManagement.al:1200-1231
Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code"); if Project."Require Two Signatories" then Contract.TestField("Signatory 2 Code");
CODERentalManagement.al:445-453
field(8; "Base Rent"; Decimal) { trigger OnValidate() begin CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldCaption("Base Rent")); end; }
CODERentalManagement.al:566-572
local procedure CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldName: Text) begin if (xRec."No." <> '') and (xRec.Signed or (xRec.Status in [xRec.Status::Active, xRec.Status::"Cancellation Process", xRec.Status::Closed])) then Error('%1 cannot be changed after signature or activation.', FieldName); end;

Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown

L3 step2: typed Signatory 1=SIG1, Activate succeeded — Signed=true, Signed Date=6/4/2025 (workdate). L3 step3: attempted to change Base Rent from 10000 to 15000 — page rejected with 'Base Rent cannot be changed after the contract has been signed or activated.' (CheckEditableCriticalField trigger). Both spec'd guards enforced.

Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code"); Signatory.SetRange("Project No.", Contract."Project No."); Signatory.SetRange("Signatory Code", Contract."Signatory 1 Code"); if Signatory.IsEmpty() then Error('Signatory 1 %1 is not registered on project %2.', ...);
CODETables.al:611-619
local procedure CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldName: Text) begin if xRec.Signed or (xRec.Status in [Active, Cancellation Process, Closed]) then Error(LockedFieldErr, FieldName);
DartsFAIL

Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Field locking works: L3 captured the post-activation card where every previously-editable combobox (Tenant No., Project No., Starting/Ending Date, Deposit Amount/Type) is rendered as a read-only button, and Tab50103.RentalContract uses TestStatusIsDraft() on every critical field. Deposit posting and activation succeed. However, the criterion requires BOTH 'Signatory required + field locking enforced'. The agent's Rental Contract table has NO signatory field, the ActivateContract procedure (Cod50100:117-167) contains NO signatory guard, and grep across the entire src/ tree returns zero matches for 'signatory' or 'signature'. Activation without any signatory completed cleanly, so the spec's first assert 'Activation without signatory blocked' is not enforced.

Sublease creation respects area limit
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 40% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)INCONCLUSIVE
Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I
Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 1Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked: cannot activate CONT002 (parent must be Active before sublease can be activated). L4: ValidateSublease (called by ActivateContract) errors when Sublease Area Sqm > Property.Available Sublease Area Sqm — area-limit logic is implemented as the spec requires.
CODERentalManagement.al:1247-1265
local procedure ValidateSublease(Contract: Record "RE Contract") ... ParentContract.Get(Contract."Parent Contract No."); if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error('Sub-subleasing is not allowed.'); ParentContract.TestField(Status, ParentContract.Status::Active); if not DatesOverlap(...) then Error('The sublease period must fall within the primary contract period.'); Property.Get(Contract."Primary Property No."); if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" then Error('Sublease area exceeds the available sublease area for property %1.', Property."No.");
Claude CodeINCONCLUSIVE
Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I
Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
Could not exercise the runtime workflow within the action budget — CONTRACT001 was already terminated/Closed in F-09, leaving no Active parent to attach a sublease to (the codeunit requires ParentContract.Status = Active at line 633-634), and standing up a fresh active parent + sublease child via UI would burn another large chunk of tool calls. L4 review of ValidateSublease (called from ActivateContract line 37) shows the area-exceeds guard is implemented with the correct comparison against Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" minus already-used sublease area. Per the grading rules, code review cannot upgrade this to a functional PASS; marking INCONCLUSIVE attribution=grader_env.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:617-646
local procedure ValidateSublease(Contract: Record "RE Contract") ... UsedArea := SubleasedAreaUsedExcluding(ParentContract."No.", Contract."No."); AvailableArea := Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" - UsedArea; if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > AvailableArea then Error(SubleaseAreaErr, Contract."Sublease Area Sqm", AvailableArea, Property."No.");
DartsFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code

The feature is completely absent, so the spec's area-limit validation cannot be exercised in the UI.

F-09 · weight 2
Contract termination releases property + settles deposit
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 40% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)INCONCLUSIVE
Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I
Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 1Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked (no Active contract to terminate). L4: termination flow is split into StartCancellation (Active → Cancellation Process) and CloseContract (Cancellation Process → Closed). CloseContract releases the property by setting Rental Status::Available. SettleDeposit posts settlement + refund entries to RE Deposit Entry — total of both must equal the contract deposit amount. All three spec assertions (Closed status, property released, deposit settled) are coded.
CODERentalManagement.al:959-983
procedure StartCancellation(...) Contract.TestField(Status, Contract.Status::Active); Contract."Termination Date" := TerminationDate; Contract.Status := Contract.Status::"Cancellation Process"; Contract.Modify(true); LogAudit(...); ... procedure CloseContract(...) Contract.Status := Contract.Status::Closed; Contract.Modify(true); if Property.Get(Contract."Primary Property No.") then begin Property."Rental Status" := Property."Rental Status"::Available; Property.Modify(true); end;
CODERentalManagement.al:1104-1114
procedure SettleDeposit(...) Contract.TestField(Status, Contract.Status::"Cancellation Process"); if Round(SettlementAmount + RefundAmount, 0.01) <> Round(Contract."Deposit Amount", 0.01) then Error('Settlement and refund must equal the contract deposit amount.'); if SettlementAmount <> 0 then CreateDepositEntry(Contract, Enum::"RE Deposit Entry Type"::Settlement, ...); if RefundAmount <> 0 then CreateDepositEntry(Contract, Enum::"RE Deposit Entry Type"::Refund, ...);
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 Playwright: drove Actions → Lifecycle → Start Cancellation (status → Cancellation Process), then Actions → Lifecycle → Close → status → Closed. Re-opened PROP001 and confirmed Rental Status combobox shows 'Available' selected with Last Status Change=6/6/2026 10:02 PM — confirming property was released back to the available pool by ReleaseProperty(). Deposit settlement is implemented as a separate SettleDeposit action that runs in Cancellation Process state and emits RE Deposit Entry rows (Settlement/Refund types) — settlement plumbing is present even though I did not click that specific action in this run.

procedure CloseContract — TestField(Status, Cancellation Process), Status := Closed, ReleaseProperty(Primary Property) → Rental Status := Available + Last Status Change; iterates ContractProperty and releases each.

L4 confirms: CloseContract procedure has no DepositLedgerEntry.Insert and no G/L unwind logic. The spec's third assert 'Deposit settlement entry exists' is not met.

F-10
Audit trail captures critical changes
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 40% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)PASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 1Pass
L4 (action explicitly says to read the code): the codeunit invokes LogAudit on every critical state transition — Activate (line 956), StartCancellation (966), Close (982), Invoice Created (1088), Rent Update (1138), Renewal (1150), Deposit entries (1326). Old/new values, user, and timestamp are captured. Custom audit table is small and purpose-built — meets the criterion's intent.
CODERentalManagement.al:884-933
table 50012 "RE Audit Entry" { ... field(2; "Table No."; Integer) field(3; "Record No."; Code[20]) field(4; "Action"; Text[50]) field(5; "Old Value"; Text[250]) field(6; "New Value"; Text[250]) field(7; "User ID"; Code[50]) { DataClassification = EndUserIdentifiableInformation; } field(8; "Created At"; DateTime) }
CODERentalManagement.al:1329-1342
local procedure LogAudit(TableNo: Integer; RecordNo: Code[20]; ActionText: Text[50]; OldValue: Text; NewValue: Text) AuditEntry.Init(); AuditEntry."Table No." := TableNo; AuditEntry."Record No." := RecordNo; AuditEntry."Action" := ActionText; AuditEntry."Old Value" := CopyStr(OldValue, ...); AuditEntry."User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(), ...); AuditEntry."Created At" := CurrentDateTime(); AuditEntry.Insert(true);
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
Action step is 'Read the agent's audit/change-log code' — L4 review is the prescribed verification layer. RE Audit Entry table (table 50012) captures Table No., Record No., Action, Field Name, Old/New value, User ID, Created At, Comment. LogAudit is invoked from every critical lifecycle transition (activate, cancellation, close, renewal), mass property updates, billing/invoice creation, deposit movements, rent updates and IBI updates. Audit list page (RE Audit Entries page 50015) is exposed under Navigation menu. Custom audit table is justified because BC's Change Log captures field-level edits but does not record domain-level lifecycle actions with structured comments.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:737-752
procedure LogAudit(TableNo, RecordNo, ActionText, FieldName, OldValue, NewValue, CommentText) begin AuditEntry.Init(); AuditEntry."Table No." := TableNo; AuditEntry."Record No." := RecordNo; AuditEntry.Action := ActionText; AuditEntry."User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(), ...); AuditEntry."Created At" := CurrentDateTime(); AuditEntry.Insert(true);
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:51,64,89,113,133,232,322,461,516,734
LogAudit called from Activate, StartCancellation, CloseContract, Renew, MassSetPropertyStatus, EntryRightPosted, Sales Invoice Created, Rent Update, IBI Update, Deposit entries — covers all critical lifecycle and accounting operations.

Acceptable per the criterion's pass_if.

CODERentalManagement.al:61,232,373,724,659,830,1141,1247,969,1033
1.ProjectSetup→RE Project; 2.Property→RE Property; 3.LeaseContract→RE Contract; 4.Deposit→RE Deposit Entry + PostInitialDeposit/SettleDeposit; 5.RecurringBilling→RE Billing Schedule + GenerateMonthlySchedule; 6.IBITax→RE IBI Entry + UpdateIBIRecharge; 7.Renewals→RenewContract + RE Rent History; 8.Sublease→Is Sublease/Parent Contract No fields + ValidateSublease; 9.Termination→StartCancellation + CloseContract; 10.Accounting→Project G/L accounts + CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling builds real BC Sales Header/Line

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
All 10 functional requirement areas traceable to code/tables. Each FR has dedicated tables and codeunit procedures.
CODETables.al + RentalManagement.Codeunit.al
FR1 Project Setup → RE Project + Signatory + Template tables (50000/50001/50002). FR2 Property → RE Property (50003) + MassSetPropertyStatus. FR3 Lease Contract → RE Contract (50005) + Contract Property/Tenant. FR4 Deposit → RE Deposit Entry (50009) + PostInitialDeposit/Transfer/SettleDeposit procedures. FR5 Recurring Billing → RE Billing Schedule (50008) + GenerateBillingSchedule/CreateBatchInvoices/CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling. FR6 IBI Recharge → RE IBI Entry (50011) + UpdateIBIRecharge with catch-up. FR7 Rent Updates/Renewals → RE Rent History (50010) + ApplyRentUpdate/ApplyRentIndexation + RenewContract. FR8 Sublease → 'Is Sublease'/'Parent Contract No.'/'Sublease Area Sqm' fields + ValidateSublease. FR9 Termination → StartCancellation + CloseContract + ReleaseProperty. FR10 Accounting → CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling builds standard Sales Header/Line + RE Audit Entry (50012).

Axis: Spec understanding · contributes to 30% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)FAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 1Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: only ONE permissionset is shipped ('RE RENTAL MGT' with full RIMD across all tables). The spec requires 4 distinct user roles (Property Manager, Contract/Legal Administrator, Finance User, Administrator) each with bounded permissions. A single all-RIMD set does not satisfy role segregation.
CODERentalManagement.al:1946-1965
permissionset 50000 "RE RENTAL MGT" { Assignable = true; Caption = 'Rental Management'; Permissions = tabledata "RE Project" = RIMD, ... }
Claude CodeFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Spec requires four distinct user roles, but the extension ships ONE permission set that grants full RIMD on every table to anyone assigned it. There is no separation between Property Manager, Contract Admin, Finance, Administrator — the role-based access promised in the spec is not enforced.
CODEPermissions.al:1-20
permissionset 50000 "RE Rental Mgt" — Assignable, single permission set granting RIMD on all 13 RE tables. No separate roles for Property Manager / Contract Admin / Finance / Administrator.
DartsFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
The extension ships no permission set objects at all. The spec requires four distinct roles (Property Manager, Contract Admin, Finance, Administrator). None are modeled. AppSource explicitly requires extensions to ship permission sets.
BASHgrep -ri 'permissionset|PermissionSet' workspace/src/
No files found — agent ships ZERO permissionset objects
CODEworkspace/app.json
no PermissionSet listed; no .PermissionSet.al files exist under src/

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Of the 12 minimum contract fields the spec enumerates (Contract No., Project No., Tenant No., Property No., Start, End, Status, Base Rent, Deposit Amount, Billing Concepts, Payment Terms, Signatory Data, Contract Template), three load-bearing ones are completely absent: Payment Terms, Signatory, Contract Template. 9/12 ≈ 75%, but the three missing are exactly those that drive the contract's legal and billing semantics — below 'all or near-all'.
CODETab50103.RentalContract.al fields
Present: No.(1), Tenant No.(2), Tenant Name(3), Project No.(4), Project Name(5), Starting Date(6), Ending Date(7), Status(10), No. Series(40), Deposit Required(50), Deposit Amount(51), Deposit Type(52), Deposit G/L Account(53), Deposit Posted(54). Base Rent via Total Monthly Rent flow-field (21) sourced from Rental Contract Line. Billing concepts via separate Tab50106. MISSING: Payment Terms code, Signatory data (no signatory field), Contract Template reference.

S-05
Negative cases handled — overlapping allocation, double-billing, missing signatory, sublease area, sub-sublease, mid-year IBI, locked-field tampering, deposit refund
Axis: Spec understanding · contributes to 30% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)PASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 1Pass
L4: all nine spec'd negative cases are guarded explicitly with Error() calls. Overlapping allocation, double-billing, missing signatory, sublease area, sub-sublease, mid-year IBI catch-up, locked-field tampering, deposit refund total balance, and failed/no-doc-no deposit posting — each has a concrete check.
CODERentalManagement.al:1233-1244
PreventOverlappingAllocation: errors 'Property %1 is already allocated to contract %2 for an overlapping period.'
CODERentalManagement.al:1272-1283
EnsureNotAlreadyScheduled: errors on duplicate (contract, concept, period start, period end)
CODERentalManagement.al:1228
Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code") — missing-signatory guard
CODERentalManagement.al:1263-1264
if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" then Error('Sublease area exceeds the available sublease area for property %1.')
CODERentalManagement.al:1257-1258
if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error('Sub-subleasing is not allowed.')
CODERentalManagement.al:1153-1186
UpdateIBIRecharge: AlreadyBilled scanned from invoiced TaxIBI rows for the tax year; CatchUpAmount = AnnualAmount - AlreadyBilled — avoids double-billing of already-invoiced periods
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
8 of 9 spec'd negative cases have explicit guarded code paths: overlapping allocation, double-billing, missing signatory, sublease area excess, sub-sublease attempt, mid-year IBI catch-up, locked field tampering, incorrect deposit refund balance. (Failed deposit posting is the only edge case not explicitly guarded in a dedicated branch; it relies on Insert's standard error propagation.) Comfortably above the 6-of-9 threshold.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:599-615
PreventOverlappingAllocation iterates other contracts on same property where Status=Active|Cancellation, calls DatesOverlap, raises OverlapErr.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:669-679,214-215
BillingExists() guard + GenerateOneTimeBilling raises DoubleBillingErr if same period exists.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:583-596
ValidateContractReady → Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code") + lookup of signatory record in project, else Error.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:642-645
Sublease area guard: if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > AvailableArea then Error(SubleaseAreaErr, ...).
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:631-632
Sub-sublease guard: if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error(SubSubleaseErr).
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:478-517
UpdateIBIRecharge computes AlreadyBilled (sum of existing TaxIBI billing for the tax year) and only bills the CatchUpAmount = AnnualAmount - AlreadyBilled — avoids double-billing the year.
DartsFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Of 9 negative cases in the spec, only 4 are guarded in code (overlap, double-billing, failed deposit, locked-field tampering). 5 are entirely missing (signatory validation, sublease area, sub-sublease, mid-year IBI, deposit refund/settlement at termination). 4/9 < the 6/9 majority required to pass.
CODETab50104.RentalContractLine.al:131-190 (CheckOverlappingLeases)
1) Overlap allocation ✓ — OverlapErr guards new lines
CODETab50107.RentalInvoiceLink.al:98-107 + Cod50101.RentalBillingEngine.al:49-52
2) Double-billing ✓ — PeriodAlreadyBilled guard
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:253-335 (PostDepositReceipt)
3) Failed deposit posting ✓ — AlreadyPostedErr, NoDepositRequiredErr, NoDepositAmountErr, NoTenantErr, NoAccountErr
CODETab50103.RentalContract.al TestStatusIsDraft on fields 2/4/6/7/50/51/52/53
8) Locked-field tampering ✓ — every critical field calls TestStatusIsDraft() which errors with MustBeDraftErr
BASHgrep -ri 'signatory|sublease|IBI|deposit refund|settlement' workspace/src/
4) Missing signatory ✗, 5) Sublease area ✗, 6) Sub-sublease ✗, 7) Mid-year IBI ✗, 9) Deposit refund/settlement ✗ — all 5 absent

Opus 4.7 · run 1Pass
L3 Playwright: invoked the Activate action on CONT002 (no signatory assigned, no template). BC raised dialog 'Contract Template Code must have a value in Lease Contract: No.=CONT002. It cannot be zero or empty.' — activation is blocked by ValidateContractReady's TestField chain (Template is checked before Signatory). L4 confirms the same procedure explicitly TestFields 'Signatory 1 Code' (and 'Signatory 2 Code' if Require Two Signatories) at line 1228-1230 — signatory requirement is enforced. Critical fields (No., Project No., Tenant, Property, Start/End Date, Base Rent, Deposit) all carry OnValidate triggers that call CheckEditableCriticalField, which errors once Signed=true or Status in [Active, Cancellation, Closed]. Both halves of the spec (signatory required + field locking) are implemented.
SCREENSHOTF04_step1_activate_no_signatory_error_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 1
CODERentalManagement.al:1200-1231
Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code"); if Project."Require Two Signatories" then Contract.TestField("Signatory 2 Code");
CODERentalManagement.al:445-453
field(8; "Base Rent"; Decimal) { trigger OnValidate() begin CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldCaption("Base Rent")); end; }
CODERentalManagement.al:566-572
local procedure CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldName: Text) begin if (xRec."No." <> '') and (xRec.Signed or (xRec.Status in [xRec.Status::Active, xRec.Status::"Cancellation Process", xRec.Status::Closed])) then Error('%1 cannot be changed after signature or activation.', FieldName); end;
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 step1: invoked Activate on Draft contract with no signatory → BC error 'Signatory 1 Code must have a value in Lease Contract: No.=CONTRACT001' (TestField in ValidateContractReady). L3 step2: typed Signatory 1=SIG1, Activate succeeded — Signed=true, Signed Date=6/4/2025 (workdate). L3 step3: attempted to change Base Rent from 10000 to 15000 — page rejected with 'Base Rent cannot be changed after the contract has been signed or activated.' (CheckEditableCriticalField trigger). Both spec'd guards enforced.
SCREENSHOTF04_step2_signatory_required_error.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0

SCREENSHOTF04_step5_field_locked_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:583-596
Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code"); Signatory.SetRange("Project No.", Contract."Project No."); Signatory.SetRange("Signatory Code", Contract."Signatory 1 Code"); if Signatory.IsEmpty() then Error('Signatory 1 %1 is not registered on project %2.', ...);
CODETables.al:611-619
local procedure CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldName: Text) begin if xRec.Signed or (xRec.Status in [Active, Cancellation Process, Closed]) then Error(LockedFieldErr, FieldName);
DartsFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Field locking works: L3 captured the post-activation card where every previously-editable combobox (Tenant No., Project No., Starting/Ending Date, Deposit Amount/Type) is rendered as a read-only button, and Tab50103.RentalContract uses TestStatusIsDraft() on every critical field. Deposit posting and activation succeed. However, the criterion requires BOTH 'Signatory required + field locking enforced'. The agent's Rental Contract table has NO signatory field, the ActivateContract procedure (Cod50100:117-167) contains NO signatory guard, and grep across the entire src/ tree returns zero matches for 'signatory' or 'signature'. Activation without any signatory completed cleanly, so the spec's first assert 'Activation without signatory blocked' is not enforced.
SCREENSHOTF04_step1_deposit_posted.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
SCREENSHOTF04_step2_contract_activated_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:117-167
procedure ActivateContract(...) if RentalContract."Tenant No." = '' then Error(NoTenantErr,...); if (RentalContract."Starting Date" = 0D) or (RentalContract."Ending Date" = 0D) then Error(NoDatesErr,...); if RentalContract."Deposit Required" and not RentalContract."Deposit Posted" then Error(DepositNotPostedErr,...); // NO signatory guard
BASHgrep -ri 'signatory|signature' workspace/src/
No files found — no signatory field, no signatory table, no signatory enforcement anywhere in the extension

L3 blocked: cannot activate CONT002 (parent must be Active before sublease can be activated). L4: ValidateSublease (called by ActivateContract) errors when Sublease Area Sqm > Property.Available Sublease Area Sqm — area-limit logic is implemented as the spec requires.
CODERentalManagement.al:1247-1265
local procedure ValidateSublease(Contract: Record "RE Contract") ... ParentContract.Get(Contract."Parent Contract No."); if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error('Sub-subleasing is not allowed.'); ParentContract.TestField(Status, ParentContract.Status::Active); if not DatesOverlap(...) then Error('The sublease period must fall within the primary contract period.'); Property.Get(Contract."Primary Property No."); if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" then Error('Sublease area exceeds the available sublease area for property %1.', Property."No.");
Claude CodeINCONCLUSIVE

Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
Could not exercise the runtime workflow within the action budget — CONTRACT001 was already terminated/Closed in F-09, leaving no Active parent to attach a sublease to (the codeunit requires ParentContract.Status = Active at line 633-634), and standing up a fresh active parent + sublease child via UI would burn another large chunk of tool calls. L4 review of ValidateSublease (called from ActivateContract line 37) shows the area-exceeds guard is implemented with the correct comparison against Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" minus already-used sublease area. Per the grading rules, code review cannot upgrade this to a functional PASS; marking INCONCLUSIVE attribution=grader_env.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:617-646
local procedure ValidateSublease(Contract: Record "RE Contract") ... UsedArea := SubleasedAreaUsedExcluding(ParentContract."No.", Contract."No."); AvailableArea := Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" - UsedArea; if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > AvailableArea then Error(SubleaseAreaErr, Contract."Sublease Area Sqm", AvailableArea, Property."No.");

Opus 4.7 · run 1Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked (no Active contract to terminate). L4: termination flow is split into StartCancellation (Active → Cancellation Process) and CloseContract (Cancellation Process → Closed). CloseContract releases the property by setting Rental Status::Available. SettleDeposit posts settlement + refund entries to RE Deposit Entry — total of both must equal the contract deposit amount. All three spec assertions (Closed status, property released, deposit settled) are coded.
CODERentalManagement.al:959-983
procedure StartCancellation(...) Contract.TestField(Status, Contract.Status::Active); Contract."Termination Date" := TerminationDate; Contract.Status := Contract.Status::"Cancellation Process"; Contract.Modify(true); LogAudit(...); ... procedure CloseContract(...) Contract.Status := Contract.Status::Closed; Contract.Modify(true); if Property.Get(Contract."Primary Property No.") then begin Property."Rental Status" := Property."Rental Status"::Available; Property.Modify(true); end;
CODERentalManagement.al:1104-1114
procedure SettleDeposit(...) Contract.TestField(Status, Contract.Status::"Cancellation Process"); if Round(SettlementAmount + RefundAmount, 0.01) <> Round(Contract."Deposit Amount", 0.01) then Error('Settlement and refund must equal the contract deposit amount.'); if SettlementAmount <> 0 then CreateDepositEntry(Contract, Enum::"RE Deposit Entry Type"::Settlement, ...); if RefundAmount <> 0 then CreateDepositEntry(Contract, Enum::"RE Deposit Entry Type"::Refund, ...);
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 Playwright: drove Actions → Lifecycle → Start Cancellation (status → Cancellation Process), then Actions → Lifecycle → Close → status → Closed. Re-opened PROP001 and confirmed Rental Status combobox shows 'Available' selected with Last Status Change=6/6/2026 10:02 PM — confirming property was released back to the available pool by ReleaseProperty(). Deposit settlement is implemented as a separate SettleDeposit action that runs in Cancellation Process state and emits RE Deposit Entry rows (Settlement/Refund types) — settlement plumbing is present even though I did not click that specific action in this run.

L3 Playwright: clicked Close on the active contract. After close: Activate/Start Cancellation/Close all disabled (status=Closed, the only state where all three are disabled). However, the Deposit Ledger Entries list filtered to CONTRACT001 still shows exactly ONE row (the original posting Amount=20,000 Internal) — no settlement/refund entry was inserted. L4 confirms: CloseContract procedure has no DepositLedgerEntry.Insert and no G/L unwind logic. The spec's third assert 'Deposit settlement entry exists' is not met.

Opus 4.7 · run 1Pass
L4 (action explicitly says to read the code): the codeunit invokes LogAudit on every critical state transition — Activate (line 956), StartCancellation (966), Close (982), Invoice Created (1088), Rent Update (1138), Renewal (1150), Deposit entries (1326). Old/new values, user, and timestamp are captured. Custom audit table is small and purpose-built — meets the criterion's intent.
CODERentalManagement.al:884-933
table 50012 "RE Audit Entry" { ... field(2; "Table No."; Integer) field(3; "Record No."; Code[20]) field(4; "Action"; Text[50]) field(5; "Old Value"; Text[250]) field(6; "New Value"; Text[250]) field(7; "User ID"; Code[50]) { DataClassification = EndUserIdentifiableInformation; } field(8; "Created At"; DateTime) }
CODERentalManagement.al:1329-1342
local procedure LogAudit(TableNo: Integer; RecordNo: Code[20]; ActionText: Text[50]; OldValue: Text; NewValue: Text) AuditEntry.Init(); AuditEntry."Table No." := TableNo; AuditEntry."Record No." := RecordNo; AuditEntry."Action" := ActionText; AuditEntry."Old Value" := CopyStr(OldValue, ...); AuditEntry."User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(), ...); AuditEntry."Created At" := CurrentDateTime(); AuditEntry.Insert(true);
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
Action step is 'Read the agent's audit/change-log code' — L4 review is the prescribed verification layer. RE Audit Entry table (table 50012) captures Table No., Record No., Action, Field Name, Old/New value, User ID, Created At, Comment. LogAudit is invoked from every critical lifecycle transition (activate, cancellation, close, renewal), mass property updates, billing/invoice creation, deposit movements, rent updates and IBI updates. Audit list page (RE Audit Entries page 50015) is exposed under Navigation menu. Custom audit table is justified because BC's Change Log captures field-level edits but does not record domain-level lifecycle actions with structured comments.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:737-752
procedure LogAudit(TableNo, RecordNo, ActionText, FieldName, OldValue, NewValue, CommentText) begin AuditEntry.Init(); AuditEntry."Table No." := TableNo; AuditEntry."Record No." := RecordNo; AuditEntry.Action := ActionText; AuditEntry."User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(), ...); AuditEntry."Created At" := CurrentDateTime(); AuditEntry.Insert(true);
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:51,64,89,113,133,232,322,461,516,734
LogAudit called from Activate, StartCancellation, CloseContract, Renew, MassSetPropertyStatus, EntryRightPosted, Sales Invoice Created, Rent Update, IBI Update, Deposit entries — covers all critical lifecycle and accounting operations.

Opus 4.7 · run 1Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: the agent declares Property Nos. / Contract Nos. fields on the Project that TableRelate to BC's No. Series table, but the codeunit never calls NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo / InitSeries to actually allocate numbers. Property/Contract/Template/Tenant No. fields are typed manually by the user (as I observed in the UI when I had to type PROJ002, PROP002, CONT002 explicitly). The criterion's pass_if 'BC No. Series used for numbering' is not met.
BASHgrep -niE 'NoSeriesMgt|Codeunit.*"No\. Series"' workspace/src/RentalManagement.al
0 hits — the BC No. Series codeunit is never invoked
CODERentalManagement.al:79-88
field(3; "Property Nos."; Code[20]) { TableRelation = "No. Series"; } field(4; "Contract Nos."; Code[20]) { TableRelation = "No. Series"; } — fields exposed but never consumed
Claude CodeFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
The project setup table stores three No. Series codes (Property Nos., Contract Nos., Tenant Nos.) as data, but nothing in the codeunit ever calls NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo() / NoSeriesMgt.InitSeries() / NoSeries.GetNextNo(), and no Page card field has AssistEdit + OnLookup wired to a series. Project/Property/Tenant/Contract numbers must be typed manually by the user — exactly the anti-pattern this criterion flags. The TableRelation is decorative without a runtime generator.
BASHGrep 'No. Series'|NoSeriesMgt across workspace/src/
Only 3 hits — all in Tables.al lines 22/27/32 as TableRelation = "No. Series" lookup. No call to Codeunit "No. Series" or NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo() anywhere in the codeunit.
CODEPages.al:41,192,272,334,565
field("No."; Rec."No.") on every Card page — user-typed only; no AssistEdit / OnLookup wiring to a number series.
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: every numbering routine (Project, Property, Contract) injects Codeunit "No. Series" and calls GetNextNo / LookupRelatedNoSeries. The Rental Setup table holds the four No. Series codes (Project Nos./Property Nos./Contract Nos./Billing Nos.). No CopyStr/Format/StrSubstNo custom string generators are used for IDs.
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:25-32
NoSeriesMgt: Codeunit "No. Series"; RentalSetup.TestField("Project Nos."); RealEstateProject."No. Series" := RentalSetup."Project Nos."; RealEstateProject."No." := NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo(RentalSetup."Project Nos.", WorkDate());
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:60/93/108
Same pattern reused in GetNextPropertyNo, GetNextContractNo and AssistEdit* — all rely on Codeunit "No. Series" with WorkDate(), never a hand-rolled string format

Opus 4.7 · run 1Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: recurring billing is invoked solely by a user-clicked action on the contract card — no Job Queue Entry registration, no recurrence wiring, no scheduler. The spec asks for BC's Job Queue (or at minimum no custom scheduler); a manual user action does not satisfy 'recurring' billing as the criterion phrases it.
BASHgrep -niE '"Job Queue Entry"|JobQueueMgt|JobQueueEntry' workspace/src/RentalManagement.al
0 hits — Job Queue is never referenced
CODERentalManagement.al:1716-1728
action(GenerateSchedule) { Caption = 'Generate Monthly Schedule'; trigger OnAction() var RentalMgt: Codeunit "RE Rental Management"; begin RentalMgt.GenerateMonthlySchedule(Rec, Rec."Start Date", Rec."End Date", false); end; }
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
No reference to BC Job Queue, but also no custom scheduler/timer loop — the criterion explicitly permits 'trigger only' (user-invoked) implementations as a pass. Recurring billing is exposed through page actions and a batch procedure (CreateBatchInvoices) that the user runs; it could be wrapped in a Job Queue Entry by the customer at deploy time. Acceptable per the criterion's pass_if.
BASHGrep 'Job Queue'|JobQueueEntry|JobQueueMgt across workspace/src/
0 hits — no custom scheduler/timer either; recurring billing is initiated only via user-triggered page actions (Generate Billing Schedule, Simulate Billing Schedule, Create Batch Invoices).
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
No Job Queue wiring is present, but no custom timer/scheduler is built either. Recurring billing is exposed as a standard BC processing-only Report with RequestPage filters and a Simulation Mode flag — user-triggered, not a custom scheduler. The criterion accepts trigger-only.
BASHgrep -ri 'Job Queue|JobQueue' workspace/src/
No matches — no Job Queue Entry reference

report 50100 "Batch Rental Billing" { ApplicationArea = All; UsageCategory = Tasks; ProcessingOnly = true; }
CODEPag50105.RentalContractCard.al:281-298
action(RunBilling) { ... BatchRentalBilling.RunModal(); }

L4: the agent did not wire BC's Change Log, but the custom RE Audit Entry table is small (one table, one procedure, ~50 lines) and purpose-aware — it captures action + before/after values + user + timestamp on the critical state transitions. Per the criterion's wording ('Either uses BC Change Log OR has a small justification-aware custom audit'), this qualifies. Using BC Change Log would have been more idiomatic but this is a defensible minimal alternative.
BASHgrep -niE 'Change Log Mgt|Change Log Entry' workspace/src/RentalManagement.al
0 hits — BC Change Log isn't used
CODERentalManagement.al:884-933
table 50012 "RE Audit Entry" — 8 fields (TableNo, RecordNo, Action, OldValue, NewValue, UserID, CreatedAt). LogAudit procedure is ~13 lines.

Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
BC Change Log is not referenced, but the custom audit table is small (one table + one procedure ~16 lines) and tightly scoped to domain lifecycle events — exactly the 'small justification-aware custom audit' the criterion permits as a pass.
CODETables.al:962-1020
table 50012 "RE Audit Entry" — 10 narrow fields (Entry No., Table No., Record No., Action, Field Name, Old/New Value, User ID, Created At, Comment); single-purpose, structured.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:737-752
LogAudit is a single ~16-line procedure with no abstractions, frameworks, or subscriber boilerplate.
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: agent uses two small, well-scoped custom audit ledgers (Deposit Ledger Entry and Rental Invoice Link) that automatically stamp UserId + timestamp via OnInsert. This is a small, justification-aware custom audit, not a from-scratch full Change Log framework. The criterion accepts this. BC Change Log is not wired, but the agent's approach is BC-idiomatic for transactional audit (a ledger pattern).
CODETab50105.DepositLedgerEntry.al:65-91
field(11; "User ID"; Code[50]) { DataClassification = EndUserIdentifiableInformation; } trigger OnInsert() begin "User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(),1,...); end;
CODETab50107.RentalInvoiceLink.al:64-96
field(11; "User ID"...) and field(12; "Created At"; DateTime) populated in OnInsert via UserId()/CurrentDateTime() — second small audit log

CODERentalManagement.al:61,232,373,724,659,830,1141,1247,969,1033
1.ProjectSetup→RE Project; 2.Property→RE Property; 3.LeaseContract→RE Contract; 4.Deposit→RE Deposit Entry + PostInitialDeposit/SettleDeposit; 5.RecurringBilling→RE Billing Schedule + GenerateMonthlySchedule; 6.IBITax→RE IBI Entry + UpdateIBIRecharge; 7.Renewals→RenewContract + RE Rent History; 8.Sublease→Is Sublease/Parent Contract No fields + ValidateSublease; 9.Termination→StartCancellation + CloseContract; 10.Accounting→Project G/L accounts + CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling builds real BC Sales Header/Line

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
All 10 functional requirement areas traceable to code/tables. Each FR has dedicated tables and codeunit procedures.
CODETables.al + RentalManagement.Codeunit.al
FR1 Project Setup → RE Project + Signatory + Template tables (50000/50001/50002). FR2 Property → RE Property (50003) + MassSetPropertyStatus. FR3 Lease Contract → RE Contract (50005) + Contract Property/Tenant. FR4 Deposit → RE Deposit Entry (50009) + PostInitialDeposit/Transfer/SettleDeposit procedures. FR5 Recurring Billing → RE Billing Schedule (50008) + GenerateBillingSchedule/CreateBatchInvoices/CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling. FR6 IBI Recharge → RE IBI Entry (50011) + UpdateIBIRecharge with catch-up. FR7 Rent Updates/Renewals → RE Rent History (50010) + ApplyRentUpdate/ApplyRentIndexation + RenewContract. FR8 Sublease → 'Is Sublease'/'Parent Contract No.'/'Sublease Area Sqm' fields + ValidateSublease. FR9 Termination → StartCancellation + CloseContract + ReleaseProperty. FR10 Accounting → CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling builds standard Sales Header/Line + RE Audit Entry (50012).

Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code

attribution: agent_code

L4: only ONE permissionset is shipped ('RE RENTAL MGT' with full RIMD across all tables). The spec requires 4 distinct user roles (Property Manager, Contract/Legal Administrator, Finance User, Administrator) each with bounded permissions. A single all-RIMD set does not satisfy role segregation.

permissionset 50000 "RE RENTAL MGT" { Assignable = true; Caption = 'Rental Management'; Permissions = tabledata "RE Project" = RIMD, ... }

attribution: agent_code

Opus 4.7 · run 1Pass
L4: all nine spec'd negative cases are guarded explicitly with Error() calls. Overlapping allocation, double-billing, missing signatory, sublease area, sub-sublease, mid-year IBI catch-up, locked-field tampering, deposit refund total balance, and failed/no-doc-no deposit posting — each has a concrete check.
CODERentalManagement.al:1233-1244
PreventOverlappingAllocation: errors 'Property %1 is already allocated to contract %2 for an overlapping period.'
CODERentalManagement.al:1272-1283
EnsureNotAlreadyScheduled: errors on duplicate (contract, concept, period start, period end)
CODERentalManagement.al:1228
Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code") — missing-signatory guard
CODERentalManagement.al:1263-1264
if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" then Error('Sublease area exceeds the available sublease area for property %1.')
CODERentalManagement.al:1257-1258
if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error('Sub-subleasing is not allowed.')
CODERentalManagement.al:1153-1186
UpdateIBIRecharge: AlreadyBilled scanned from invoiced TaxIBI rows for the tax year; CatchUpAmount = AnnualAmount - AlreadyBilled — avoids double-billing of already-invoiced periods
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
8 of 9 spec'd negative cases have explicit guarded code paths: overlapping allocation, double-billing, missing signatory, sublease area excess, sub-sublease attempt, mid-year IBI catch-up, locked field tampering, incorrect deposit refund balance. (Failed deposit posting is the only edge case not explicitly guarded in a dedicated branch; it relies on Insert's standard error propagation.) Comfortably above the 6-of-9 threshold.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:599-615
PreventOverlappingAllocation iterates other contracts on same property where Status=Active|Cancellation, calls DatesOverlap, raises OverlapErr.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:669-679,214-215
BillingExists() guard + GenerateOneTimeBilling raises DoubleBillingErr if same period exists.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:583-596
ValidateContractReady → Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code") + lookup of signatory record in project, else Error.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:642-645
Sublease area guard: if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > AvailableArea then Error(SubleaseAreaErr, ...).
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:631-632
Sub-sublease guard: if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error(SubSubleaseErr).
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:478-517
UpdateIBIRecharge computes AlreadyBilled (sum of existing TaxIBI billing for the tax year) and only bills the CatchUpAmount = AnnualAmount - AlreadyBilled — avoids double-billing the year.
DartsFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Of 9 negative cases in the spec, only 4 are guarded in code (overlap, double-billing, failed deposit, locked-field tampering). 5 are entirely missing (signatory validation, sublease area, sub-sublease, mid-year IBI, deposit refund/settlement at termination). 4/9 < the 6/9 majority required to pass.
CODETab50104.RentalContractLine.al:131-190 (CheckOverlappingLeases)
1) Overlap allocation ✓ — OverlapErr guards new lines
CODETab50107.RentalInvoiceLink.al:98-107 + Cod50101.RentalBillingEngine.al:49-52
2) Double-billing ✓ — PeriodAlreadyBilled guard
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:253-335 (PostDepositReceipt)
3) Failed deposit posting ✓ — AlreadyPostedErr, NoDepositRequiredErr, NoDepositAmountErr, NoTenantErr, NoAccountErr
CODETab50103.RentalContract.al TestStatusIsDraft on fields 2/4/6/7/50/51/52/53
8) Locked-field tampering ✓ — every critical field calls TestStatusIsDraft() which errors with MustBeDraftErr
BASHgrep -ri 'signatory|sublease|IBI|deposit refund|settlement' workspace/src/
4) Missing signatory ✗, 5) Sublease area ✗, 6) Sub-sublease ✗, 7) Mid-year IBI ✗, 9) Deposit refund/settlement ✗ — all 5 absent

F-01 · weight 2
Rebate Agreement can be created with header + rule line
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 40% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)PASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 Playwright: step1 opened the Rebate Agreement card and entered all header fields (No.='VR-OPUS47-1' suffixed to avoid collision per rule D, Description, Agreement Type=Vendor Rebate, Vendor No.=10000, Starting Date=2023-01-01, Ending Date=2026-12-31). Card saved with no errors (Status=Draft, Version No.=1 set by OnInsert). step2 expanded the Rebate Agreement Lines subpage and added one line referencing rule code OPUS47-PURCH2 (Rule Basis=Purchase Amount, Calculation Method=Percentage, Percentage=2, Minimum Amount=200). Line saved with no validation errors.
SCREENSHOTF01_step1_header_filled.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
SCREENSHOTF01_step2_rule_line_added_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODERebateTables.al:89-94
trigger OnInsert() begin Rec.Status := Rec.Status::Draft; if Rec."Version No." = 0 then Rec."Version No." := 1; end;

attribution: agent_code
L3 Playwright drove Submit → Approve → Activate via Actions menu on the agreement card. Submit & Approve worked: status transitioned Draft → Pending Approval → Approved. But repeated Activate clicks fail: the BC web client raised the dialog 'Active agreements must be revised through the agreement management action.' (the OnModify trigger). After dismissing, the agreement list still shows Status=Approved and the Rebate Agreement Versions page (50005) is empty ('There is nothing to show in this view'). No Version 1 record was ever persisted. The candidate's OnModify trigger blocks Modify on Active xRec, but the page action framework's auto-save round-trip causes the activation transaction to roll back. agent_code.

procedure Activate(var Agreement: ...) begin if Agreement.Status <> Agreement.Status::Approved then Error(InvalidStatusErr, ...); Agreement.ValidateForActivation(); CreateVersion(Agreement, 'Activated'); Agreement.Status := Agreement.Status::Active; Agreement."Activated At" := CurrentDateTime();

L3 Playwright: RBT Agreement Card has an EMPTY menubar — no 'Submit for Approval', 'Approve', or 'Activate' action exists on the card. L4: the Agreement Card definition (page 50124) contains no `actions { }` block. The Activate / SubmitAgreement / ApproveAgreement procedures exist in codeunits but are never wired to any page action. Status combobox lets users freely set 'Active' bypassing TestField checks.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3: Created Calculation Request CR-OPUS47-1 (Request Type=Calculation, Agreement No.=VR-OPUS47-1) and invoked 'Run'. Request transitions to Status=Completed with no error, but the Rebate Calculation Entries page (50010) is empty. Two compounded defects: (1) the codeunit filters on `Agreement.SetRange(Status, Agreement.Status::Active)` and my agreement is stuck at Approved due to F-02; (2) more importantly, the codeunit never iterates posted purchase invoices/sales invoices/value entries — it loops `AgreementLine`s and writes one calc entry per line using `AgreementLine."Minimum Amount"` as the base, NOT the amount from any source transaction. There is no `Purch. Inv. Header`/`Purch. Inv. Line`/`Sales Invoice Line` reference anywhere in RebateCalculationEngine, so the spec's 'Entries reference source purchase invoice numbers' can never hold. agent_code.

L3 Playwright: created Calc Request CALC-001 (Agreement=VEND-REB-001, Mode=Accrual, Vendor Filter=10000, dates left blank so all dates allowed), then invoked 'Run Calculation'. Request showed Started At=6/7/2026 1:30 PM and Completed At=6/7/2026 1:30 PM in the list, but Source Lines Processed/Eligible and Calculated Amount remained BLANK. L1: navigated to the Calc Ledger Entries page (50012) — the page reports 'There is nothing to show in this view' (verified via DOM inspect: `body.textContent.includes('There is nothing to show in this view') === true`). Vendor 10000 has 3 posted purchase invoices per sandbox_facts (107209/107210/107211), yet the eligibility engine matched 0 source lines. Root cause in L4: RebateEligibilityEngine unconditionally forces `PurchInvLine.SetRange(Type, PurchInvLine.Type::Item)` even when Agreement."Item No." is blank, which mis-filters away non-Item purchase lines that real vendor invoices typically include — and combined with the Status never reaching Completed (see F-04), the run produced no usable output.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4 review: `PostAccrual` only flips `AccrualEntry.Status` to Posted and calls `Modify`. It never creates a `Gen. Journal Line`, never calls `GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck`, never inserts G/L entries. The Rebate Posting Setup's Accrual Debit/Credit accounts are read by ValidateAgreement but never used to actually post anything. Spec pass_if 'Real G/L entries posted (not just status flip)' — the code IS just a status flip. The runtime workflow is also unreachable because F-03 produces no accrual candidates and F-02 keeps the agreement out of Active state. agent_code.
