Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I
Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L4: ApplyCurrencyConversion resolves the partner Currency Rule, calls GetCurrencyExchangeRate which reads the standard Currency Exchange Rate table for the Posting Date and writes Exchange Rate + Exchange Rate Amount onto each line (read-only). MissingExchangeRateErr blocks posting when no rate is available; InvalidExchangeRateErr blocks zero rates. The exchange rate IS persisted on the line at processing time. UI walkthrough blocked — IC Partner / No. Series not seeded; cannot drive cross-currency posting interactively. L4 alone is insufficient for a functional PASS.
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:239-260
ExchRate := GetCurrencyExchangeRate(EffectiveCurrCode, PostingDate, ReqLine."Line No."); ReqLine."Currency Code" := EffectiveCurrCode; ReqLine."Exchange Rate" := ExchRate; ReqLine."Exchange Rate Amount" := Round(ReqLine."Recharge Amount" / ExchRate, GetRoundingPrecision());
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:306-323
if not CurrExchRate.FindLast() then Error(MissingExchangeRateErr, CurrencyCode, PostingDate, LineNo); if (ExchRateAmt = 0) or (RelationalAmt = 0) then Error(InvalidExchangeRateErr, CurrencyCode, PostingDate, LineNo);
CODETab50103.ICRechargeRequestLine.al:205-221
field(20; "Exchange Rate"; Decimal) { ... Editable = false; ... } field(21; "Exchange Rate Amount"; Decimal) { ... Editable = false; ... }

AccountMapping.SetRange("Partner Code", RequestHeader."Partner Code"); AccountMapping.SetRange("Recharge Type", RequestHeader."Recharge Type"); AccountMapping.SetRange("Source G/L Account", RequestLine."Source G/L Account"); if AccountMapping.IsEmpty() then Error('Account mapping is missing for partner %1, recharge type %2, source account %3.', ...); ... if DimensionMapping.IsEmpty() then Error('Dimension mapping is missing for partner %1, dimension %2 value %3.', ...);

attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked. L4: IC Partner Code is enforced via TestField in ValidateRechargeRequest (proven at runtime in F-01). However ValidateMappings uses Message (not Error), meaning unmapped dimensions only WARN and do not block posting — the Recharge-Post codeunit never calls ValidateMappings before posting. Posting is blocked for missing partner mapping but NOT blocked for missing dimension translation, contrary to the spec.
CODECod50000.RechargeManagement.al:17-25
RechargeLine.TestField("Recharge Partner Code"); RechargeLine.TestField("IC Partner Code"); RechargeLine.TestField("Source G/L Account No."); RechargeLine.TestField("Target IC G/L Account No.");
CODECod50004.RechargeReconciliation.al:54-76
procedure ValidateMappings(...) ... RechargeDimensionMapping.SetRange("Recharge Partner Code", RechargeLine."Recharge Partner Code"); if RechargeDimensionMapping.IsEmpty then UnmappedFound := true; if UnmappedFound then begin Message('Unmapped dimensions found for recharge %1.', RechargeHeader."No."); exit(false); end;

attribution: agent_code
L4: posting partially guards mapping — TestField(IC Partner Code) prevents posting without a partner, and IC G/L Account is via standard TableRelation. But there is NO target-company dimension-translation routine, no per-partner dimension-mapping table, and no exception-queue routing of mapping failures. The spec explicitly requires 'validation of unmapped accounts or dimension values before posting' and the agent's implementation doesn't translate dimensions across companies. Partial implementation = fail.

RechargeHeader.TestField("IC Partner Code"); RechargeHeader.TestField("Posting Date"); RechargeHeader.TestField("Document Date"); EnsureLinesExist(RechargeHeader);

attribution: agent_code
L4: Self-approval is blocked by user-id check. However the approval threshold only sets an 'Amount Requires Escalation' flag — it does NOT enforce any role-based routing or block the Approver based on permissions/role. Any authenticated user can call Approve(); there is no permission set or role gating because the extension ships NO permission sets (see S-04). The Analyst-cannot-approve-high-value half of the spec is not enforced — only segregation (self-approval) is. Partial implementation.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:215-241
if RequestHeader.Status <> RequestHeader.Status::"Pending Approval" then Error('Only pending approval recharge requests can be approved.'); if RequestHeader."Submitted By" = UserId() then Error('Users cannot approve their own recharge request.');
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:194-201
Threshold := GetThreshold(RequestHeader."Partner Code"); ... ApprovalEntry."Amount Requires Escalation" := (Threshold <> 0) and (RequestHeader."Source Amount" > Threshold);

attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked. L4: Permission sets gate the Analyst to read-only on Approval Entry (cannot approve) and exclude Recharge-Post codeunit from Analyst/Manager, so segregation-of-duties is enforced via table/codeunit permissions. Approval threshold logic is implemented. However the bespoke ApproveRequest procedure in Cod50000 does NOT check that the approver is different from the submitter — segregation is enforced only by permission sets at the codeunit/table level, not by the approval routing logic itself. The platform Approvals Management framework (which provides self-approval guards) is NOT used (see C-04). Partial coverage of the spec.
CODECod50000.RechargeManagement.al:143-173
local procedure CheckApprovalRequired(RechargeHeader: Record "Recharge Header"): Boolean ... Threshold := RechargeSetup."Default Approval Threshold"; ... if TotalAmount > Threshold then exit(true);
CODEPermissionSet50000.RechargeAnalyst.al:8-13

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: the candidate's 'approval' is purely a status flip performed by any user with access to the page. AdvanceStatus moves Pending Approval → Approved with no role check, no approval threshold, no UserSetup substitute lookup, and no segregation-of-duties guard. There is no integration with the platform Approvals Management/Workflow framework. An Analyst can trivially click Advance Status past the approval step. The criterion (threshold-enforced approval routing + SoD-blocked self-approval) is unsatisfied.
CODETab50102.ICRechargeRequestHeader.al:205-234
// AdvanceStatus blindly flips Status::Validated → Pending Approval → Approved → Posted // with no approver lookup, no threshold check, no SoD guard Status::"Pending Approval": Validate(Status, Status::Approved);

attribution: agent_code
L4: Implementation uses a CUSTOM ICR Processing Queue table — it does NOT integrate with BC's standard IC Inbox/Outbox tables (no use of "IC Outbox Transaction", "IC Inbox Transaction", "ICInboxOutboxMgt", or "IC Setup" codeunits). The Accept/Reject actions only flip a status field on the custom queue table; no real intercompany handoff to a partner company occurs. Rejection routes to exception via the header's Exception Flag but there is no real partner-side processing. The spec says "transfer to intercompany outbox" and "receipt and processing through intercompany inbox" — both refer to BC's standard mechanics which the implementation bypasses.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:341-371
procedure AcceptQueue(var ProcessingQueue: Record "ICR Processing Queue") ... ProcessingQueue.Status := ProcessingQueue.Status::Accepted; ProcessingQueue."Last Attempt At" := CurrentDateTime(); ProcessingQueue.Modify(true);
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:326-331
ProcessingQueue.Init(); ProcessingQueue."Request No." := RequestHeader."No."; ProcessingQueue.Direction := ProcessingQueue.Direction::Outbox; ProcessingQueue.Status := ProcessingQueue.Status::Pending; ProcessingQueue.Insert(true);

L3 blocked. L4: The posting codeunit writes to standard BC 'IC Outbox Jnl. Line', i.e. it correctly hands off to the native IC outbox mechanism. However NO custom code handles inbox-side acceptance, rejection, or correction routing within the extension — there is no Recharge-side inbox processor or rejection-routing codeunit (only the standard BC IC inbox would surface entries). The acceptance/rejection/resubmission lifecycle expected by the spec is not implemented in the extension.
CODECod50001.RechargePost.al:80-112
ICOutboxJnlLine.Init(); ICOutboxJnlLine."Transaction No." := TransactionNo; ICOutboxJnlLine."IC Partner Code" := PostedRechargeLine."IC Partner Code"; ... ICOutboxJnlLine.Insert(true);

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: the extension relies entirely on standard BC's IC engine — by writing IC Partner Code on Gen Jnl lines and posting via Gen. Jnl.-Post Batch, outbox entries are created. There is NO partner-side acceptance/rejection routing, NO correction workflow code, and NO routing of rejected inbox transactions to an exception queue. The spec specifically requires that 'a rejected inbox transaction without proper correction is not silently completed and routes to correction/exception handling' — this is missing.
CODECod50101.ICRechargePostMgt.al:65-130
// CreateICGeneralJournalLines sets IC Partner Code + IC Account No/Type on each Gen Jnl Line // then PostJournalBatch calls Codeunit::"Gen. Jnl.-Post Batch"

L4: Approval is implemented as a bespoke ICR Workflow Mgt. codeunit writing to a custom ICR Approval Entry table. ZERO usage of BC's standard "Approvals Mgmt." codeunit, "Workflow" framework, OnSendDocForApproval events, or standard Approval Entry table. This is a side-channel implementation, not the platform framework the spec requires.
BASHgrep -E '(Approvals Mgmt|ApprovalsMgmt|WorkflowMgt|Approval Workflow|OnSendDocForApproval)' workspace/src/*.al
0 matches — no use of BC's native Approvals Management framework, no Workflow events, no Codeunit "Approvals Mgmt."
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:181-281
codeunit 50003 "ICR Workflow Mgt." procedure SubmitForApproval(var RequestHeader: Record "ICR Request Header") ... ApprovalEntry.Init(); // candidate's own ICR Approval Entry table

L4: The extension uses a fully bespoke approval engine — its own 'Recharge Approval Entry' table and ApproveRequest/RejectRequest procedures — and does NOT call Codeunit 'Approvals Mgmt.', does NOT subscribe to workflow events (OnSendDocForApproval, OnCancelDocApproval), and does NOT integrate with the standard 'Workflow' / 'Approval User Setup' framework. This directly contradicts the spec requirement to reuse the native Approvals Management framework.

Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Post() has three guards (status, posting reference, existing entries). Reverse() has two guards (status, already-reversed marker). Reversal entries link to Original Entry No. for traceability. The idempotency intent is clear — though the underlying 'posting' is fake (custom ledger). Re-running posting on a posted request will Error rather than create duplicate output.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:293-300
if RequestHeader.Status <> RequestHeader.Status::Approved then Error(...); if RequestHeader."Posting Reference" <> '' then Error('Recharge request %1 has already been posted.', RequestHeader."No."); PostingEntry.SetRange("Request No.", RequestHeader."No."); if not PostingEntry.IsEmpty() then Error('Posting entries already exist for request %1.', RequestHeader."No.");
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:383-386
if RequestHeader.Status <> RequestHeader.Status::Posted then Error(...); if RequestHeader."Reversed By Request No." <> '' then Error('Recharge request %1 has already been reversed.', RequestHeader."No.");
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: posting guards on Status=Approved, 'Posted Document No.' empty, and not Reversed — three idempotency checks. Reversal guards on PostedRechargeHeader.Reversed. Reversal correctly links Posted document to reversal document via 'Reversal Document No.' and back-links the original Recharge Header. Posted Recharge Header/Line tables have no exposed Modify actions on relevant pages (Editable = false on key fields), supporting immutability. Replay-safe by status guard.
CODECod50001.RechargePost.al:31-38
if RechargeHeader.Status <> RechargeHeader.Status::Approved then Error(NotApprovedErr, RechargeHeader."No."); if RechargeHeader."Posted Document No." <> '' then Error(DuplicatePostingErr, ...); if RechargeHeader.Reversed then Error(AlreadyReversedErr, RechargeHeader."No.");
CODECod50002.RechargeReversal.al:14-15,56-60
if PostedRechargeHeader.Reversed then Error('Recharge document %1 has already been reversed.', ...); ... PostedRechargeHeader.Reversed := true; PostedRechargeHeader."Reversal Document No." := ReversalDocNo;
CODECod50002.RechargeReversal.al:62-67
if RechargeHeader.Get(PostedRechargeHeader."Pre-Assigned No.") then begin RechargeHeader.Status := RechargeHeader.Status::Reversed; RechargeHeader."Reversal Document No." := ReversalDocNo;
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: posting is guarded by the Posted boolean (read-only, set once at end of successful post), AlreadyPostedErr is raised on rerun, and stale Gen Jnl Lines left over from a previous failed attempt are cleared before re-writing — that's a basic but real idempotency design. However, no replay-safety on correction (no reposting / linkage routine) since reversal isn't implemented. Idempotency on posting itself is adequate to PASS this code-quality criterion.
CODETab50102.ICRechargeRequestHeader.al:134-140
field(18; "Posted"; Boolean) { ... Editable = false; ToolTip = 'Indicates whether this IC Recharge Request has already been posted to the IC General Journal and IC Outbox. Prevents duplicate posting.'; }
CODECod50101.ICRechargePostMgt.al:23-25,42-47
if RechargeHeader."Posted" then Error(AlreadyPostedErr, RechargeHeader."No."); // also clears any leftover lines in the IC batch before re-writing GenJnlLine.DeleteAll(true);

Source Company = Recharge Header."Source Company" + CompanyName(); Target/IC Partner = Recharge Partner table + Header line IC Partner Code; Recharge Type = Header field; Allocation Basis = Recharge Allocation Rule table (Cod ...AllocationRule with Allocation Method enum); Source/Target IC G/L = Recharge Partner fields 40/41; Dimension Mapping Rules = Tab50006; Currency Handling = Currency Code + Currency Factor on lines; Approval Threshold = Setup field 40 + Partner field 50; Auto-Send/Auto-Accept = Setup fields 30/31 + Partner fields 60/61.

L4: setup and partner tables exist and capture Source Company (via current Company), Target/IC Partner, Source and Target IC G/L Accounts, Currency Handling Rule (enum), Auto-Send / Auto-Accept flags. MISSING per the minimum-data spec: Recharge Type (the partner Recharge Method enum is the only proxy and lacks 'service type/cost pool' semantics), Approval Threshold (no field anywhere — and no UI for it), explicit Dimension Mapping Rules (only Shortcut Dimensions on the line; no per-partner translation rule table). Several required fields are absent.
CODETab50100.ICRechargeSetup.al + Tab50101.ICRechargePartner.al

L4: Zero permission set (.PermissionSet.al or 'permissionset' object) declarations. The Recharge Analyst, Recharge Manager, Finance Poster, Intercompany Administrator, and Auditor roles are not modeled. There is no role-gating in code beyond the self-approval check in Approve(). The five-role boundary matrix the spec requires is entirely absent.
BASHgrep -E 'permissionset|PermissionSet' workspace/src/*.al
0 matches — there is NO permission set object anywhere in the codebase
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: All five roles are represented as Assignable=true permission sets. Boundaries enforced via tabledata permissions and codeunit access — Analyst lacks Recharge-Post (cannot post), Manager lacks Recharge-Post (cannot post final), Finance Poster has Posted tables RIMD + Recharge-Post X (can post but read-only on Recharge Partner), IC Admin manages setup/partners/dimensions, Auditor is read-only across all tables. Matches the role matrix.
CODEPermissionSet50000.RechargeAnalyst.al:1-26
permissionset 50000 "Recharge Analyst" ... tabledata "Posted Recharge Header" = R, tabledata "Recharge Approval Entry" = R, (no codeunit Recharge-Post X)
CODEPermissionSet50001.RechargeManager.al:6-30
tabledata "Posted Recharge Header" = R, tabledata "Recharge Approval Entry" = RIMD, codeunit "Recharge Management" = X (no Recharge-Post)
CODEPermissionSet50002.FinancePoster.al:6-19
tabledata "Posted Recharge Header" = RIMD, codeunit "Recharge-Post" = X, codeunit "Recharge Reversal" = X
CODEPermissionSet50003.ICAdmin.al:1-39
tabledata "Recharge Setup" = RIMD, tabledata "Recharge Partner" = RIMD, tabledata "Recharge Dimension Mapping" = RIMD
CODEPermissionSet50004.Auditor.al:1-21
tabledata ... = R (read-only across all recharge tables)

L4: Audit log table exists and is populated by the Log() procedure with old/new status + user + action + message — sufficient as an audit trail. However: (1) there is NO reconciliation page or codeunit that ties source amount, allocation output, and posted entries together with filters by company/partner/period/status/currency as the spec requires, and (2) the audit log table is NOT immutable — fields are not Editable=false at the table level and no OnModify/OnDelete trigger blocks tampering. No export action is provided. Partial implementation.
CODEICRechargeLedger.Table.al:249-307
table 50009 "ICR Audit Log" { fields { Entry No., Request No., Action, User ID, Created At, Message, Old Status, New Status } keys { key(Request; "Request No.", "Created At") } }
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Recharge Audit Log table records action types Created/Modified/Validated/Submitted/Approved/Rejected/Posted/Reversed/Deleted with user, datetime, old/new values, source table/field. Audit log writes are AutoIncrement (no key allowed to be modified) and posted tables are not exposed for edit on pages, supporting immutability. Reconciliation codeunit compares source vs allocated vs posted totals. Reconciliation report exists. Sufficient for the spec.
CODETab50007.RechargeAuditLog.al:7-75
field(10; "Document No.") field(20; "Action Type"; Option) ... OptionMembers = Created,Modified,Validated,Submitted,Approved,Rejected,Posted,Reversed,Deleted; field(30; "User ID") field(40; "Date Time") field(50; Description) field(60; "Old Value") field(61; "New Value")
CODECod50004.RechargeReconciliation.al:3-32
procedure ReconcileRechargeDocument(...) ... TotalSource += PostedRechargeLine."Source Amount"; TotalAllocated += PostedRechargeLine."Allocated Amount"; ... BalanceVariance := PostedRechargeHeader."Total Allocated Amount" - TotalAllocated;

Report 50000 'Recharge Reconciliation' (RDL layout exists in workspace)

L4: no dedicated audit-log table, no reconciliation page that compares source amount vs allocation output vs posted entries, no exportable change log, and no immutability enforcement beyond the per-record Posted flag. Header carries only Created By / Created/Modified DateTime — far short of the spec's exportable, immutable audit log + reconciliation views by company/partner/period/status/currency.

L4: ICR Processing Queue page exposes all 6 required states (Pending, Processing, Accepted, Rejected, Failed, Reversed). However there is no dedicated exception queue page (failures rely on the Exception Flag on the request header + Last Error text). No dashboard / role-center cue tile is wired up. The list page satisfies the visibility-of-states half but not the dedicated exception queue half.
CODEICRechargeMonitor.Pages.al:1-62
page 50008 "ICR Processing Queue" { PageType = List; UsageCategory = Tasks; layout: shows Status (Pending, Processing, Accepted, Rejected, Failed, Reversed) actions: Accept, Reject }
CODEICRechargeEnums.al:59-69
enum 50005 "ICR Queue Status" { value(0; Pending); value(1; Processing); value(2; Accepted); value(3; Rejected); value(4; Failed); value(5; Reversed); }
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Cue table exposes Draft / Pending Approval / Approved / Posted Today / Posted This Week count tiles via FlowFields. Activities/Role Center pages render the cue. Exception Flag + Exception Reason on Recharge Header support routing failed recharges to an exception queue (filterable in the request list). The spec's required states 'pending, failed, accepted, rejected, reversed' are all derivable from the Status enum + Exception Flag; cue coverage is partial (no explicit Failed/Accepted/Rejected tile but they are reachable via status filters).
CODETab50010.RechargeCue.al:7-58
field(10; Draft; ...) field(11; "Pending Approval"; ...) field(12; Approved; ...) field(20; "Posted Today"; ...) field(21; "Posted This Week"; ...)
CODEPag50013.RechargeActivities.al & Pag50012.RechargeRoleCenter.al
Recharge Activities page (Cue) + Recharge Role Center page exist in workspace

field(110; "Exception Flag"; Boolean) field(111; "Exception Reason"; Text[250])

attribution: agent_code

BASHls workspace/src/Pages/ && grep -rEi 'Monitor|Dashboard|Exception Queue|Failed Queue' workspace/src/
Pages shipped: Setup Card, Partner List/Card, Request List/Card, Request Lines. No monitoring/dashboard page, no exception-queue page or table.

Codex (GPT-5 CLI)PASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Validation rejects the partner config Auto Accept + Manual Review Required combo. SubmitForApproval flags the request with Exception Flag when manual review is required OR when amount exceeds threshold. The threshold-escalation flag is set but does not by itself BLOCK the Approve() call from completing — the spec asks that auto-accept can't complete a recharge requiring manual review, which is enforced via Validation. Adequate for the spec wording.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:117-118
if Partner."Auto Accept" and Partner."Manual Review Required" then Error('Partner %1 cannot auto-accept when manual review is required.', RequestHeader."Partner Code");
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:201-211
ApprovalEntry."Amount Requires Escalation" := (Threshold <> 0) and (RequestHeader."Source Amount" > Threshold); ... RequestHeader."Exception Flag" := ApprovalEntry."Amount Requires Escalation" or Partner."Manual Review Required"; if RequestHeader."Exception Flag" then RequestHeader."Exception Text" := CopyStr('Approval escalation or manual review required.', ...);
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: AutoProcessRechargeQueue routes every auto-process candidate through SubmitForApproval, which calls CheckApprovalRequired — only setting Status to Approved if BELOW threshold and NOT an exception. If approval is required, Status becomes Pending Approval and the immediate RechargePost.Run is gated by the `if Status = Approved` check, so auto-accept cannot complete a recharge that exceeds the threshold or carries an Exception Flag. The auto-accept guard against bypassing manual review is implemented correctly.
CODECod50003.RechargeAutomation.al:27-58
AutoSend := true; ... if not RechargePartner."Auto-Send" then AutoSend := false; ... RechargeMgt.SubmitForApproval(RechargeHeader); if RechargeHeader.Status = RechargeHeader.Status::Approved then begin RechargePost.Run(RechargeHeader);
CODECod50000.RechargeManagement.al:64-95,143-173
SubmitForApproval ... if not CheckApprovalRequired(RechargeHeader) then begin Status := Approved else Status := Pending Approval ... if TotalAmount > Threshold then exit(true);
DartsFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Auto-Send and Auto-Accept booleans exist on Setup and Partner master, but they are dead fields — neither the calculation codeunit nor the post-management codeunit nor the header's status flow ever reads them. There is also no Approval Threshold or manual-review gate that they could be overridden by. The spec requires auto-accept to be overridden by manual-review / threshold logic; with no threshold logic in the codebase at all, this requirement cannot be satisfied.
CODETab50100.ICRechargeSetup.al:46-55 + Tab50101.ICRechargePartner.al:86-100
Auto-Send / Auto-Accept boolean fields on Setup AND Partner
BASHgrep -rEi 'Auto.?Send|Auto.?Accept' workspace/src/Codeunits/ workspace/src/Tables/Tab50102*
0 hits in posting/approval/calculation codeunits or the request header — flags are declared but never consulted in business logic
Gemini_CLIN/A
Speckit + ClaudeN/A
Speckit + CodexN/A
Speckit + GeminiN/A

PTE Hello World 01-pte-hello-world

Minimal Customer-table extension with a Hello World note field and a 3-character validation. Sanity-check task.

spec.md129 lines

Feature Specification: Business Central Hello World Extension

Feature Branch: feat/rebate-setup-seeding-layer

Created: 2026-06-13

Status: Draft

Input: User description: "Technical Requirement Document – Minimal "Hello World" Extension for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central. This requirement defines a minimal per-tenant AL extension that extends the standard Customer table, adds one custom field, shows it on the Customer Card, and includes a small validation rule to demonstrate core Business Central extensibility."

User Scenarios & Testing (mandatory)

User Story 1 - Add Custom Note Field (Priority: P1)

As a Business Central user managing customer records, I need to add a custom "Hello World Note" field to capture demo notes on customer records, so that I can demonstrate Business Central extensibility capabilities.

Why this priority: This is the core functionality that delivers the primary value - extending the Customer table with a custom field and making it visible to users. Without this, the extension has no purpose.

Independent Test: Can be fully tested by opening the Customer Card, verifying the "Hello World Note" field is visible in the General section, entering a note with at least 3 characters, saving the record, and confirming the value persists after closing and reopening the customer card. Delivers immediate value by allowing users to store custom notes on customer records.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a Business Central environment with the extension installed, When a user opens an existing customer card, Then the "Hello World Note" field is visible in the General FastTab with the caption "Hello World Note"
  2. Given the Customer Card is open, When the user enters a valid note (e.g., "Test note") with 3 or more characters and saves the record, Then the system saves the note successfully without errors
  3. Given a customer record with a saved note, When the user closes and reopens the same customer card, Then the saved note value remains visible in the "Hello World Note" field
  4. Given the Customer Card is open, When the user leaves the "Hello World Note" field blank and saves the record, Then the system saves successfully (blank values are allowed)

User Story 2 - Validate Minimum Length (Priority: P2)

As a Business Central user, when I enter a note shorter than 3 characters (but not blank), I need the system to prevent me from saving and display a clear error message, so that data quality standards are enforced.

Why this priority: This demonstrates the validation capabilities of AL extensions and ensures data quality. While important for demonstrating Business Central's validation framework, the field can still function without this validation.

Independent Test: Can be fully tested by opening a customer card, entering "Hi" (2 characters) in the "Hello World Note" field, attempting to move to another field or save, and verifying that the system displays the error "Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters" and prevents the invalid value from being saved. Delivers value by enforcing data quality rules.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given the Customer Card is open, When the user enters exactly 2 characters (e.g., "Hi") in the "Hello World Note" field and moves to another field, Then the system displays the error "Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters" and prevents the value from being saved
  2. Given the Customer Card is open, When the user enters exactly 3 characters (e.g., "ABC"), Then the system accepts the value and saves successfully without errors
  3. Given the user has entered an invalid 2-character value, When the error is displayed, Then the user can correct the value to 3+ characters or clear it completely to proceed

User Story 3 - Create New Customers with Custom Field (Priority: P3)

As a Business Central user creating new customer records, I need the "Hello World Note" field to be available and functional during customer creation, so that I can capture notes from the moment the customer record is created.

Why this priority: This ensures the extension works for both existing and new customer records, providing a complete user experience. However, the primary value is already delivered by P1 and P2 for existing records.

Independent Test: Can be fully tested by clicking "New Customer" in Business Central, filling in mandatory customer fields (Name, etc.), entering a valid note in the "Hello World Note" field, saving the new customer, and verifying the field value is stored correctly. Delivers value by ensuring the extension supports the complete customer lifecycle.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given the user is creating a new customer record, When the user fills in mandatory fields and enters a valid "Hello World Note" (3+ characters), Then the new customer is created successfully with the note value saved
  2. Given the user is creating a new customer record, When the user fills in mandatory fields but leaves "Hello World Note" blank, Then the new customer is created successfully (the field is optional)
  3. Given the user is creating a new customer record, When the user enters an invalid 2-character note, Then the system displays the validation error and prevents creation until corrected or cleared

Edge Cases

  • What happens when a user enters exactly 100 characters (the field's maximum length)?
  • How does the system handle special characters, Unicode, or emoji in the note field?
  • What happens when multiple users edit the same customer record simultaneously?
  • How does the validation behave when the field is modified programmatically via API or batch processes?
  • What happens when users attempt to import customer data with invalid note lengths via Excel or data migration tools?

Requirements (mandatory)

Functional Requirements

  • FR-001: System MUST extend the standard Customer table with a new field named "PTE Hello World Note" using object ID 50100 within the per-tenant extension range (50000-99999)
  • FR-002: The custom field MUST be of type Text with a maximum length of 100 characters
  • FR-003: The custom field MUST have the caption "Hello World Note" displayed to users
  • FR-004: The custom field MUST be classified as CustomerContent for data classification compliance
  • FR-005: System MUST validate that non-blank field values contain at least 3 characters
  • FR-006: System MUST display the error message "Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters" when validation fails
  • FR-007: System MUST allow blank/empty values in the "Hello World Note" field (the field is optional)
  • FR-008: System MUST expose the custom field on the Customer Card page in the General FastTab
  • FR-009: The page extension MUST use object ID 50110 within the per-tenant extension range (50000-99999)
  • FR-010: The custom field MUST be visible in ApplicationArea All
  • FR-011: The custom field MUST display the tooltip "Specifies a short demo note used to demonstrate Business Central extensibility."
  • FR-012: System MUST implement the extension using only tableextension and pageextension objects (no direct modification of base Microsoft objects)
  • FR-013: All custom objects and fields MUST use the "PTE" prefix for naming consistency
  • FR-014: The extension MUST compile cleanly without warnings or errors
  • FR-015: The extension MUST be packaged as a deployable .app file
  • FR-016: The validation logic MUST be triggered via the OnValidate trigger on the field
  • FR-017: System MUST persist the custom field value to the database table
  • FR-018: The custom field MUST support standard Customer table operations (create, read, update, delete)
  • FR-019: Standard Customer table fields and functionality MUST remain unaffected by the extension (no regression)

Key Entities

  • Customer: The standard Business Central Customer table (base object) that will be extended. Represents customer master data including name, address, contact information, and business relationships. The extension adds one custom field to this entity without modifying the base object directly.
  • PTE Hello World Note: A custom text field (max 100 characters) added to the Customer entity. Stores demo notes for demonstrating Business Central extensibility. Optional field with minimum length validation when non-blank (≥3 characters required).

Success Criteria (mandatory)

Measurable Outcomes

  • SC-001: Users can open the Customer Card and view the "Hello World Note" field in the General section without any errors or performance degradation
  • SC-002: Users can successfully save customer records with valid notes (3+ characters or blank) in under 2 seconds
  • SC-003: The system blocks 100% of invalid note entries (1-2 characters) with a clear error message before allowing save
  • SC-004: Customer records with saved notes retain their values with 100% accuracy after closing and reopening
  • SC-005: The extension deploys to sandbox/UAT environment without compilation errors or runtime failures
  • SC-006: 100% of standard Customer Card functionality remains operational after extension installation (zero regression)
  • SC-007: New customer creation with the custom field completes successfully in under 3 seconds
  • SC-008: All 15 test scenarios defined in the technical requirements pass during User Acceptance Testing
  • SC-009: Business users can complete the UAT sign-off checklist with all critical items marked "Pass"
  • SC-010: The extension code adheres to AL best practices with zero base object modifications (verified by code review)

Assumptions

  • The target environment is Dynamics 365 Business Central running a recent runtime version that supports AL extensions and the extension object model
  • Developers have access to Visual Studio Code with the AL Language extension installed and configured for Business Central development
  • Testers have access to a Sandbox or UAT environment separate from production for safe testing and validation
  • The organization has allocated object ID range 50000-99999 for per-tenant customizations without conflicts from other extensions
  • Users performing testing have standard customer maintenance permissions in Business Central (no additional custom permissions required)
  • The extension will be initially deployed to a non-production environment for User Acceptance Testing before production deployment
  • The "PTE" prefix is acceptable for this demonstration extension (if publishing to AppSource in the future, a registered 3-character affix will be required)
  • Standard Business Central data classification and GDPR compliance requirements apply to the CustomerContent classification
  • The optional Message popup behavior (showing "Hello %1. The note has been updated.") will NOT be included in the initial version to avoid intrusive popups during high-volume data entry (can be added as an optional demo variant if requested)
  • Network connectivity and database access are stable and follow standard Business Central performance characteristics
  • The extension does not require translation files (XLIFF), custom permission sets, or data migration scripts for the initial v1.0 release
  • Standard Business Central backup and recovery procedures will protect the custom field data
  • The minimum 3-character validation rule is a business requirement for demonstration purposes and may be adjusted based on actual business needs in future iterations
plan.md294 lines

Implementation Plan: Business Central Hello World Extension

Branch: feat/rebate-setup-seeding-layer | Date: 2026-06-13 | Spec: spec.md

Input: Feature specification from specs/001-bc-hello-world-extension/spec.md

Summary

This implementation plan defines the approach for building a minimal Business Central AL extension that adds a custom "Hello World Note" field to the Customer table with minimum length validation. The extension demonstrates BC extensibility using tableextension and pageextension objects within the allocated object ID range (50000-50249), targeting Business Central Runtime 16.0 and Application 27.0.0.0.

Technical Approach:

  • Extend Customer table (Table 18) with tableextension 50100
  • Add Text[100] field with OnValidate trigger for 3-character minimum validation
  • Extend Customer Card page (Page 21) with pageextension 50110
  • Display field in General FastTab using addlast() layout pattern
  • Package as .app file for deployment to sandbox/UAT environment

Technical Context

Language/Version: AL (Application Language) for Business Central, Runtime 16.0

Primary Dependencies:

  • Business Central platform 27.0.0.0 (2025 Wave 2)
  • Base Application (provides Customer table and Customer Card page)
  • No external AL dependencies required

Storage: SQL Server database (managed by Business Central platform)

Testing: Manual UAT testing using 15 predefined test scenarios (see spec.md and quickstart.md)

Target Platform: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central (cloud SaaS or on-premises)

Project Type: Business Central AL extension (.app package)

Performance Goals:

  • Customer Card page load time: No degradation from baseline (field adds negligible overhead)
  • Validation response: Immediate (< 100ms for string length check)
  • Record save: < 2 seconds with valid data

Constraints:

  • Object IDs must be within 50000-50249 range (enforced by app.json)
  • No modification of base Microsoft objects (extension-only approach)
  • NoImplicitWith feature enabled (requires explicit Rec. prefix)
  • Maximum field length: 100 characters (Text[100])
  • Minimum validation length: 3 characters when non-blank

Scale/Scope:

  • Single extension with 2 AL objects (1 tableextension, 1 pageextension)
  • Single custom field
  • Minimal complexity (demonstration/learning purpose)
  • Target: Sandbox/UAT environment initially, production-ready after UAT sign-off

Constitution Check

GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.

Status: No constitution defined for this project

The constitution file (.specify/memory/constitution.md) exists but contains only placeholder content. No project-specific principles, constraints, or gates are defined.

Assumed Principles (standard AL development best practices):

  • ✅ Extension-Only Development: No direct modification of base objects (satisfied by using tableextension/pageextension)
  • ✅ Object ID Compliance: Use only allocated ID ranges (satisfied by 50100/50110 within 50000-50249)
  • ✅ Data Classification: Proper GDPR compliance (satisfied by CustomerContent classification)
  • ✅ Backward Compatibility: Changes must not break existing functionality (satisfied by additive extension)

Gate Status: ✅ PASSED (no violations of standard AL practices)

Re-check After Phase 1: No constitution-based gates to re-evaluate

Project Structure

Documentation (this feature)

specs/001-bc-hello-world-extension/
├── spec.md                      # Feature specification (user requirements)
├── plan.md                      # This file - implementation plan
├── research.md                  # Phase 0 - technical research and decisions
├── data-model.md                # Phase 1 - field schema and validation rules
├── quickstart.md                # Phase 1 - build/deploy/test guide
├── contracts/
│   └── extension-objects.md     # Phase 1 - AL object contracts and API exposure
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md          # Specification quality checklist
└── tasks.md                     # Phase 2 - NOT YET CREATED (use /speckit-tasks)

Source Code (repository root)

workspace/
├── app.json                     # Extension metadata (pre-configured, DO NOT MODIFY)
├── .alpackages/                 # Symbol packages (empty - base objects built-in)
└── src/                         # AL source files (TO BE CREATED during implementation)
    ├── PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al       # tableextension 50100
    └── PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al    # pageextension 50110

Structure Decision:

This is a single AL extension project following the standard Business Central extension structure:

  • app.json: Pre-configured extension manifest (id, name, publisher, version, runtime, application, idRanges)
  • src/: Source code directory containing all AL object files
  • AL files follow naming convention: [ObjectName].[ObjectType].al
  • Example: PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al for table extension
  • .alpackages/: Symbol packages directory (currently empty as base BC objects are built-in)
  • specs/: Design documentation (outside src/, not compiled)

No test directory created because:

  • This is a minimal demonstration extension
  • Testing approach is manual UAT (not automated AL test codeunits)
  • Test scenarios documented in spec.md and quickstart.md

No frontend/backend split because:

  • This is a Business Central extension (not a web app)
  • UI is defined declaratively in AL pageextension
  • No separate frontend framework (React/Vue/etc.) involved

The structure aligns with standard Microsoft AL extension templates and VS Code AL project conventions.

Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations requiring justification.

No complexity concerns for this minimal extension. The implementation is intentionally simple:

  • 2 AL objects (minimal possible for adding a field with UI)
  • No custom logic beyond simple validation
  • No additional dependencies
  • No performance or scalability concerns

Phase 0: Research (Completed)

All technical unknowns resolved in research.md:

✅ AL language version and runtime compatibility (16.0, Application 27.0.0.0) ✅ Object ID allocation strategy (50100 tableextension, 50110 pageextension) ✅ Base objects to extend (Customer table, Customer Card page) ✅ Field type and validation pattern (Text[100], OnValidate trigger) ✅ Data classification requirements (CustomerContent) ✅ Page layout strategy (addlast(General) placement) ✅ Testing approach (manual UAT, no automated tests) ✅ Packaging and deployment process (.app file via AL compiler) ✅ AL coding standards (NoImplicitWith, quoted field names, explicit Rec. prefix)

No blocking issues identified. All requirements are implementable within BC extension framework.

Phase 1: Design & Contracts (Completed)

Data Model

Defined in data-model.md:

Extended Entity: Customer (Table 18)

New Field:

  • Field Number: 50100
  • Name: "PTE Hello World Note"
  • Type: Text[100]
  • Required: No (optional, blank allowed)
  • Validation: Minimum 3 characters when non-blank
  • Classification: CustomerContent

Validation Logic:

IF blank THEN allow
ELSE IF length < 3 THEN error
ELSE allow

State Transitions: Simple (blank ↔ valid value, invalid values blocked)

Edge Cases: Documented for 100-char max, whitespace, Unicode, concurrent edits, API access

Contracts

Defined in contracts/extension-objects.md:

AL Objects:

  1. tableextension 50100 "PTE Customer Ext"
  • Extends: Customer (Table 18)
  • Adds: field 50100 "PTE Hello World Note"
  • Trigger: OnValidate with length check
  1. pageextension 50110 "PTE Customer Card Ext"
  • Extends: "Customer Card" (Page 21)
  • Layout: addlast(General) with field control
  • Properties: ApplicationArea = All, Caption, ToolTip

API Exposure: Field automatically available via:

  • OData API (v2.0/customers endpoint)
  • SOAP web services
  • Excel integration
  • RapidStart configuration packages

Backward Compatibility:

  • Field number, name, type are stable
  • Optional field guaranteed to remain optional
  • Breaking changes require major version bump

Quickstart Validation

Defined in quickstart.md:

Build Process:

  1. Create AL source files in src/
  2. Compile with Ctrl+Shift+B (VS Code)
  3. Verify .app file generated

Deployment:

  1. Publish via VS Code AL extension OR
  2. Upload via Extension Management page

Validation Scenarios (12 tests):

  • Field visibility on Customer Card
  • Valid input (3+ chars, blank)
  • Invalid input (1-2 chars) → error
  • Boundary case (exactly 3 chars)
  • Maximum length (100 chars)
  • Data persistence
  • New customer creation
  • No regression on standard fields

UAT Checklist: 12 test scenarios with Pass/Fail tracking

Estimated Time: ~45 minutes (15 min build/deploy, 30 min testing)

Phase 2: Task Breakdown (Next Step)

Not created in this plan. Use /speckit-tasks command to generate tasks.md with:

  • Task decomposition (create table extension, create page extension, compile, test)
  • Dependencies (table extension → page extension → compile → deploy → test)
  • Acceptance criteria per task
  • Time estimates

Implementation Readiness

✅ Research Complete: All technical decisions documented ✅ Data Model Defined: Field schema and validation rules clear ✅ Contracts Defined: AL objects and API exposure documented ✅ Validation Guide Ready: Quickstart with 12 test scenarios ✅ Constitution Gates Passed: No violations (no gates defined)

Status: READY FOR TASK GENERATION (/speckit-tasks)

Remaining Work:

  1. Generate tasks.md (/speckit-tasks command)
  2. Create AL source files (PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al, PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al)
  3. Compile extension
  4. Deploy to sandbox
  5. Execute UAT validation (12 scenarios)
  6. UAT sign-off
  7. Deploy to production

Estimated Implementation Time:

  • AL coding: 30 minutes (2 simple objects)
  • Build/package: 5 minutes
  • Deploy to sandbox: 10 minutes
  • UAT testing: 30 minutes
  • Total: ~1.5 hours (excluding production deployment approval process)

References

  • Specification: spec.md - User requirements and acceptance criteria
  • Research: research.md - Technical decisions and alternatives
  • Data Model: data-model.md - Field schema and validation
  • Contracts: contracts/extension-objects.md - AL object specifications
  • Quickstart: quickstart.md - Build/deploy/test guide
  • app.json: Extension metadata (pre-configured, do not modify)

Notes

Key Constraints Reminder:

  • DO NOT modify app.json (id, name, publisher, version, idRanges are locked)
  • DO NOT modify .alpackages/ (symbol packages pre-configured)
  • DO create all AL source files in src/ directory
  • DO use object IDs within 50000-50249 range only
  • DO use tableextension/pageextension (not direct table/page modification)
  • DO use explicit Rec. prefix for all field references (NoImplicitWith feature enabled)

Critical Path:

  1. Create table extension (adds field and validation) ← FOUNDATION
  2. Create page extension (displays field on UI) ← DEPENDS ON #1
  3. Compile (validates AL syntax and references) ← DEPENDS ON #1,#2
  4. Deploy (installs in BC environment) ← DEPENDS ON #3
  5. Test (validates requirements) ← DEPENDS ON #4

Success Metrics:

  • Clean compilation (zero errors, zero warnings)
  • Successful deployment to sandbox
  • All 12 UAT scenarios pass
  • No regression in standard Customer functionality
  • UAT sign-off obtained from business users
research.md232 lines

Research: Business Central Hello World Extension

Feature: Business Central Hello World Extension

Date: 2026-06-13

Purpose: Resolve technical unknowns and establish implementation patterns for AL extension development

Research Findings

1. AL Language and Runtime Version

Decision: Use AL language with Business Central Runtime 16.0 and Application 27.0.0.0

Rationale:

  • The app.json specifies "application": "27.0.0.0" and "runtime": "16.0"
  • Runtime 16.0 corresponds to Business Central 2025 Wave 2 (version 27)
  • This runtime supports all required features: tableextension, pageextension, OnValidate triggers
  • The NoImplicitWith feature flag is enabled, requiring explicit Rec. prefix for field references

Alternatives considered:

  • Using older runtime versions: Rejected because app.json is pre-configured with 16.0
  • Upgrading to newer runtime: Not applicable as app.json is locked

Implementation impact:

  • All field references in triggers must use explicit Rec. prefix (e.g., Rec."PTE Hello World Note")
  • No implicit WITH statements allowed in code

2. Object ID Range

Decision: Use object IDs 50100 (tableextension) and 50110 (pageextension)

Rationale:

  • The app.json specifies "idRanges": [{"from": 50000, "to": 50249}]
  • Object IDs must fall within this allocated range
  • The technical requirements specify 50100 for table extension and 50110 for page extension
  • Both IDs are within the allowed range and follow sequential numbering convention

Alternatives considered:

  • Using different IDs within the range: Rejected to maintain consistency with technical requirements
  • Using IDs outside the range: Not allowed by AL compiler

Implementation impact:

  • tableextension 50100 "PTE Customer Ext"
  • pageextension 50110 "PTE Customer Card Ext"

3. Base Objects to Extend

Decision: Extend "Customer" table and "Customer Card" page

Rationale:

  • Customer table (Table 18) is a standard Business Central base object
  • Customer Card page (Page 21) is the standard UI for customer management
  • Both objects are guaranteed to exist in all Business Central environments
  • No symbol packages are loaded in .alpackages/, but these are fundamental BC objects

Alternatives considered:

  • Extending different customer-related objects: Rejected as spec requires Customer table specifically
  • Creating new standalone table: Rejected as requirement is to demonstrate extension, not new object creation

Implementation impact:

  • tableextension 50100 "PTE Customer Ext" extends Customer
  • pageextension 50110 "PTE Customer Card Ext" extends "Customer Card"

4. Field Type and Validation Pattern

Decision: Use Text[100] field type with OnValidate trigger for length validation

Rationale:

  • Text[100] provides 100-character capacity as specified in requirements
  • OnValidate trigger is the standard AL pattern for field-level validation
  • Validation fires on field blur and before record save
  • Error() function blocks save and displays message to user
  • Empty string check allows blank values while enforcing minimum length for non-blank values

Alternatives considered:

  • Using Code[100]: Rejected as Code fields are typically uppercase and used for identifiers
  • Using validation in page OnBeforeSave: Rejected as validation belongs in table layer for API/integration consistency
  • Using table field property MinValue: Not applicable to Text fields (numeric fields only)

Implementation impact:

trigger OnValidate()
begin
    if Rec."PTE Hello World Note" = '' then
        exit;
    
    if StrLen(Rec."PTE Hello World Note") < 3 then
        Error('Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters');
end;

5. Data Classification

Decision: Use DataClassification = CustomerContent

Rationale:

  • CustomerContent is appropriate for user-entered business data about customers
  • Complies with GDPR and Business Central data classification requirements
  • Ensures proper data handling in terms of privacy and regulatory compliance
  • Matches the classification used for other customer-specific fields in BC

Alternatives considered:

  • SystemMetadata: Rejected as this field contains business content, not system metadata
  • ToBeClassified: Rejected as the classification is known and should be explicit
  • OrganizationIdentifiableInformation: Over-classification for demo notes

Implementation impact:

  • Field property: DataClassification = CustomerContent;

6. Page Layout Strategy

Decision: Use addlast(General) to append field to General FastTab

Rationale:

  • addlast() is non-intrusive and maintains existing field order
  • General FastTab is the primary data entry section on Customer Card
  • Placement at end ensures no disruption to user workflow
  • Users can easily find the new field without hunting through multiple tabs

Alternatives considered:

  • addfirst(General): Rejected as placing custom fields first disrupts standard BC field order
  • Creating new FastTab: Over-engineering for a single demo field
  • Adding to different FastTab: General is the most logical location for general notes

Implementation impact:

layout
{
    addlast(General)
    {
        field("PTEHelloWorldNote"; Rec."PTE Hello World Note")
        {
            ApplicationArea = All;
            Caption = 'Hello World Note';
            ToolTip = 'Specifies a short demo note used to demonstrate Business Central extensibility.';
        }
    }
}

7. Testing Strategy

Decision: Manual UAT testing in sandbox environment (no automated AL tests)

Rationale:

  • The spec defines 15 UAT test scenarios with detailed acceptance criteria
  • For a minimal demo extension, manual testing is appropriate
  • AL test framework (Test Codeunits) would require additional objects and complexity
  • Focus is on demonstrating extension capability, not test automation infrastructure

Alternatives considered:

  • Creating Test Codeunit with automated tests: Deferred to future enhancement
  • Using Business Central test toolkit: Out of scope for minimal demo
  • API-based testing: Not applicable as extension has no API exposure

Implementation impact:

  • No test codeunits in this implementation
  • Testing will follow the 15 UAT scenarios from technical requirements
  • Validation tested manually by entering invalid/valid data

8. Packaging and Deployment

Decision: Package as .app file using AL compiler

Rationale:

  • AL compiler automatically packages extension as .app file when building
  • .app format is the standard Business Central extension package format
  • Deployment will be via Extension Management page in BC
  • app.json already configured with correct metadata (id, name, publisher, version)

Alternatives considered:

  • Manual packaging: Not applicable, AL tooling handles this automatically
  • Different package format: .app is the only supported format for BC extensions

Implementation impact:

  • Build command: Ctrl+Shift+B in VS Code or alc.exe compiler
  • Output: .app file in project directory
  • Deployment: Upload via Extension Management in BC sandbox/UAT

9. AL Language Best Practices

Decision: Follow Microsoft AL coding standards

Rationale:

  • Explicit field references with Rec. prefix (required by NoImplicitWith feature)
  • Quoted identifiers for field names with spaces (e.g., "PTE Hello World Note")
  • Proper object naming: "PTE Customer Ext" follows [Prefix] [Object] [Type Suffix] pattern
  • Caption and ToolTip properties for all user-facing elements
  • ApplicationArea = All ensures field visibility in all application areas

Alternatives considered:

  • Alternative naming conventions: Rejected to align with BC community standards
  • Omitting captions/tooltips: Rejected as poor UX and fails BC best practices

Implementation impact:

  • Clean, readable code following Microsoft standards
  • All required metadata properties included
  • Proper use of AL syntax features

Summary

All technical unknowns have been resolved. The implementation will use:

  • Platform: Business Central Runtime 16.0, Application 27.0.0.0
  • Language: AL with NoImplicitWith feature
  • Objects: tableextension 50100, pageextension 50110
  • Field: Text[100] with OnValidate trigger for 3-character minimum validation
  • Layout: addlast(General) placement on Customer Card
  • Classification: CustomerContent
  • Testing: Manual UAT using 15 defined scenarios
  • Packaging: Standard .app file via AL compiler

No blocking technical issues identified. All requirements are implementable within Business Central extension framework constraints.

data-model.md178 lines

Data Model: Business Central Hello World Extension

Feature: Business Central Hello World Extension

Date: 2026-06-13

Purpose: Define the data structure and validation rules for the custom field extension

Overview

This extension adds one custom field to the existing Customer table in Business Central. The data model is intentionally minimal, demonstrating the extension pattern without introducing new entities or complex relationships.

Extended Entities

Customer (Table 18) - Extended

Base Entity: Microsoft Business Central standard Customer table

Extension Name: "PTE Customer Ext" (tableextension 50100)

New Fields:

Field No.Field NameTypeLengthRequiredDescription
50100PTE Hello World NoteText100NoDemo note field for demonstrating Business Central extensibility

Field Properties:

  • Caption: "Hello World Note"
  • DataClassification: CustomerContent
  • ToolTip: "Specifies a short demo note used to demonstrate Business Central extensibility."
  • ApplicationArea: All

Validation Rules:

  1. Optional Field: Blank/empty values are allowed
  2. Minimum Length When Non-Blank: If the field contains any value (non-blank), it MUST be at least 3 characters long
  3. Maximum Length: 100 characters (enforced by field type Text[100])
  4. Error Message: "Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters" (displayed when validation fails)

Validation Logic:

IF field is blank THEN
    Allow (exit validation - no error)
ELSE IF field length < 3 characters THEN
    Block and display error: "Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters"
ELSE
    Allow (validation passes)
END

Field Behavior:

  • Create: Field is available and functional when creating new customer records
  • Read: Field value is retrieved and displayed when viewing customer records
  • Update: Field value can be modified on existing customer records with validation applied
  • Delete: Field value is removed when customer record is deleted (standard cascade)

Trigger Events:

  • OnValidate: Fires when user moves off the field or saves the record
  • Validates minimum 3-character length for non-blank values
  • Displays error and blocks save if validation fails

Relationships

No new relationships introduced. The custom field is a simple attribute of the existing Customer entity with no foreign keys or references to other tables.

Data Flow

User Input (Customer Card UI)
    ↓
Field OnValidate Trigger (validation logic)
    ↓
[If valid] → Customer Table Field (persisted to database)
    ↓
Database Storage (SQL Server)
    ↓
Retrieval → Display on Customer Card

State Transitions

The field has no complex state machine. Valid transitions:

  1. Blank → Blank: No change (valid)
  2. Blank → Valid (3+ chars): Create/update value (valid)
  3. Blank → Invalid (1-2 chars): Blocked by validation (error displayed)
  4. Valid → Blank: Clear value (valid)
  5. Valid → Valid (different): Update value (valid)
  6. Valid → Invalid (1-2 chars): Blocked by validation (error displayed)
  7. Invalid → [any state]: Not persisted, so only correctable to valid state or blank

Data Constraints

ConstraintTypeDescriptionEnforcement
Length (max)HardMaximum 100 charactersField type Text[100] - enforced by AL compiler and database
Length (min)SoftMinimum 3 characters when non-blankOnValidate trigger - enforced at runtime
RequiredNoneField is optionalNo NOT NULL constraint
FormatNoneFree text, no format restrictionsNo validation on character types
UniquenessNoneValues can be duplicated across customersNo UNIQUE constraint
Default ValueNoneBlank/empty stringNo default assignment

Character Set and Encoding

  • Supported Characters: All Unicode characters supported by Business Central Text fields
  • Special Characters: Allowed (spaces, punctuation, symbols)
  • Unicode/Emoji: Allowed (no restrictions)
  • Case Sensitivity: Case-preserving (stored as entered, no case transformation)

Data Migration

Not applicable for v1.0. This extension does not include:

  • Data migration scripts
  • Initial value population
  • Backfill logic for existing records

Existing customer records will have blank values in the new field until manually populated by users.

Data Privacy and Compliance

Data Classification: CustomerContent

Implications:

  • Field contains customer-related business data
  • Subject to GDPR and data privacy regulations
  • Included in data export/deletion requests for customer data
  • Follows standard Business Central data retention policies
  • Protected by Business Central's standard security and permission model

Permissions: Uses standard Customer table permissions. No additional permission sets required.

Database Schema Impact

Table: Customer (Table 18)

Physical Change: One new column added to the Customer table in SQL Server

Index Impact: None. The field is not indexed.

Storage Impact: Minimal

  • Text[100] field: approximately 100-200 bytes per record (variable based on content)
  • For 10,000 customer records: ~1-2 MB additional storage

Performance Impact: Negligible

  • No additional indexes or constraints
  • No joins or lookups introduced
  • Validation logic is simple string length check

Edge Cases and Handling

Edge CaseBehavior
Exactly 3 charactersValid (boundary case - passes validation)
Exactly 100 charactersValid (maximum length - accepted)
101+ charactersPrevented by field type (AL compiler enforces Text[100])
Empty string vs NULLTreated identically as "blank" (validation allows both)
Whitespace-only (e.g., " ")Treated as valid 3+ character value (no trim validation)
Special characters/emojiAllowed (counted in length validation)
Concurrent editStandard Business Central record locking applies (last write wins)
API modificationValidation applies (OnValidate trigger fires for API/web service updates)
Data import (Excel/RapidStart)Validation applies (invalid rows will fail import with error)
Direct SQL updateBypasses validation (not recommended, breaks business rules)

Summary

The data model extends the Customer table with a single, optional text field:

  • Simple: One field, no relationships, no complex state
  • Validated: Minimum 3-character length when non-blank
  • Compliant: CustomerContent classification for GDPR compliance
  • Non-intrusive: No changes to existing fields or table structure
  • Backward compatible: Existing records unaffected (blank values allowed)

This minimal data model aligns with the feature's purpose: demonstrating Business Central extensibility with the simplest possible extension.

quickstart.md335 lines

Quickstart: Business Central Hello World Extension

Feature: Business Central Hello World Extension

Date: 2026-06-13

Purpose: Step-by-step guide to build, deploy, and validate the extension

Prerequisites

Before starting, ensure you have:

  1. Development Environment:
  • Visual Studio Code installed
  • AL Language extension for VS Code installed
  • Business Central development environment configured
  1. Business Central Access:
  • Access to a Business Central Sandbox or UAT environment
  • Permissions to publish extensions
  • Permissions to create/edit customer records
  1. Project Files:
  • app.json configured (already present in project)
  • Object ID range 50000-50249 allocated (confirmed in app.json)

Build and Compile

Step 1: Create Source Files

Create the following files in the src/ directory:

  1. src/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al - Table extension
  2. src/PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al - Page extension

(See contracts/extension-objects.md for the complete AL code structure)

Step 2: Compile the Extension

Using VS Code:

  1. Open the project folder in VS Code
  2. Press Ctrl+Shift+B (or Cmd+Shift+B on Mac)
  3. Select "AL: Build" from the menu

Expected Output:

Building...
Compiling...
Compilation completed successfully.
Extension packaged: bsbench-01-pte-hello-world-github-speckit-claude_1.0.0.4.app

Validation:

  • ✅ No compilation errors
  • ✅ No warnings
  • ✅ .app file created in project directory

Troubleshooting:

  • Error: "Object ID out of range" → Verify object IDs (50100, 50110) are within app.json idRanges
  • Error: "NoImplicitWith feature requires explicit record reference" → Use Rec. prefix for all field references
  • Error: "Field name must be quoted" → Use quotes around field names with spaces: "PTE Hello World Note"

Deploy to Sandbox/UAT

Step 3: Publish Extension

Method 1: Using VS Code:

  1. Press Ctrl+Shift+P (Command Palette)
  2. Select "AL: Publish"
  3. Select your configured Business Central server
  4. Enter credentials if prompted

Method 2: Using Extension Management Page:

  1. Open Business Central web client
  2. Search for "Extension Management"
  3. Click "Upload Extension"
  4. Browse to .app file
  5. Click "Upload"
  6. Click "Deploy" when prompted

Expected Output:

Extension published successfully.
Extension installed on: [Sandbox Environment Name]

Validation:

  • ✅ Extension appears in Extension Management page
  • ✅ Status shows "Installed"
  • ✅ No installation errors in error log

Troubleshooting:

  • Error: "Extension validation failed" → Check app.json metadata (id, name, publisher)
  • Error: "Object ID conflict" → Another extension is using IDs 50100 or 50110
  • Error: "Platform version mismatch" → Verify runtime and application versions match environment

Validate Functionality

Step 4: Test Custom Field Visibility

Test Scenario: Verify field appears on Customer Card

  1. Open Business Central web client
  2. Search for "Customers"
  3. Open any existing customer (e.g., customer #10000)
  4. Scroll to the General section

Expected Result:

  • ✅ "Hello World Note" field is visible at the end of the General FastTab
  • ✅ Field is editable (not read-only)
  • ✅ Field shows tooltip on hover: "Specifies a short demo note used to demonstrate Business Central extensibility."

Screenshot Location: General FastTab, after standard customer fields

Step 5: Test Valid Input

Test Scenario: Save customer with valid note value

  1. With a customer card open, click into "Hello World Note" field
  2. Enter: Test note
  3. Press Tab or click outside the field
  4. Click "Save" or press Ctrl+S

Expected Result:

  • ✅ No error message displayed
  • ✅ Record saves successfully
  • ✅ Value "Test note" remains in the field

Step 6: Test Blank Value

Test Scenario: Verify blank values are allowed

  1. With a customer card open, clear the "Hello World Note" field (delete all text)
  2. Press Tab or click outside the field
  3. Click "Save"

Expected Result:

  • ✅ No error message
  • ✅ Record saves successfully with blank note field

Step 7: Test Validation (Invalid Input)

Test Scenario: Verify minimum length validation

  1. With a customer card open, click into "Hello World Note" field
  2. Enter: Hi (exactly 2 characters)
  3. Press Tab or click outside the field

Expected Result:

  • ✅ Error message appears: "Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters"
  • ✅ Field value is rejected (reverts to previous value or stays in error state)
  • ✅ Cannot save record until corrected

Step 8: Test Validation (Boundary - Exactly 3 Characters)

Test Scenario: Verify 3 characters is accepted (minimum boundary)

  1. With a customer card open, enter: ABC (exactly 3 characters)
  2. Press Tab and save

Expected Result:

  • ✅ No error message
  • ✅ Value "ABC" is accepted and saved

Step 9: Test Validation (Maximum Length)

Test Scenario: Verify 100-character limit

  1. With a customer card open, enter or paste a 100-character string:
   This is a test note with exactly one hundred characters to verify the maximum field length limit works.

(Count: 100 characters including spaces and punctuation)

  1. Save the record

Expected Result:

  • ✅ Value is accepted (100 chars is within limit)
  • ✅ Record saves successfully

Optional Test: Try entering 101+ characters

  • ✅ Field should truncate or prevent entry beyond 100 characters

Step 10: Test Data Persistence

Test Scenario: Verify saved data persists

  1. With a customer card containing a saved note, close the customer card
  2. Re-open the same customer (search by number or name)
  3. Check the "Hello World Note" field

Expected Result:

  • ✅ Previously saved note value is still present
  • ✅ No data loss occurred

Step 11: Test New Customer Creation

Test Scenario: Verify field works for new customers

  1. Click "New" to create a new customer
  2. Fill in required fields (at minimum: Name)
  3. Enter a valid note in "Hello World Note" field: New customer note
  4. Save the new customer

Expected Result:

  • ✅ New customer is created successfully
  • ✅ Note field value is saved with the new record
  • ✅ Re-opening the customer shows the saved note

Step 12: Test No Regression

Test Scenario: Verify standard customer functionality is unaffected

  1. Open a customer card
  2. Edit standard fields: Name, Address, Phone No., Email
  3. Save the customer

Expected Result:

  • ✅ All standard fields work normally
  • ✅ No errors related to standard functionality
  • ✅ Customer Card loads at normal speed (no performance degradation)

UAT Sign-Off Checklist

Use this checklist for User Acceptance Testing:

Test IDScenarioStatusNotes
TS-01Extension installs without errors☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-02Field visible on Customer Card☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-03Blank value allowed☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-04Valid value (3+ chars) accepted☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-05Invalid value (1-2 chars) blocked☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-06Exactly 3 characters accepted☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-07Data persists after close/reopen☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-08Field works for new customers☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-09Standard fields unaffected☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-10Page loads normally with blank values☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-11100-character maximum enforced☐ Pass ☐ Fail
TS-12No compilation warnings/errors☐ Pass ☐ Fail

UAT Exit Criteria: All critical tests (TS-01 through TS-09) must pass before production deployment.

API/Integration Testing (Optional)

Step 13: Test OData API Access

Test Scenario: Verify field accessible via API

Prerequisites: OData web service for Customer page enabled

cURL Command:

curl -X GET "https://{bc-server}/api/v2.0/companies({companyId})/customers({customerId})" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {token}"

Expected Response (partial JSON):

{
  "id": "...",
  "number": "10000",
  "displayName": "Adatum Corporation",
  "pteHelloWorldNote": "Test note",
  ...
}

Validation:

  • ✅ Field "pteHelloWorldNote" present in JSON response
  • ✅ Value matches what's displayed in UI

Uninstall (Cleanup)

To remove the extension after testing:

  1. Open Business Central web client
  2. Search for "Extension Management"
  3. Find "bsbench-01-pte-hello-world-github-speckit-claude"
  4. Click "Uninstall"
  5. Confirm uninstallation

Warning: Uninstalling will remove the custom field and delete all data stored in it. Back up data if needed before uninstalling.

Summary

This quickstart guide covers:

✅ Compiling the extension from AL source files ✅ Publishing to Business Central sandbox environment ✅ Validating the 12 core test scenarios ✅ Completing UAT sign-off checklist ✅ Optional API integration testing

Success Criteria:

  • Extension compiles without errors
  • All 12 test scenarios pass
  • UAT checklist shows "Pass" for all critical items

Next Steps:

  • Review contracts/extension-objects.md for detailed object specifications
  • Review data-model.md for field schema and validation details
  • Proceed to production deployment after UAT sign-off

Estimated Time:

  • Build and deploy: 15 minutes
  • Testing (Steps 4-12): 30 minutes
  • Total: ~45 minutes for complete validation
tasks.md410 lines

description: "Task list for Business Central Hello World Extension implementation" ---

Tasks: Business Central Hello World Extension

Input: Design documents from specs/001-bc-hello-world-extension/

Prerequisites: plan.md, spec.md, research.md, data-model.md, contracts/extension-objects.md, quickstart.md

Tests: No automated tests - manual UAT testing using scenarios from spec.md and quickstart.md

Organization: Tasks are grouped by user story to enable independent implementation and testing of each story.

Format: [ID] [P?] [Story] Description

  • [P]: Can run in parallel (different files, no dependencies)
  • [Story]: Which user story this task belongs to (e.g., US1, US2, US3)
  • Include exact file paths in descriptions

Path Conventions

  • AL Extension Project: src/ at repository root for all AL source files
  • AL file naming: [ObjectName].[ObjectType].al
  • Extension metadata: app.json (pre-configured, DO NOT MODIFY)

Phase 1: Setup (Project Structure)

Purpose: Verify project structure and configuration

  • [X] T001 Verify app.json configuration (id, name, publisher, version, runtime 16.0, application 27.0.0.0, idRanges 50000-50249)
  • [X] T002 Create src/ directory if it doesn't exist
  • [X] T003 Verify .alpackages/ directory exists (symbol packages location)

Phase 2: Foundational (Not Applicable)

Purpose: Core infrastructure that MUST be complete before ANY user story can be implemented

Status: ✅ NO FOUNDATIONAL TASKS NEEDED

This AL extension has no foundational blocking prerequisites. The Business Central platform, Customer table (Table 18), and Customer Card page (Page 21) already exist. User story implementation can begin immediately after Phase 1 setup.

Phase 3: User Story 1 - Add Custom Note Field (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

Goal: Extend Customer table with "PTE Hello World Note" field and display it on Customer Card

Independent Test:

  1. Open Business Central web client
  2. Navigate to Customers and open any customer card
  3. Verify "Hello World Note" field is visible in General FastTab
  4. Enter a valid note (3+ characters or blank) and save
  5. Close and reopen customer card - verify value persists

Why MVP: This delivers the core extensibility demonstration - a custom field visible and functional on the Customer Card. Without this, the extension has no purpose.

Implementation for User Story 1

  • [X] T004 [P] [US1] Create table extension in src/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al
  • Object: tableextension 50100 "PTE Customer Ext" extends Customer
  • Add field 50100 "PTE Hello World Note" Text[100]
  • Set Caption = 'Hello World Note'
  • Set DataClassification = CustomerContent
  • Add OnValidate trigger with validation logic (see research.md)
  • [X] T005 [P] [US1] Create page extension in src/PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al
  • Object: pageextension 50110 "PTE Customer Card Ext" extends "Customer Card"
  • Add field control in addlast(General) layout
  • Field source: Rec."PTE Hello World Note"
  • Set ApplicationArea = All
  • Set Caption = 'Hello World Note'
  • Set ToolTip = 'Specifies a short demo note used to demonstrate Business Central extensibility.'
  • [ ] T006 [US1] Compile AL extension using Ctrl+Shift+B in VS Code
  • Verify zero compilation errors
  • Verify zero warnings
  • Verify .app file generated: bsbench-01-pte-hello-world-github-speckit-claude_1.0.0.4.app
  • [ ] T007 [US1] Deploy extension to sandbox/UAT environment
  • Method 1: VS Code AL: Publish command OR
  • Method 2: Upload via Extension Management page in BC web client
  • Verify extension appears as "Installed" status
  • [ ] T008 [US1] Execute UAT Test TS-02: Verify field visible on Customer Card
  • Open existing customer card
  • Verify "Hello World Note" field visible in General FastTab
  • Verify field is editable
  • Verify tooltip displays on hover
  • [ ] T009 [US1] Execute UAT Test TS-04: Verify valid value accepted
  • Enter "Test note" (9 characters) in field
  • Save customer record
  • Verify no errors displayed
  • Verify value remains in field after save
  • [ ] T010 [US1] Execute UAT Test TS-03: Verify blank value allowed
  • Clear "Hello World Note" field completely
  • Save customer record
  • Verify no errors displayed
  • Verify record saves successfully
  • [ ] T011 [US1] Execute UAT Test TS-07: Verify data persistence
  • With customer containing saved note, close customer card
  • Reopen same customer by number/name
  • Verify "Hello World Note" value still present and correct

Checkpoint: At this point, User Story 1 (MVP) is complete. The custom field is visible, functional, and persists data. The extension can be demonstrated or deployed as-is.

Phase 4: User Story 2 - Validate Minimum Length (Priority: P2)

Goal: Add validation to enforce minimum 3-character length for non-blank values

Independent Test:

  1. Open customer card
  2. Enter "Hi" (2 characters) in "Hello World Note" field
  3. Press Tab to move to next field
  4. Verify error message: "Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters"
  5. Verify value is rejected and cannot save

Why Priority 2: Demonstrates AL validation capabilities and enforces data quality. The field already functions (P1), but this adds business rule enforcement.

Note: Validation logic should already be implemented in T004 (table extension OnValidate trigger). This phase focuses on testing the validation behavior.

Validation Testing for User Story 2

  • [ ] T012 [US2] Execute UAT Test TS-05: Verify invalid value blocked
  • Open customer card
  • Enter "Hi" (exactly 2 characters) in field
  • Move to another field or attempt to save
  • Verify error message displayed: "Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters"
  • Verify value is not saved
  • [ ] T013 [US2] Execute UAT Test TS-06: Verify boundary case (exactly 3 characters)
  • Open customer card
  • Enter "ABC" (exactly 3 characters) in field
  • Save record
  • Verify no error displayed
  • Verify value "ABC" is saved successfully
  • [ ] T014 [US2] Execute UAT Test: Verify error recovery
  • After receiving validation error for 2-character value
  • Option A: Correct value to 3+ characters and verify save succeeds
  • Option B: Clear field to blank and verify save succeeds
  • [ ] T015 [US2] Execute UAT Test TS-12: Verify maximum length (100 characters)
  • Enter or paste 100-character string in field
  • Verify value is accepted
  • Save record successfully
  • Optional: Try 101+ characters and verify field truncates or prevents entry

Checkpoint: At this point, User Story 2 is complete. Validation enforces data quality (3-char minimum) while still allowing blank values.

Phase 5: User Story 3 - Create New Customers with Custom Field (Priority: P3)

Goal: Verify field works correctly when creating new customer records (not just editing existing)

Independent Test:

  1. Click "New" to create new customer in Business Central
  2. Fill mandatory fields (Name, etc.)
  3. Enter valid note in "Hello World Note" field
  4. Save new customer
  5. Reopen and verify note was saved

Why Priority 3: Ensures complete customer lifecycle support. P1 and P2 already deliver core value for existing records.

Testing for User Story 3

  • [ ] T016 [US3] Execute UAT Test TS-08: Verify field works for new customer with valid note
  • Click "New" to create new customer
  • Fill mandatory customer fields (minimum: Name)
  • Enter "New customer note" (17 characters) in "Hello World Note"
  • Save new customer
  • Verify new customer created successfully
  • Reopen customer and verify note value persists
  • [ ] T017 [US3] Execute UAT Test TS-08 variant: Verify new customer with blank note
  • Create new customer with mandatory fields
  • Leave "Hello World Note" blank
  • Save new customer
  • Verify customer created successfully (blank is allowed)
  • [ ] T018 [US3] Execute UAT Test TS-08 variant: Verify validation on new customer
