  • Create new customer with mandatory fields
  • Enter "Hi" (2 characters) in "Hello World Note"
  • Attempt to save
  • Verify validation error displayed
  • Verify customer cannot be created until field corrected or cleared

Checkpoint: All user stories (P1, P2, P3) are now complete. The extension supports full customer lifecycle with validation.

Phase 6: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

Purpose: Complete remaining UAT tests and verify no regression

  • [ ] T019 [P] Execute UAT Test TS-09: Verify standard fields unaffected
  • Open customer card
  • Edit standard fields: Name, Address, Phone No., Email
  • Save customer
  • Verify all standard fields work normally
  • Verify no errors or performance degradation
  • [ ] T020 [P] Execute UAT Test TS-10: Verify page loads normally with unused field
  • Open multiple customer cards with blank "Hello World Note" values
  • Verify Customer Card opens without errors
  • Verify no performance impact or forced input
  • [ ] T021 Execute UAT Test TS-01: Verify installation without errors
  • Review Extension Management page
  • Verify extension status shows "Installed"
  • Verify no errors in error log
  • Verify Business Central continues to function normally
  • [ ] T022 Review and complete UAT sign-off checklist from quickstart.md
  • All critical tests (TS-01 through TS-09) marked Pass
  • Business user reviews and approves field behavior
  • Business user approves field placement and caption
  • No blocking defects remain
  • [ ] T023 [P] Execute edge case testing (optional but recommended)
  • Test exactly 100 characters (max length boundary)
  • Test special characters and Unicode in field
  • Test whitespace-only values (e.g., " " - 3 spaces)
  • Test emoji and international characters
  • Verify all edge cases behave as expected per data-model.md
  • [ ] T024 Verify compilation cleanliness
  • Re-run Ctrl+Shift+B in VS Code
  • Confirm zero errors
  • Confirm zero warnings
  • Verify app.json unchanged (id, name, publisher, version, idRanges)
  • [ ] T025 [P] Review extension against AL best practices checklist
  • All field references use explicit Rec. prefix (NoImplicitWith compliance)
  • Field names with spaces are quoted: "PTE Hello World Note"
  • All objects use "PTE" prefix for naming consistency
  • Object IDs within allocated range (50100, 50110 within 50000-50249)
  • DataClassification set appropriately (CustomerContent)
  • Caption and ToolTip provided for all user-facing elements
  • Only tableextension and pageextension used (no base object modification)

Dependencies & Execution Order

Phase Dependencies

  • Setup (Phase 1): No dependencies - can start immediately
  • Foundational (Phase 2): ✅ Not applicable (skipped - no foundational work needed)
  • User Story 1 (Phase 3): Depends on Setup completion - Creates core field and UI
  • User Story 2 (Phase 4): Depends on User Story 1 completion - Tests validation already in table extension
  • User Story 3 (Phase 5): Depends on User Story 1 completion - Tests new customer creation flow
  • Polish (Phase 6): Depends on all user stories completion - Final validation and sign-off

User Story Dependencies

  • User Story 1 (P1): Foundation - MUST complete first. Creates table extension and page extension.
  • User Story 2 (P2): Depends on US1 (needs field and validation to exist). Tests validation behavior.
  • User Story 3 (P3): Depends on US1 (needs field and UI to exist). Tests new record creation.

Critical Path: T001-T003 (Setup) → T004-T011 (US1 implementation and testing) → T012-T015 (US2 validation testing) → T016-T018 (US3 new customer testing) → T019-T025 (Polish)

Within Each User Story

User Story 1 (Implementation-focused):

  1. Create AL source files (T004, T005) - can be done in parallel
  2. Compile (T006) - depends on T004 and T005
  3. Deploy (T007) - depends on T006 (successful compilation)
  4. Test (T008-T011) - depends on T007 (deployed extension)

User Story 2 (Testing-focused):

  1. All validation tests (T012-T015) depend on US1 being complete
  2. Tests can be executed sequentially or in any order

User Story 3 (Testing-focused):

  1. All new customer tests (T016-T018) depend on US1 being complete
  2. Tests can be executed sequentially or in any order

Parallel Opportunities

  • Phase 1 Setup: All tasks (T001-T003) can run in parallel (verification tasks)
  • Phase 3 User Story 1: T004 and T005 can run in parallel (different AL files)
  • Phase 6 Polish: T019, T020, T023, T025 can run in parallel (independent verification tasks)

Limited Parallelization: This is a minimal 2-file AL extension. Most tasks are sequential due to compile → deploy → test dependencies.

Parallel Example: User Story 1 Implementation

# Create both AL source files simultaneously:
Task T004: "Create table extension in src/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al"
Task T005: "Create page extension in src/PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al"

# Then proceed sequentially:
# T006: Compile (needs both files)
# T007: Deploy (needs successful compilation)
# T008-T011: Test (needs deployed extension)

Implementation Strategy

MVP First (User Story 1 Only) - Recommended

  1. Complete Phase 1: Setup (T001-T003) - ~5 minutes
  2. Complete Phase 3: User Story 1 (T004-T011) - ~30 minutes
  • Create AL files (T004, T005) - 15 minutes
  • Compile and deploy (T006, T007) - 10 minutes
  • Test basic functionality (T008-T011) - 5 minutes
  1. STOP and VALIDATE: Field is visible, accepts valid input, persists data
  2. MVP READY: Extension can be demonstrated or deployed

Total MVP Time: ~35 minutes

Incremental Delivery (All User Stories)

  1. Complete Setup → Foundation ready (~5 minutes)
  2. Add User Story 1 → Test independently → MVP Deployed (~35 minutes total)
  3. Add User Story 2 → Test validation → Deploy update (~15 minutes)
  4. Add User Story 3 → Test new customers → Deploy update (~15 minutes)
  5. Polish & UAT Sign-off → Production Ready (~20 minutes)

Total Complete Time: ~1.5 hours for all user stories + polish + UAT sign-off

Sequential vs Parallel

Sequential (Single Developer - Recommended):

  • Follow phases in order: Setup → US1 → US2 → US3 → Polish
  • Each checkpoint validates before proceeding
  • Lower risk, easier to debug

Parallel (Not Applicable):

  • This extension has only 2 AL objects with dependencies
  • User Stories 2 and 3 are testing phases, not implementation
  • No true parallelization opportunities beyond T004/T005

Notes

  • [P] tasks: Different files or independent verifications, no dependencies
  • [Story] label: Maps task to specific user story for traceability
  • Each user story should be independently completable and testable
  • No automated tests: Testing is manual UAT using scenarios from spec.md and quickstart.md
  • Critical AL constraints:
  • DO NOT modify app.json
  • DO NOT modify .alpackages/
  • USE explicit Rec. prefix (NoImplicitWith feature enabled)
  • USE object IDs within 50000-50249 range only
  • USE tableextension/pageextension (never modify base objects)
  • Commit strategy: Commit after each completed user story phase
  • Stop at any checkpoint to validate story independently
  • MVP = User Story 1: Fully functional extension demonstrating BC extensibility

UAT Acceptance Criteria

Before production deployment, verify:

✅ All critical tests (TS-01 through TS-09) pass ✅ Field visible on Customer Card in expected location ✅ Valid values (3+ chars or blank) save successfully ✅ Invalid values (1-2 chars) blocked with clear error message ✅ Data persists across sessions ✅ New customer creation works with field ✅ Standard customer functionality unaffected (zero regression) ✅ Extension compiles with zero errors and warnings ✅ Business user completes UAT sign-off checklist

Exit Criteria: All checkboxes above marked complete before production deployment approved.

Quick Reference

Total Tasks: 25 tasks (T001-T025)

Task Breakdown by Phase:

  • Phase 1 (Setup): 3 tasks
  • Phase 2 (Foundational): 0 tasks (not applicable)
  • Phase 3 (User Story 1 - MVP): 8 tasks (4 implementation + 4 testing)
  • Phase 4 (User Story 2): 4 tasks (validation testing)
  • Phase 5 (User Story 3): 3 tasks (new customer testing)
  • Phase 6 (Polish): 7 tasks (final validation and sign-off)

Parallel Opportunities:

  • Setup phase: T001, T002, T003 (all can run together)
  • US1 implementation: T004, T005 (both AL files simultaneously)
  • Polish phase: T019, T020, T023, T025 (independent validations)

Critical Path: Setup → US1 Implementation → US1 Testing → US2 Testing → US3 Testing → Polish

Suggested MVP Scope: Phase 1 + Phase 3 (User Story 1 only) = 11 tasks, ~35 minutes

Estimated Total Time: ~1.5 hours (all phases including UAT sign-off)

checklists/requirements.md50 lines

Specification Quality Checklist: Business Central Hello World Extension

Purpose: Validate specification completeness and quality before proceeding to planning

Created: 2026-06-13

Feature: spec.md

Content Quality

  • [x] No implementation details (languages, frameworks, APIs)
  • [x] Focused on user value and business needs
  • [x] Written for non-technical stakeholders
  • [x] All mandatory sections completed

Requirement Completeness

  • [x] No [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers remain
  • [x] Requirements are testable and unambiguous
  • [x] Success criteria are measurable
  • [x] Success criteria are technology-agnostic (no implementation details)
  • [x] All acceptance scenarios are defined
  • [x] Edge cases are identified
  • [x] Scope is clearly bounded
  • [x] Dependencies and assumptions identified

Feature Readiness

  • [x] All functional requirements have clear acceptance criteria
  • [x] User scenarios cover primary flows
  • [x] Feature meets measurable outcomes defined in Success Criteria
  • [x] No implementation details leak into specification

Notes

Validation Result: PASSED

All checklist items have been validated and passed:

  • Content Quality: The specification focuses on WHAT users need (custom field on Customer Card with validation) and WHY (demonstrate Business Central extensibility), without specifying HOW to implement beyond the necessary AL object types required by the platform architecture. The spec is written for business stakeholders and testers.
  • Requirements: All 19 functional requirements are testable and unambiguous. No [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers are present - the technical requirements document provided comprehensive details including exact field names, object IDs, validation rules, and error messages.
  • Success Criteria: All 10 success criteria are measurable (with specific metrics like "under 2 seconds", "100% accuracy", "zero regression") and technology-agnostic (focused on user-facing outcomes rather than system internals).
  • Acceptance Scenarios: Three prioritized user stories (P1-P3) cover the complete feature scope with detailed Given-When-Then scenarios. Edge cases are identified for boundary conditions, special characters, concurrent access, API usage, and data import scenarios.
  • Scope: The feature is clearly bounded to a minimal Customer table extension with one field and validation. The Assumptions section explicitly lists what is in scope (single text field, basic validation) and what is out of scope (translation files, custom permissions, data migration, optional Message popup).

Ready for next phase: The specification is complete and ready for /speckit-plan to generate the implementation plan.

contracts/extension-objects.md282 lines

Extension Objects Contract

Feature: Business Central Hello World Extension

Date: 2026-06-13

Purpose: Define the AL extension objects, their structure, and interface contracts

Overview

This Business Central extension exposes its functionality through AL extension objects. These contracts define what the extension adds to the Business Central application and how users/systems interact with it.

Extension Metadata

Extension ID: f18f7b09-b29a-5558-88cf-24c0d4c08a7a

Extension Name: bsbench-01-pte-hello-world-github-speckit-claude

Publisher: bs_bench

Version: 1.0.0.4

Runtime: 16.0

Application: 27.0.0.0

Object ID Range: 50000-50249

Extension Objects

1. Table Extension: PTE Customer Ext

Object Type: tableextension

Object ID: 50100

Object Name: "PTE Customer Ext"

Extends: Customer (Table 18)

Purpose: Adds custom "PTE Hello World Note" field to the Customer table

Contract:

tableextension 50100 "PTE Customer Ext" extends Customer
{
    fields
    {
        field(50100; "PTE Hello World Note"; Text[100])
        {
            Caption = 'Hello World Note';
            DataClassification = CustomerContent;
            
            trigger OnValidate()
            // Validation contract: see below
        }
    }
}

Field Contract:

PropertyValueContract Guarantee
Field Number50100Stable - will not change in future versions
Field Name"PTE Hello World Note"Stable - API/integration safe to reference
Field TypeText[100]Stable - max 100 characters enforced
Caption"Hello World Note"Stable - UI label for end users
DataClassificationCustomerContentStable - GDPR compliance classification
RequiredNo (optional)Stable - blank values always allowed

Validation Contract:

INPUT: Field value (string, 0-100 characters)

VALIDATION RULES:
1. IF value is empty/blank THEN 
     ACCEPT (no error)
   
2. ELSE IF length(value) < 3 THEN
     REJECT with Error('Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters')
   
3. ELSE
     ACCEPT (validation passes)

OUTPUT: 
  - Success: Field value persisted to database
  - Failure: Error message displayed, save blocked, field reverted to previous value

Trigger Events:

TriggerWhen FiredContract
OnValidateUser moves off field, or record save initiatedValidates minimum 3-character length for non-blank values

Breaking Changes - None Expected:

  • Field number, name, and type are stable
  • Validation logic may be adjusted (e.g., changing minimum length from 3 to 5) in future versions - this would be documented as a behavioral change

2. Page Extension: PTE Customer Card Ext

Object Type: pageextension

Object ID: 50110

Object Name: "PTE Customer Card Ext"

Extends: "Customer Card" (Page 21)

Purpose: Displays "PTE Hello World Note" field on Customer Card UI

Contract:

pageextension 50110 "PTE Customer Card Ext" extends "Customer Card"
{
    layout
    {
        addlast(General)
        {
            field("PTEHelloWorldNote"; Rec."PTE Hello World Note")
            {
                ApplicationArea = All;
                Caption = 'Hello World Note';
                ToolTip = 'Specifies a short demo note used to demonstrate Business Central extensibility.';
            }
        }
    }
}

UI Contract:

PropertyValueContract Guarantee
Field Control Name"PTEHelloWorldNote"Stable - internal identifier
Source FieldRec."PTE Hello World Note"Stable - bound to table field 50100
ApplicationAreaAllStable - visible in all application areas
Caption"Hello World Note"Stable - UI label
ToolTip"Specifies a short demo note..."Stable - hover text for users
LocationGeneral FastTab, last positionSemi-stable - will remain in General, but may not always be last if other extensions add fields

User Interaction Contract:

USER ACTION: Open Customer Card (Page 21)
SYSTEM RESPONSE: "Hello World Note" field visible in General FastTab

USER ACTION: Click into "Hello World Note" field
SYSTEM RESPONSE: Text input active, max 100 characters

USER ACTION: Enter text and move to next field
SYSTEM RESPONSE: 
  - IF text is blank OR >= 3 characters: Field accepts value
  - IF text is 1-2 characters: Error message displayed, value rejected

USER ACTION: Save customer record
SYSTEM RESPONSE:
  - IF field passes validation: Record saved with field value
  - IF field fails validation: Save blocked, error displayed

API/Integration Contract

The extended field is automatically available through all standard Business Central integration channels:

OData Web Services

Endpoint: /api/v2.0/companies({companyId})/customers({customerId})

Field Exposure: The "PTE Hello World Note" field is included in the Customer entity JSON/XML

Example JSON:

{
  "id": "...",
  "number": "10000",
  "displayName": "Adatum Corporation",
  "pteHelloWorldNote": "This is a test note",
  ...
}

Validation: OnValidate trigger fires on API writes - invalid values (1-2 chars) are rejected with HTTP 400 error

SOAP Web Services

Service: Customer (Page 21 web service)

Field Name: PTE_Hello_World_Note

Validation: OnValidate trigger fires on SOAP writes - invalid values rejected with SOAP fault

Excel Integration (Edit in Excel)

Column Name: "Hello World Note"

Behavior:

  • Field appears as editable column in Excel
  • On publish back to BC, validation applies
  • Rows with invalid values (1-2 chars) fail to import with error message

RapidStart Configuration Packages

Field Inclusion: Field is included in Customer table configuration packages

Import Behavior: Validation applies during import - invalid values cause import errors

Extension Dependencies

Dependencies: None

This extension has no dependencies on other extensions. It only depends on:

  • Base Application (built-in Customer table and Customer Card page)
  • Business Central platform runtime 16.0+

Conflicts:

  • May conflict with other extensions that modify the same field number (50100) on Customer table
  • No known conflicts with standard Microsoft extensions

Backward Compatibility

Version 1.0.0.4 Contract Guarantees:

  1. Field Stability: Field number 50100 and name "PTE Hello World Note" will not change
  2. Optional Field: Field will always be optional (no future change to required)
  3. Data Preservation: Existing data in the field will be preserved across extension updates
  4. Validation Relaxation: Validation may become less strict (e.g., reducing min length), but never more strict without major version bump

Breaking Changes Requiring Major Version:

  • Deleting the field
  • Changing field type (e.g., Text[100] to Integer)
  • Making the field required
  • Changing object IDs (50100, 50110)
  • Making validation more restrictive (e.g., minimum length 3 → 10)

Non-Breaking Changes (minor version):

  • Changing Caption or ToolTip text
  • Relaxing validation (e.g., minimum length 3 → 0)
  • Adding additional fields to the extension
  • UI layout adjustments (moving field within same page)

Testing Contract

To verify the extension contract is fulfilled:

  1. Field Exists: Query Customer table, verify field 50100 exists
  2. Field Visible: Open Customer Card, verify "Hello World Note" field in General tab
  3. Blank Allowed: Save customer with blank note - should succeed
  4. Valid Value: Save customer with "Test" (4 chars) - should succeed
  5. Invalid Value: Enter "Hi" (2 chars) - should display error and block save
  6. Persistence: Save value, close/reopen customer - value should persist
  7. API Access: Query customer via OData API - field should be in response

Summary

This extension contract defines two AL objects:

  • tableextension 50100: Adds one field to Customer table with validation
  • pageextension 50110: Displays field on Customer Card UI

The contract guarantees:

  • Stable field numbers and names
  • Consistent validation behavior (3-char minimum for non-blank)
  • Backward compatibility for data and integrations
  • No breaking changes without major version bump

The extension is self-contained, has no dependencies, and integrates seamlessly with Business Central's standard data access methods (UI, OData, SOAP, Excel, RapidStart).

Speckit + Codex8 files
spec.md111 lines

Feature Specification: Customer Note Extension

Feature Branch: [001-customer-note-extension]

Created: 2026-06-15

Status: Draft

Input: User description: "Minimal Hello World extension for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central that adds a PTE Hello World Note field to Customer, shows it on Customer Card, and validates non-blank values are at least 3 characters."

User Scenarios & Testing (mandatory)

User Story 1 - Capture a Customer Demo Note (Priority: P1)

A business user maintaining customer records can view and edit a short Hello World Note on a customer card without disrupting standard customer maintenance.

Why this priority: The primary value is demonstrating that users can add and maintain a simple customer-specific note through the existing customer workflow.

Independent Test: Can be fully tested by installing the feature, opening an existing customer card, entering a valid note, saving, closing, and reopening the same customer to confirm the value remains visible.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given the feature is available and an existing customer card is open, When the user reviews the General customer information, Then the Hello World Note field is visible with a clear business label.
  2. Given a customer card is open, When the user enters Test in the Hello World Note field and saves the customer, Then the customer record saves successfully.
  3. Given a valid Hello World Note has been saved for a customer, When the user closes and reopens that customer card, Then the saved note remains visible.

User Story 2 - Enforce Minimum Note Quality (Priority: P2)

A business user is prevented from saving a non-blank Hello World Note that is too short to be meaningful, while still being allowed to leave the field blank.

Why this priority: The validation rule demonstrates controlled data quality without making the new note mandatory.

Independent Test: Can be tested by attempting to enter blank, two-character, three-character, and longer values and confirming that only non-blank values shorter than 3 characters are blocked.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a customer card is open, When the user clears the Hello World Note field and saves the customer, Then the customer record saves successfully.
  2. Given a customer card is open, When the user enters a non-blank value shorter than 3 characters, Then the system shows a validation error and does not accept the value.
  3. Given a customer card is open, When the user enters exactly 3 characters in the Hello World Note field and saves the customer, Then the customer record saves successfully.

User Story 3 - Confirm Non-Intrusive Customer Maintenance (Priority: P3)

A tester or business reviewer can confirm that standard customer card behavior remains unchanged when the Hello World Note is unused or while normal customer fields are edited.

Why this priority: The feature is intended to be minimal and non-intrusive, so standard customer maintenance must continue to work normally.

Independent Test: Can be tested by opening multiple customer cards with blank notes and editing standard customer information without interacting with the new field.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given the feature is available, When the user opens customer cards where the Hello World Note is blank, Then the cards open without errors or forced input.
  2. Given a customer card is open, When the user edits standard customer fields such as name, address, or phone number, Then those fields continue to save and behave normally.
  3. Given a user with normal customer maintenance access opens a customer card, When they edit the Hello World Note field, Then no additional permission setup is required beyond their normal customer edit access.

Edge Cases

  • Blank Hello World Note values are allowed and must not trigger validation errors.
  • Non-blank values with 1 or 2 characters are rejected.
  • Values with exactly 3 characters are accepted.
  • Values up to the defined note length limit are accepted.
  • Existing and newly created customers both support the Hello World Note field.
  • Customer cards with no note values continue to load normally.
  • Standard customer fields and workflows remain unaffected by the presence of the new field.

Requirements (mandatory)

Functional Requirements

  • FR-001: The system MUST add exactly one new customer note field named PTE Hello World Note.
  • FR-002: The PTE Hello World Note field MUST store text values up to 100 characters.
  • FR-003: The system MUST display the Hello World Note field on the Customer Card in the General customer information area.
  • FR-004: Users with normal customer maintenance access MUST be able to view and edit the Hello World Note field when they can edit customer records.
  • FR-005: The system MUST allow the Hello World Note field to remain blank.
  • FR-006: The system MUST reject non-blank Hello World Note values shorter than 3 characters.
  • FR-007: The system MUST accept Hello World Note values that are exactly 3 characters.
  • FR-008: The system MUST accept Hello World Note values longer than 3 characters up to the field length limit.
  • FR-009: The system MUST preserve saved Hello World Note values after the customer card is closed and reopened.
  • FR-010: The system MUST support the Hello World Note field for both existing customers and newly created customers.
  • FR-011: The system MUST deliver the customer note capability without directly modifying standard customer records or screens outside the supported customization model.
  • FR-012: The system MUST allow standard customer fields and customer card behavior to continue without regression.
  • FR-013: The feature MUST install in a sandbox or UAT environment without blocking access to existing customer cards.
  • FR-014: The feature MUST not show informational popups during normal note entry unless explicitly enabled for demonstration or UAT review.

Key Entities

  • Customer: Represents a business customer record. The feature adds one optional Hello World Note value to this existing entity.
  • Hello World Note: An optional customer-specific text value with a maximum length of 100 characters and a minimum length rule only when populated.

Success Criteria (mandatory)

Measurable Outcomes

  • SC-001: 100% of tested customer cards show the Hello World Note field in the agreed customer information location after the feature is installed.
  • SC-002: 100% of tested blank, three-character, and longer valid note entries save successfully.
  • SC-003: 100% of tested one-character and two-character note entries are blocked with a validation error.
  • SC-004: 100% of tested saved valid notes remain visible after closing and reopening the customer card.
  • SC-005: 100% of tested standard customer field edits continue to save successfully during regression testing.
  • SC-006: A business reviewer can complete the primary note entry and validation tests in under 10 minutes using an existing sandbox or UAT customer record.

Assumptions

  • The target users already have normal access to create or maintain customer records.
  • The feature targets a sandbox or UAT validation flow before production release.
  • Blank notes are acceptable because the feature is demonstrative and should not force data entry.
  • The Hello World Note label is the business-facing label shown to users.
  • Optional informational popup behavior is excluded from the default scope to avoid disrupting normal data entry.
  • API integrations, translation files, permission set changes, and data migration are outside the scope of this minimal feature.
plan.md78 lines

Implementation Plan: Customer Note Extension

Branch: [001-customer-note-extension] | Date: 2026-06-15 | Spec: spec.md

Input: Feature specification from /specs/001-customer-note-extension/spec.md

Note: This template is filled in by the /speckit-plan command. See .specify/templates/plan-template.md for the execution workflow.

Summary

Implement a minimal Business Central AL extension that adds one optional Hello World Note value to Customer records, exposes it on the Customer Card, and enforces a minimum length only when the value is populated. The implementation will use extension objects only, placed under src/, with object IDs selected from the configured 50000..50249 range and references limited to objects available in the loaded Microsoft symbol packages.

Technical Context

Language/Version: AL for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central; app.json runtime 16.0, application 27.0.0.0, platform 1.0.0.0

Primary Dependencies: Microsoft symbol packages in .alpackages/, including Microsoft_Base Application_27.0.38460.40691.app; verified symbols include table Customer (18) and page Customer Card (21)

Storage: Business Central customer table extension field stored with Customer records; no separate storage service or migration file

Testing: AL compile/package validation plus manual sandbox/UAT checks from the feature spec and quickstart

Target Platform: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central sandbox/UAT or compatible on-premises environment matching application 27.0.0.0

Project Type: Business Central per-tenant AL extension

Performance Goals: Customer Card opens normally with no measurable delay from the added field; validation completes during standard field validation interaction

Constraints: Do not modify app.json or .alpackages/; do not change app id, name, publisher, or version; use only object IDs 50000..50249; write AL source under src/; use extension objects rather than modifying Microsoft base objects; default implementation excludes informational popups

Scale/Scope: One Customer field, one Customer Card placement, one validation rule, no APIs, no translations, no permission set changes, no data migration

Constitution Check

GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.

The constitution file currently contains placeholder principle text only and defines no enforceable gates. This plan therefore applies the explicit project and prompt constraints as the active gates:

  • Use extension-only customization for standard Business Central objects.
  • Keep source files in src/.
  • Respect app.json runtime, application version, and object ID range.
  • Do not modify app.json or .alpackages/.
  • Keep scope minimal and aligned to the feature specification.

Initial gate status: PASS.

Post-design gate status: PASS. The generated design artifacts keep the scope to one table extension, one page extension, no external contracts, and manual validation guidance.

Project Structure

Documentation (this feature)

specs/001-customer-note-extension/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   └── customer-card-ui.md
└── tasks.md

Source Code (repository root)

src/
├── tableextensions/
│   └── PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al
└── pageextensions/
    └── PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al

Structure Decision: Use a single AL project rooted at the repository with all feature source under src/. Split table and page extensions into dedicated subfolders to keep Business Central object types easy to scan while remaining minimal.

Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations or complexity exceptions are required.

research.md41 lines

Research: Customer Note Extension

Decision: Target Business Central AL Runtime and Symbols

Use AL runtime 16.0 and target application 27.0.0.0 from app.json.

Rationale: The project metadata is preconfigured and must not be changed. Planning and implementation must avoid syntax or platform behavior newer than those values.

Alternatives considered: Updating project metadata was rejected because the planning prompt explicitly forbids modifying app.json.

Decision: Use Loaded Microsoft Base Application Symbols

Reference standard table Customer (18) and page Customer Card (21), both verified in Microsoft_Base Application_27.0.38460.40691.app.

Rationale: The feature extends the existing Customer data and Customer Card UI. Verifying symbols confirms the object names exist in the loaded package before implementation tasks are generated.

Alternatives considered: Referencing unverified object names was rejected because the prompt requires using only objects available in .alpackages/.

Decision: Use Object IDs from 50000..50249

Assign the feature's extension objects and field IDs from the configured app.json range 50000..50249.

Rationale: This range is the only object ID range available to the project. It also fits the minimal per-tenant extension sample.

Alternatives considered: The source requirement's example IDs 50100 and 50110 are valid within the range and may be used, but implementation tasks should still treat app.json as the authority.

Decision: Default to Non-Intrusive Validation Only

Implement only the minimum-length error for non-blank values and exclude informational popups from the default behavior.

Rationale: The specification says informational popups should not appear during normal note entry unless explicitly enabled. The minimal production-oriented behavior is less disruptive for repeated data entry.

Alternatives considered: Adding a demonstration message was rejected for default implementation because it creates extra interaction cost and is optional in the requirements.

Decision: Manual Sandbox/UAT Validation

Use compile/package validation and manual Business Central customer card scenarios as the validation path for this feature.

Rationale: The project does not currently include an AL test app or automated test framework, and the feature request is framed around UAT scenarios. Manual validation directly exercises the user-facing workflow.

Alternatives considered: Creating a full automated AL test app was rejected as outside the minimal feature scope and would require additional objects not requested by the specification.

data-model.md50 lines

Data Model: Customer Note Extension

Entity: Customer

Existing Business Central customer record extended by this feature.

Added Field

FieldTypeRequiredRules
PTE Hello World NoteText up to 100 charactersNoBlank is allowed. Non-blank values must contain at least 3 characters.

Relationships

  • The Hello World Note belongs to exactly one Customer record.
  • No new standalone entity or relationship table is introduced.

Validation Rules

  • Blank value: accepted.
  • One-character or two-character value: rejected with a validation error.
  • Three-character value: accepted.
  • Four to 100 character value: accepted.
  • Values over 100 characters: rejected by the field length limit.

State Transitions

Blank -> Valid note: allowed
Valid note -> Blank: allowed
Valid note -> Different valid note: allowed
Blank or valid note -> Short non-blank note: rejected

Entity: Customer Card Display

Existing customer maintenance screen extended by this feature.

Added Display Element

ElementLocationBehavior
Hello World Note fieldCustomer Card, General customer information areaDisplays and edits the Customer note value using the same validation rules as the Customer entity.

Non-Goals

  • No additional permission entity.
  • No data migration entity.
  • No external API contract.
  • No translation resource model.
quickstart.md35 lines

Quickstart: Customer Note Extension

Prerequisites

  • Business Central AL development environment with the AL extension for Visual Studio Code.
  • Existing app.json and .alpackages/ from the repository root.
  • Access to a sandbox or UAT environment where the extension can be published.
  • A user account with normal customer maintenance access.

Build Validation

  1. Open the repository root in Visual Studio Code.
  2. Confirm source files are under src/.
  3. Package the extension using the AL tooling.
  4. Confirm packaging completes without compile errors or warnings caused by this feature.
  5. Confirm app.json and .alpackages/ were not modified.

Sandbox/UAT Validation

  1. Publish and install the generated extension package to the sandbox or UAT environment.
  2. Open Business Central and navigate to an existing customer card.
  3. Confirm the Hello World Note field is visible in the General customer information area.
  4. Leave the field blank and save or close the customer. Expected result: no validation error.
  5. Enter Hi and move off the field or save. Expected result: validation error and value is not accepted.
  6. Enter ABC and save. Expected result: customer saves successfully.
  7. Enter Test and save. Expected result: customer saves successfully.
  8. Close and reopen the same customer. Expected result: the saved valid note remains visible.
  9. Create a new customer using normal required fields and a valid Hello World Note. Expected result: customer saves successfully.
  10. Edit standard customer fields such as name, address, or phone number. Expected result: standard behavior is unchanged.

Scope Checks

  • Confirm no informational popup appears during normal valid note entry.
  • Confirm no API, translation, permission set, or data migration changes were introduced.
  • Confirm implementation uses extension objects only and does not modify Microsoft base objects.
tasks.md175 lines

Tasks: Customer Note Extension

Input: Design documents from /specs/001-customer-note-extension/

Prerequisites: plan.md, spec.md, research.md, data-model.md, contracts/customer-card-ui.md, quickstart.md

Tests: Automated tests were not requested. Validation tasks use AL compile/package checks and manual sandbox/UAT scenarios from quickstart.md.

Organization: Tasks are grouped by user story to enable independent implementation and testing of each story.

Format: [ID] [P?] [Story] Description

  • [P]: Can run in parallel (different files, no dependencies)
  • [Story]: Which user story this task belongs to (US1, US2, US3)
  • Include exact file paths in descriptions

Phase 1: Setup (Shared Infrastructure)

Purpose: Prepare the AL source layout and confirm project metadata constraints before implementation.

  • [X] T001 Create AL source folder structure in src/tableextensions/ and src/pageextensions/
  • [X] T002 Verify app.json keeps runtime 16.0, application 27.0.0.0, and id range 50000..50249 without modifying app.json
  • [X] T003 Verify .alpackages/Microsoft_Base Application_27.0.38460.40691.app contains table Customer and page Customer Card without modifying .alpackages/

Phase 2: Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)

Purpose: Establish the object ID and naming decisions that all user stories depend on.

CRITICAL: No user story work can begin until this phase is complete.

  • [X] T004 Reserve object and field IDs from app.json range in specs/001-customer-note-extension/tasks.md: tableextension 50100, field 50100, pageextension 50110
  • [X] T005 Confirm implementation excludes optional informational popup behavior in specs/001-customer-note-extension/tasks.md

Checkpoint: Foundation ready - user story implementation can now begin.

Phase 3: User Story 1 - Capture a Customer Demo Note (Priority: P1) MVP

Goal: Business users can view, edit, save, and reopen a short Hello World Note on a customer card.

Independent Test: Install the feature, open an existing customer card, enter Test, save, close, and reopen the same customer to confirm the value remains visible.

Implementation for User Story 1

  • [X] T006 [US1] Create table extension PTE Customer Ext in src/tableextensions/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al extending Customer with field PTE Hello World Note as Text[100]
  • [X] T007 [US1] Set field caption Hello World Note and data classification CustomerContent in src/tableextensions/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al
  • [X] T008 [US1] Create page extension PTE Customer Card Ext in src/pageextensions/PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al extending Customer Card
  • [X] T009 [US1] Add field control for Rec."PTE Hello World Note" to the General area in src/pageextensions/PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al
  • [X] T010 [US1] Set page field ApplicationArea, caption, and tooltip in src/pageextensions/PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al
  • [X] T011 [US1] Package or compile the AL project from the repository root and resolve any compile errors in src/tableextensions/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al or src/pageextensions/PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al
  • [ ] T012 [US1] Run quickstart validation steps 1-3 and 6-8 from specs/001-customer-note-extension/quickstart.md in sandbox/UAT

Checkpoint: User Story 1 is fully functional and testable independently.

Phase 4: User Story 2 - Enforce Minimum Note Quality (Priority: P2)

Goal: Business users can leave the note blank, but non-blank values shorter than 3 characters are rejected.

Independent Test: On a customer card, try blank, Hi, ABC, and Test; blank and values with at least 3 characters save, while Hi is rejected.

Implementation for User Story 2

  • [X] T013 [US2] Add OnValidate logic to field PTE Hello World Note in src/tableextensions/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al allowing blank values
  • [X] T014 [US2] Add minimum length validation and error message for non-blank values shorter than 3 characters in src/tableextensions/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al
  • [X] T015 [US2] Confirm the OnValidate logic does not show informational Message popups in src/tableextensions/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al
  • [X] T016 [US2] Package or compile the AL project from the repository root and resolve validation-related compile errors in src/tableextensions/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al
  • [ ] T017 [US2] Run quickstart validation steps 4-7 from specs/001-customer-note-extension/quickstart.md in sandbox/UAT

Checkpoint: User Stories 1 and 2 both work independently.

Phase 5: User Story 3 - Confirm Non-Intrusive Customer Maintenance (Priority: P3)

Goal: Standard customer card behavior remains unchanged when the new field is unused or while normal customer fields are edited.

Independent Test: Open multiple customer cards with blank notes and edit standard customer fields without interacting with the new field.

Implementation for User Story 3

  • [X] T018 [US3] Review src/pageextensions/PTECustomerCardExt.PageExt.al to confirm only the Hello World Note control is added to the Customer Card
  • [X] T019 [US3] Review src/tableextensions/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al to confirm only the PTE Hello World Note field is added to Customer
  • [X] T020 [US3] Package or compile the AL project from the repository root and confirm no warnings or errors caused by src/
  • [ ] T021 [US3] Run quickstart validation steps 9-10 from specs/001-customer-note-extension/quickstart.md in sandbox/UAT
  • [ ] T022 [US3] Run quickstart scope checks from specs/001-customer-note-extension/quickstart.md

Checkpoint: All user stories are independently functional.

Phase 6: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

Purpose: Final verification across all stories and documentation.

  • [X] T023 [P] Verify specs/001-customer-note-extension/data-model.md matches implemented field name, length, and validation behavior
  • [X] T024 [P] Verify specs/001-customer-note-extension/contracts/customer-card-ui.md matches implemented Customer Card field label and behavior
  • [X] T025 Verify app.json and .alpackages/ remain unchanged after implementation
  • [ ] T026 Run the full quickstart validation in specs/001-customer-note-extension/quickstart.md

Dependencies & Execution Order

Phase Dependencies

  • Setup (Phase 1): No dependencies - can start immediately.
  • Foundational (Phase 2): Depends on Setup completion - blocks all user stories.
  • User Story 1 (Phase 3): Depends on Foundational completion.
  • User Story 2 (Phase 4): Depends on User Story 1 field creation in src/tableextensions/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al.
  • User Story 3 (Phase 5): Depends on User Stories 1 and 2 because it verifies the completed extension behavior.
  • Polish (Phase 6): Depends on all desired user stories being complete.

User Story Dependencies

  • User Story 1 (P1): MVP. Can start after Phase 2.
  • User Story 2 (P2): Requires the Customer field from US1 before adding validation.
  • User Story 3 (P3): Requires the final table and page extension behavior before regression validation.

Within Each User Story

  • Create or update AL source before compile/package validation.
  • Compile/package before sandbox/UAT validation.
  • Complete the story checkpoint before moving to the next priority.

Parallel Opportunities

  • T002 and T003 can be checked in parallel after T001 if separate reviewers are available.
  • T023 and T024 can run in parallel after implementation because they review different documentation files.
  • Manual UAT checks can be split by scenario after a compiled package is available, but the AL source tasks in US1 and US2 touch dependent files and should remain sequential.

Parallel Example: Polish Documentation Checks

Task: "Verify specs/001-customer-note-extension/data-model.md matches implemented field name, length, and validation behavior"
Task: "Verify specs/001-customer-note-extension/contracts/customer-card-ui.md matches implemented Customer Card field label and behavior"

Implementation Strategy

MVP First (User Story 1 Only)

  1. Complete Phase 1: Setup.
  2. Complete Phase 2: Foundational decisions.
  3. Complete Phase 3: User Story 1.
  4. Stop and validate the visible, persistent Hello World Note field independently.

Incremental Delivery

  1. Add the visible Customer note field and Customer Card placement for MVP.
  2. Add minimum-length validation for non-blank notes.
  3. Complete regression and non-intrusive behavior checks.
  4. Finish documentation and full quickstart validation.

Single-Developer Strategy

This feature is small and touches two AL files. Implement sequentially in task order to avoid conflicting edits to src/tableextensions/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al.

Notes

  • [P] tasks use different files and can run in parallel.
  • [US1], [US2], and [US3] labels map to the user stories in specs/001-customer-note-extension/spec.md.
  • Every implementation task includes an exact file path.
  • Do not modify app.json or .alpackages/.
  • Keep all AL source under src/.

contracts/customer-card-ui.md41 lines

UI Contract: Customer Card Hello World Note

Surface

Customer Card.

User-Visible Field

LabelBacking ValueLocationEditable
Hello World NotePTE Hello World NoteGeneral customer information areaYes, when the user can edit the customer record

Interaction Contract

View Field

Given a user opens a Customer Card When the General customer information area is displayed Then the Hello World Note field is visible.

Save Blank Value

Given a user can edit a customer When the Hello World Note field is blank and the customer is saved Then the save succeeds.

Save Valid Value

Given a user can edit a customer When the Hello World Note field contains 3 to 100 characters and the customer is saved Then the save succeeds and the value is visible after reopening the customer.

Reject Short Value

Given a user can edit a customer When the Hello World Note field contains 1 or 2 characters Then the value is rejected with a validation error and is not accepted as the saved note.

Regression Contract

Standard customer fields such as name, address, and phone number continue to display, edit, and save using their existing behavior.

Hotel Management 02-hotel-management

Reservation lifecycle: room inventory, deposit, check-in, service charges, checkout posting with VAT.

Speckit + Claude9 files
spec.md189 lines

Feature Specification: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Feature Branch: feat/rebate-setup-seeding-layer

Created: 2026-06-13

Status: Draft

Input: User description: "Build a Business Central extension that supports the hotel guest lifecycle from reservation through billing. The extension is intended for hospitality operations using Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central."

User Scenarios & Testing (mandatory)

User Story 1 - Create Reservation and Capture Deposit (Priority: P1)

A Front Desk staff member creates a new guest reservation, assigns a room, and captures a deposit to confirm the booking.

Why this priority: This is the entry point for all guest interactions. Without the ability to create reservations and capture deposits, no other workflows can proceed. This is the foundation of the hotel operation.

Independent Test: Can be fully tested by creating a reservation record with customer and room details, capturing a deposit payment, and verifying that the reservation status is "Confirmed" and the room is marked as assigned. Delivers immediate value by enabling staff to accept bookings.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given the Front Desk has customer details and an available room, When they create a reservation and successfully capture a deposit, Then the reservation is saved with status "Confirmed", the room is assigned, and deposit amount is recorded
  2. Given a reservation exists for a specific room and date range, When Front Desk attempts to create another reservation for the same room with overlapping dates, Then the system prevents the duplicate booking and displays an overbooking error
  3. Given Front Desk attempts to capture a deposit, When the payment processing fails, Then the system does not mark the deposit as captured and notifies the user of the payment failure

User Story 2 - Check In Guest and Post Service Charges (Priority: P2)

A Front Desk staff member checks in a guest with a confirmed reservation and posts service charges (breakfast, laundry, transfer) during their stay.

Why this priority: This represents the active guest stay phase where most operational activity occurs. It enables revenue capture from ancillary services and tracks the guest's occupancy status.

Independent Test: Can be tested by updating a confirmed reservation to "Occupied" status and adding service charge line items. Delivers value by enabling staff to manage active guests and track billable services.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a reservation with status "Confirmed", When Front Desk performs check-in, Then the reservation status changes to "Occupied" and the check-in date is recorded
  2. Given a guest is checked in, When Front Desk posts a service charge (e.g., breakfast for $25), Then a service charge line is created linked to the reservation with the correct amount and description
  3. Given a guest is checked in, When Front Desk posts multiple service charges over several days, Then all charges are accumulated and linked to the same reservation

User Story 3 - Checkout Guest and Generate Invoice (Priority: P3)

A Front Desk staff member checks out a guest, triggering Finance to generate an invoice that includes room charges, service charges, VAT calculation, deposit application, and final payment settlement.

Why this priority: This is the final step in the guest lifecycle and is required to complete the financial transaction. It depends on prior steps (reservation, check-in, charges) being completed.

Independent Test: Can be tested by performing checkout on an occupied reservation, verifying that Finance can generate a single invoice containing all charges with correct VAT, deposit is applied, and final payment is recorded. Delivers value by completing the revenue cycle and releasing the room for new bookings.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a guest is checked in with service charges posted, When Front Desk performs checkout, Then the reservation status changes to "Closed" and the room is marked as available
  2. Given a reservation is closed, When Finance generates an invoice, Then the invoice includes nightly room charges, all service charges, VAT calculated at the configured rate, deposit is applied as a credit, and remaining balance is calculated
  3. Given an invoice has been generated for a reservation, When Finance attempts to generate another invoice for the same reservation, Then the system prevents duplicate invoice creation and displays an error
  4. Given an invoice is generated, When Finance records final payment, Then the payment is posted to the appropriate financial ledgers and the invoice is marked as paid

User Story 4 - Process Authorized Refund (Priority: P4)

A Finance staff member processes a refund for a guest in accordance with the hotel's refund policy, while Front Desk staff are prevented from initiating refunds.

Why this priority: This is an exception workflow that occurs infrequently and requires financial oversight. It depends on prior billing and payment steps.

Independent Test: Can be tested by Finance user initiating a refund action on a paid invoice and by Front Desk user attempting the same action and being denied. Delivers value by enabling proper refund handling while maintaining financial controls.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a paid invoice exists, When a Finance user initiates a refund, Then the refund is processed, posted to financial ledgers, and the invoice is updated with refund details
  2. Given a paid invoice exists, When a Front Desk user attempts to initiate a refund, Then the system denies access and displays an authorization error
  3. Given a Finance user is processing a refund, When they attempt to refund more than the paid amount, Then the system validates the refund amount and prevents over-refunding

Edge Cases

  • What happens when a reservation is created but the deposit payment fails during processing?
  • How does the system handle a guest who checks out before the originally scheduled check-out date?
  • What happens when VAT configuration is changed after a reservation is created but before invoice generation?
  • How does the system prevent a room from being assigned to multiple reservations for overlapping dates?
  • What happens when Finance attempts to generate an invoice for a reservation that is still in "Confirmed" status (not yet checked in)?
  • How does the system handle service charges posted after checkout but before invoice generation?

Requirements (mandatory)

Functional Requirements

Reservation Management

  • FR-001: System MUST allow Front Desk to create a new reservation with Reservation No., Customer No., Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date, and initial Status of "Confirmed"
  • FR-002: System MUST prevent creation of a reservation when the selected room is already assigned to another reservation with overlapping dates (overbooking prevention)
  • FR-003: System MUST allow Front Desk to update reservation details (dates, room assignment) before check-in
  • FR-004: System MUST support three reservation statuses: "Confirmed", "Occupied", and "Closed"
  • FR-005: System MUST allow Front Desk to transition a reservation from "Confirmed" to "Occupied" during check-in
  • FR-006: System MUST allow Front Desk to transition a reservation from "Occupied" to "Closed" during checkout

Room Inventory Management

  • FR-007: System MUST maintain a room inventory with Room No., Room Type, Nightly Rate, and Occupied (boolean) status
  • FR-008: System MUST mark a room as occupied when assigned to a reservation in "Occupied" status
  • FR-009: System MUST mark a room as available when a reservation is transitioned to "Closed" status
  • FR-010: System MUST prevent manual modification of room occupancy status; occupancy must be controlled exclusively through reservation lifecycle transitions

Deposit and Payment Processing

  • FR-011: System MUST allow Front Desk to capture a deposit payment for a reservation
  • FR-012: System MUST record the deposit amount on the reservation record
  • FR-013: System MUST track deposit capture success or failure status
  • FR-014: System MUST NOT mark a deposit as successfully captured if the payment processing fails
  • FR-015: System MUST apply captured deposit as a credit against the final invoice total

Service Charges

  • FR-016: System MUST allow Front Desk to post service charge line items linked to a reservation
  • FR-017: System MUST support service charge types including but not limited to: breakfast, laundry, and transfer services
  • FR-018: System MUST record service charge amount, description, date posted, and link to reservation
  • FR-019: System MUST include all service charges posted to a reservation in the final invoice calculation

Checkout and Billing

  • FR-020: System MUST allow Finance to generate an invoice for a reservation in "Closed" status
  • FR-021: System MUST prevent generation of more than one invoice per reservation (duplicate invoice prevention)
  • FR-022: System MUST calculate invoice total including: room charges (nightly rate × number of nights), all service charges, and applicable VAT
  • FR-023: System MUST calculate VAT based on configured tax rates at the time of invoice generation
  • FR-024: System MUST recalculate VAT if tax configuration changes occur before invoice generation
  • FR-025: System MUST apply deposit amount as a credit line item on the invoice
  • FR-026: System MUST calculate final amount due as: (room charges + service charges + VAT) - deposit
  • FR-027: System MUST allow Finance to record final payment against the invoice
  • FR-028: System MUST post invoice and payment transactions to appropriate financial ledgers (G/L accounts)

Security and Authorization

  • FR-029: System MUST restrict refund processing to users assigned the Finance role
  • FR-030: System MUST prevent users assigned the Front Desk role from accessing refund processing functions
  • FR-031: System MUST prevent Front Desk users from directly modifying tax setup configuration
  • FR-032: System MUST prevent Front Desk users from directly adjusting financial postings or ledger entries

Error Handling and Validation

  • FR-033: System MUST validate that Customer No. exists in Business Central Customer master data before creating a reservation
  • FR-034: System MUST validate that Room No. exists in room inventory before assignment
  • FR-035: System MUST validate that Check-out Date is after Check-in Date
  • FR-036: System MUST display clear error messages for validation failures including: overbooking, failed payment, duplicate invoice, and unauthorized access attempts

Key Entities

  • Reservation: Represents a guest's hotel booking, containing Reservation No., Customer No., Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date, Status (Confirmed/Occupied/Closed), Deposit Amount, Deposit Captured (boolean), and Invoice No. (when generated). Links to Customer and Room entities.
  • Room: Represents a hotel room in the inventory, containing Room No., Room Type (category/description), Nightly Rate (base price per night), and Occupied (boolean). Multiple reservations may reference the same room for different date ranges, but the system prevents overlapping occupancy.
  • Service Charge: Represents a billable service posted to a guest's account during their stay, containing Service Charge No., Reservation No. (foreign key), Service Type (breakfast/laundry/transfer/etc.), Amount, Description, and Date Posted. Multiple service charges link to a single reservation.
  • Invoice: Represents the final bill generated for a reservation, containing Invoice No., Reservation No. (foreign key), Invoice Date, Subtotal (room + services), VAT Amount, Deposit Credit, Total Amount Due, and Payment Status. One invoice is generated per reservation after checkout.
  • Customer: Standard Business Central customer entity (not modified by this extension). Referenced by Reservation via Customer No.

Success Criteria (mandatory)

Measurable Outcomes

  • SC-001: Front Desk users can create a complete reservation (customer, room, dates, deposit) in under 3 minutes
  • SC-002: System prevents 100% of overbooking attempts by validating room availability before confirming reservations
  • SC-003: System accurately reflects deposit capture failures with zero false-positive confirmations
  • SC-004: Finance can generate a complete invoice for a checked-out guest in under 2 minutes
  • SC-005: VAT calculations are 100% accurate based on configured tax rates at time of invoice generation
  • SC-006: System prevents 100% of unauthorized refund attempts by Front Desk users through role-based security
  • SC-007: Duplicate invoice prevention is 100% effective (no reservation can have more than one invoice)
  • SC-008: All financial postings from reservations, invoices, and payments correctly appear in Business Central G/L without manual reconciliation
  • SC-009: Room occupancy status is 100% accurate (occupied when guest is checked in, available after checkout)
  • SC-010: Service charges posted during a stay appear on the final invoice with 100% accuracy (no missing or duplicate charges)

Assumptions

  • Users have appropriate Business Central licenses and permissions configured by administrators before using this extension
  • Standard Business Central Customer master data is already populated and maintained
  • Payment processing integration with a payment gateway or cash receipt system exists separately; this extension records deposit amounts and success/failure status but does not implement payment provider integration
  • VAT/tax setup is configured in Business Central's standard tax configuration before invoice generation
  • Room inventory is set up and maintained by administrators before operational use
  • The extension will be deployed in a Business Central container environment (cloud or on-premises)
  • Standard Business Central financial posting patterns and G/L account structure will be followed
  • Users will access the extension through Business Central web client or desktop client
  • The extension does not handle loyalty programs, advance bookings beyond simple date reservations, or dynamic pricing (rate changes based on demand)
  • The extension does not implement payment gateway integration; it records payment status from external payment processing
  • Email notifications, confirmation letters, and guest communication are out of scope for this version
  • Reporting and analytics beyond standard Business Central capabilities are out of scope
  • Integration with property management systems (PMS), channel managers, or third-party booking platforms is out of scope
plan.md269 lines

Implementation Plan: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Branch: feat/rebate-setup-seeding-layer | Date: 2026-06-13 | Spec: spec.md

Input: Feature specification from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/spec.md

Summary

Build a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central AL extension supporting the complete hotel guest lifecycle: reservation creation, room assignment, deposit capture, check-in, service charge posting, checkout, invoice generation with VAT calculation, and role-based security for refund processing. The extension enforces operational workflows (overbooking prevention, duplicate invoice prevention, deposit tracking) while integrating with BC's standard financial posting mechanisms for G/L and customer ledger entries.

Technical Approach: Custom AL tables for domain entities (Reservation, Room, Service Charge), AL codeunits for business logic and validation, AL pages for UI, and permission sets for role-based access control. Integration with BC standard Customer table, VAT Posting Setup, and General Ledger posting via standard APIs.

Technical Context

Language/Version: AL (Application Language) for Business Central, Runtime 16.0, Application 27.0

Primary Dependencies:

  • Microsoft Base Application 27.0.38460.40691
  • Microsoft System Application 27.0.38460.40586
  • Microsoft Business Foundation 27.0.38460.40586
  • Standard BC number series and posting routines (Codeunit 12 "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line")

Storage: Business Central SQL Server database (managed by platform), AL table objects define schema

Testing: AL Test Codeunits (Subtype = Test) with test methods, executed via BC Test Runner or Invoke-ALTestTool

Target Platform: Business Central cloud or on-premises (BC 27.0), BC container for development/testing

Project Type: Business Central AL extension (app package .app file)

Performance Goals:

  • Create reservation: < 2 seconds response time
  • Invoice generation: < 5 seconds for typical reservation (3-night stay, 2-5 service charges)
  • Room availability query: < 1 second for 100-room hotel with 1000 reservations

Constraints:

  • Object ID range: 50000-50249 (250 IDs, allocated from app.json)
  • No modification of BC base application objects (must use tableextension/pageextension)
  • Must use BC standard posting patterns for financial transactions (no custom G/L manipulation)
  • AL runtime 16.0 feature set (NoImplicitWith enabled)

Scale/Scope:

  • Small to medium hotels (50-500 rooms)
  • ~100-200 active reservations at any time
  • ~25,000 reservations per year (70% occupancy, 100 rooms)
  • 3 user roles (Front Desk, Finance, Admin)
  • 8-10 custom tables, ~6-8 pages, ~4-6 codeunits, ~1-2 reports

Constitution Check

GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.

Status: ✅ PASSED (No constitution file configured - template only)

Note: The project has a .specify/memory/constitution.md file but it contains only template placeholders with no actual principles defined. Therefore, there are no constitutional gates to check. This plan proceeds without constitutional constraints.

Re-check after Phase 1: ✅ PASSED (No violations - no active constitution)

Project Structure

Documentation (this feature)

specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/
├── spec.md                          # Feature specification (/speckit-specify output)
├── plan.md                          # This file (/speckit-plan command output)

│   └── ui-pages.md                  #   Page definitions (UI contracts)

Source Code (repository root)

workspace/
├── app.json                         # Extension manifest (pre-configured, DO NOT MODIFY)
├── .alpackages/                     # Symbol packages (pre-configured, DO NOT MODIFY)
│   ├── Microsoft_Application_27.0.38460.40691.app
│   ├── Microsoft_Base Application_27.0.38460.40691.app
│   ├── Microsoft_Business Foundation_27.0.38460.40586.app
│   ├── Microsoft_System Application_27.0.38460.40586.app
│   └── System.app
└── src/                             # AL source files (create if missing)
    ├── tables/                      # Table objects (ID 50000-50099)
    │   ├── Tab50000.Reservation.al

    │   ├── Pag50121.ReservationCard.al

    │   ├── Pag50123.RoomCard.al
    │   ├── Pag50124.ServiceChargeList.al
    │   ├── Pag50125.InvoiceList.al
    │   ├── Pag50126.InvoiceCard.al
    │   └── Pag50127.HotelSetup.al
    ├── pageextensions/              # Page extensions (ID 50180-50199, if needed)
    ├── codeunits/                   # Codeunit objects (ID 50200-50239)
    │   ├── Cod50200.ReservationManagement.al

    │   ├── Cod50202.ServiceChargeManagement.al
    │   ├── Cod50203.InvoiceManagement.al

        └── Cod50252.TestSecurityRoles.al

Structure Decision: Single AL extension project structure (Option 1 adapted for BC AL). All AL objects under src/ organized by object type following BC community conventions. No separate frontend/backend - BC provides integrated UI/data/business logic stack. Test codeunits in dedicated test/ subfolder to separate production code from test code.

Rationale: BC AL extensions follow object-type organization pattern for clarity and maintainability. ID ranges allocated by object type (tables, pages, codeunits) as documented in research.md. This structure supports BC compiler requirements and aligns with standard AL project templates.

Complexity Tracking

Fill ONLY if Constitution Check has violations that must be justified

Status: No complexity tracking needed - no constitutional violations present.

Phase 0: Research (Complete)

Output: research.md

Research Summary

All technical unknowns resolved. Key decisions documented:

  1. AL Extension Architecture: Custom tables + codeunits + pages, no base object modifications
  2. ID Range Allocation: 50000-50249 divided by object type (tables, pages, codeunits, etc.)
  3. Financial Integration: Use BC standard Gen. Journal Line posting (Codeunit 12)
  4. VAT Calculation: Use BC standard VAT Posting Setup (posting groups)
  5. Security: AL permissionset objects with role-based assignments
  6. Overbooking Prevention: Table trigger validation with date range overlap query
  7. Duplicate Invoice Prevention: Foreign key + transaction-based check
  8. Deposit Tracking: Two-field approach (Amount + Captured boolean)
  9. Room Occupancy: FlowField (calculated field) for real-time status
  10. Testing: AL Test Codeunits with BC test libraries

No open questions remain. Implementation can proceed.

Phase 1: Design & Contracts (Complete)

Prerequisites: research.md complete ✓

Design Artifacts Generated

1. Data Model (data-model.md)

Core Entities:

  • Reservation (Table 50000): Central entity tracking guest bookings with fields for customer, room, dates, status (Confirmed/Occupied/Closed), deposit, and invoice linkage. Includes validation triggers for overbooking prevention and date range checks.
  • Room (Table 50001): Master data for hotel room inventory with room type, nightly rate, VAT posting group, and calculated occupancy status (FlowField).
  • Service Charge (Table 50002): Billable services posted to reservations during guest stay (breakfast, laundry, transfer, etc.) with amount, VAT group, and invoice posting flag.
  • Invoice: Decision deferred to implementation - either extend BC Sales Invoice (Table 36/112) or create custom table. Minimum fields: Invoice No., Reservation No., amounts, VAT, deposit credit, payment status.

Relationships:

  • Customer (BC standard) 1-to-N Reservation
  • Room 1-to-N Reservation
  • Reservation 1-to-N Service Charge
  • Reservation 1-to-0..1 Invoice (optional until billing)

State Machine: Reservation lifecycle: Confirmed → Occupied → Closed (one-way transitions enforced in codeunits)

Indexes: Strategic keys defined for performance-critical queries (room availability check, customer lookup, service charge retrieval)

2. Interface Contracts (contracts/)

Business Operations Contract (contracts/business-operations.md)

Defines AL codeunit public procedures (business logic APIs):

  • ReservationManagement (Codeunit 50200):
  • CreateReservation(): Create reservation with overbooking validation
  • UpdateReservation(): Modify reservation before check-in
  • CheckInGuest(): Transition Confirmed → Occupied
  • CheckOutGuest(): Transition Occupied → Closed
  • PaymentManagement (Codeunit 50201):
  • CaptureDeposit(): Record deposit with success/failure tracking
  • ServiceChargeManagement (Codeunit 50202):
  • PostServiceCharge(): Add billable service to reservation
  • InvoiceManagement (Codeunit 50203):
  • GenerateInvoice(): Create invoice with VAT calculation and deposit application
  • RefundManagement (Codeunit 50204):
  • ProcessRefund(): Handle refunds (Finance role only, enforces FR-029/FR-030)

Error Handling: Consistent error codes, boolean return values, transaction rollback on failure

Permission Enforcement: Procedures respect permission set assignments (HOTEL-FRONTDESK, HOTEL-FINANCE, HOTEL-ADMIN)

UI Pages Contract (contracts/ui-pages.md)

Defines BC page objects (user interface):

Front Desk Pages:

  • Reservation List (Page 50120): Grid view with filters, actions for check-in/check-out
  • Reservation Card (Page 50121): Detail view with FastTabs for general info, payment, service charges
  • Room List (Page 50122): Room inventory management
  • Room Card (Page 50123): Room detail editing
  • Service Charge List (Page 50124): Service charge entry and viewing

Finance Pages:

  • Invoice List (Page 50125): Invoice management with generation and payment actions
  • Invoice Card (Page 50126): Invoice detail with line items and financial breakdown

Admin Pages:

  • Hotel Setup (Page 50127): Extension configuration (number series, posting setup, G/L accounts)

Permission-Based Visibility: Actions and pages hidden/disabled based on user's permission set

UI/UX Guidelines: Standard BC controls (date pickers, lookups, validation messages), colored status indicators, keyboard shortcuts

3. Quickstart Validation Guide (quickstart.md)

Purpose: Runnable end-to-end scenarios proving feature correctness

Scenarios Defined:

  1. Complete Guest Lifecycle (Happy Path): Admin setup → create room → create reservation → capture deposit → check in → post service charges → check out → generate invoice → record payment. Validates all 10 success criteria (SC-001 through SC-010).
  1. Overbooking Prevention (Negative Test): Attempt to create overlapping reservation for same room, verify error (SC-002 validation).
  1. Unauthorized Refund Attempt (Security Test): Front Desk attempts refund (blocked), Finance processes refund (succeeds). Validates FR-029/FR-030 and SC-006.
  1. Duplicate Invoice Prevention (Negative Test): Attempt to generate second invoice for same reservation, verify error (SC-007 validation).
  1. Failed Deposit Capture (Error Handling Test): Simulate payment failure, verify "Deposit Captured" remains FALSE (FR-014 and SC-003 validation).
  1. VAT Recalculation (Configuration Change Test): Change VAT setup after reservation created but before invoice generation, verify invoice uses new rate (FR-024 and SC-005 validation).

Automated Test Suite: Commands for running AL Test Codeunits via PowerShell, test coverage targets (100% FR/SC coverage)

Troubleshooting: Common setup issues and fixes (missing customer data, VAT config, symbol packages)

4. Agent Context Update

The plan reference needs to be updated in the project's CLAUDE.md file (if it exists):
