  • Clear role-based security requirements
  • Complete data model with key entities and relationships
  • Realistic assumptions documenting scope boundaries

Notes

  • No clarifications needed - all requirements are unambiguous
  • Specification successfully avoids implementation details
  • All mandatory sections are complete with actionable content
  • Ready to proceed to /speckit-clarify (if needed) or /speckit-plan
contracts/business-operations.md433 lines

Business Operations Contract

Feature: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle Date: 2026-06-13 Purpose: Define the public interfaces (codeunit procedures) exposed by the extension for business operations

Overview

This extension exposes business logic through AL codeunits with public procedures. These procedures enforce business rules, validation, and state transitions defined in the specification.

Core Operations

1. Reservation Management

CreateReservation

Codeunit: ReservationManagement (Codeunit 50200)

Procedure Signature:

procedure CreateReservation(
    CustomerNo: Code[20]; 
    RoomNo: Code[20]; 
    CheckInDate: Date; 
    CheckOutDate: Date; 
    var ReservationNo: Code[20]
): Boolean

Purpose: Create a new reservation with automatic overbooking prevention

Inputs:

  • CustomerNo: Customer No. from BC Customer table
  • RoomNo: Room No. from Room master table
  • CheckInDate: Guest arrival date
  • CheckOutDate: Guest departure date

Outputs:

  • ReservationNo: Generated reservation number (passed by reference)
  • Return: TRUE if successful, FALSE if validation fails

Behavior:

  1. Validate CustomerNo exists in Customer table
  2. Validate RoomNo exists in Room table
  3. Validate CheckOutDate > CheckInDate
  4. Check for overlapping reservations (overbooking prevention)
  5. If validation passes:
  • Generate Reservation No. from number series
  • Create Reservation record with Status = Confirmed
  • Return TRUE
  1. If validation fails:
  • Set ReservationNo = '' (empty)
  • Raise error with specific validation message
  • Return FALSE

Error Codes:

  • CUSTOMER_NOT_FOUND: Customer No. does not exist
  • ROOM_NOT_FOUND: Room No. does not exist
  • INVALID_DATE_RANGE: Check-out date must be after check-in date
  • ROOM_NOT_AVAILABLE: Room is already booked for overlapping dates

UpdateReservation

Codeunit: ReservationManagement (Codeunit 50200)

Procedure Signature:

procedure UpdateReservation(
    ReservationNo: Code[20];
    NewRoomNo: Code[20];
    NewCheckInDate: Date;
    NewCheckOutDate: Date
): Boolean

Purpose: Update reservation details before check-in

Inputs:

  • ReservationNo: Existing reservation to update
  • NewRoomNo: New room assignment (or same room)
  • NewCheckInDate: New check-in date
  • NewCheckOutDate: New check-out date

Outputs:

  • Return: TRUE if successful, FALSE if validation fails

Behavior:

  1. Validate reservation exists and Status = Confirmed (cannot update after check-in)
  2. Validate new dates and room (same validation as CreateReservation)
  3. Check overbooking for new room/dates (excluding current reservation)
  4. Update Reservation record
  5. Return TRUE if successful

Error Codes:

  • RESERVATION_NOT_FOUND: Reservation No. does not exist
  • INVALID_STATUS: Cannot update reservation after check-in (Status ≠ Confirmed)
  • ROOM_NOT_AVAILABLE: New room is booked for overlapping dates

2. Deposit Processing

CaptureDeposit

Codeunit: PaymentManagement (Codeunit 50201)

Procedure Signature:

procedure CaptureDeposit(
    ReservationNo: Code[20];
    DepositAmount: Decimal;
    var PaymentSuccess: Boolean
): Boolean

Purpose: Record deposit payment for a reservation

Inputs:

  • ReservationNo: Reservation to apply deposit to
  • DepositAmount: Deposit amount to capture

Outputs:

  • PaymentSuccess: TRUE if payment processing succeeded, FALSE if failed (passed by reference)
  • Return: TRUE if operation completed (regardless of payment outcome), FALSE if validation error

Behavior:

  1. Validate reservation exists
  2. Validate DepositAmount > 0
  3. Call external payment processing (simulated or integrated)
  4. Update Reservation record:
  • Set "Deposit Amount" = DepositAmount
  • Set "Deposit Captured" = PaymentSuccess (TRUE only if payment succeeded)
  1. Return TRUE (operation completed)

Error Codes:

  • RESERVATION_NOT_FOUND: Reservation No. does not exist
  • INVALID_AMOUNT: Deposit amount must be greater than zero

Note: If payment processing fails, the procedure still returns TRUE (operation completed), but sets PaymentSuccess = FALSE and Deposit Captured = FALSE on the reservation. This satisfies FR-014 (MUST NOT mark deposit as captured if payment fails).

3. Guest Lifecycle Operations

CheckInGuest

Codeunit: ReservationManagement (Codeunit 50200)

Procedure Signature:

procedure CheckInGuest(ReservationNo: Code[20]): Boolean

Purpose: Transition reservation from Confirmed to Occupied status

Inputs:

  • ReservationNo: Reservation to check in

Outputs:

  • Return: TRUE if successful, FALSE if validation fails

Behavior:

  1. Validate reservation exists and Status = Confirmed
  2. Update Reservation.Status = Occupied
  3. Room.Occupied FlowField automatically updates to TRUE
  4. Return TRUE

Error Codes:

  • RESERVATION_NOT_FOUND: Reservation No. does not exist
  • INVALID_STATUS: Reservation must be in Confirmed status to check in

CheckOutGuest

Codeunit: ReservationManagement (Codeunit 50200)

Procedure Signature:

procedure CheckOutGuest(ReservationNo: Code[20]): Boolean

Purpose: Transition reservation from Occupied to Closed status and release room

Inputs:

  • ReservationNo: Reservation to check out

Outputs:

  • Return: TRUE if successful, FALSE if validation fails

Behavior:

  1. Validate reservation exists and Status = Occupied
  2. Update Reservation.Status = Closed
  3. Room.Occupied FlowField automatically updates to FALSE (if no other reservations for this room are Occupied)
  4. Return TRUE

Error Codes:

  • RESERVATION_NOT_FOUND: Reservation No. does not exist
  • INVALID_STATUS: Reservation must be in Occupied status to check out

4. Service Charge Operations

PostServiceCharge

Codeunit: ServiceChargeManagement (Codeunit 50202)

Procedure Signature:

procedure PostServiceCharge(
    ReservationNo: Code[20];
    ServiceType: Code[50];
    Description: Text[100];
    Amount: Decimal;
    VATProductPostingGroup: Code[20];
    var EntryNo: Integer
): Boolean

Purpose: Post a billable service charge to a guest's reservation

Inputs:

  • ReservationNo: Reservation to charge
  • ServiceType: Service type code (e.g., "BREAKFAST", "LAUNDRY")
  • Description: Detailed description
  • Amount: Charge amount
  • VATProductPostingGroup: VAT group for tax calculation

Outputs:

  • EntryNo: Generated Service Charge Entry No. (passed by reference)
  • Return: TRUE if successful, FALSE if validation fails

Behavior:

  1. Validate reservation exists and Status IN (Confirmed, Occupied)
  2. Validate Amount > 0
  3. Validate VATProductPostingGroup exists
  4. Create Service Charge record with:
  • Auto-generated Entry No.
  • Posting Date = TODAY
  • Posted to Invoice = FALSE
  1. Return TRUE

Error Codes:

  • RESERVATION_NOT_FOUND: Reservation No. does not exist
  • INVALID_STATUS: Cannot post charges to a closed reservation
  • INVALID_AMOUNT: Amount must be greater than zero
  • VAT_GROUP_NOT_FOUND: VAT Product Posting Group does not exist

5. Invoice Generation (Finance Role)

GenerateInvoice

Codeunit: InvoiceManagement (Codeunit 50203)

Procedure Signature:

procedure GenerateInvoice(
    ReservationNo: Code[20];
    var InvoiceNo: Code[20]
): Boolean

Purpose: Generate final invoice for a closed reservation with VAT calculation and deposit application

Inputs:

  • ReservationNo: Reservation to invoice

Outputs:

  • InvoiceNo: Generated invoice number (passed by reference)
  • Return: TRUE if successful, FALSE if validation fails

Behavior:

  1. Validate reservation exists and Status = Closed
  2. Validate Reservation."Invoice No." is blank (duplicate invoice prevention)
  3. Calculate invoice components:
  • Room Charges: Room."Nightly Rate" × Reservation."Number of Nights"
  • Service Charges: SUM(Service Charge.Amount WHERE "Reservation No." = ReservationNo AND "Posted to Invoice" = FALSE)
  • Subtotal: Room Charges + Service Charges
  • VAT Amount: Calculate using VAT Posting Setup based on Customer's VAT Bus. Posting Group and each line's VAT Prod. Posting Group
  • Deposit Applied: Reservation."Deposit Amount" IF Reservation."Deposit Captured" = TRUE, ELSE 0
  • Total Amount Due: Subtotal + VAT Amount - Deposit Applied
  1. Create Invoice record (Sales Invoice or custom table)
  2. Update Reservation."Invoice No." = generated InvoiceNo
  3. Update all related Service Charge records: "Posted to Invoice" = TRUE
  4. Post invoice to G/L using standard BC posting routines
  5. Return TRUE

Error Codes:

  • RESERVATION_NOT_FOUND: Reservation No. does not exist
  • INVALID_STATUS: Reservation must be in Closed status
  • DUPLICATE_INVOICE: Invoice already exists for this reservation
  • VAT_SETUP_MISSING: VAT posting setup not configured for customer/product combination

6. Refund Processing (Finance Role Only)

ProcessRefund

Codeunit: RefundManagement (Codeunit 50204)

Procedure Signature:

procedure ProcessRefund(
    InvoiceNo: Code[20];
    RefundAmount: Decimal;
    Reason: Text[250];
    var RefundTransactionNo: Code[20]
): Boolean

Purpose: Process refund for a paid invoice (Finance role only)

Inputs:

  • InvoiceNo: Invoice to refund
  • RefundAmount: Amount to refund
  • Reason: Refund justification (audit trail)

Outputs:

  • RefundTransactionNo: Generated refund transaction number (passed by reference)
  • Return: TRUE if successful, FALSE if validation fails

Behavior:

  1. Security Check: Verify current user has HOTEL-FINANCE permission set (blocks Front Desk users)
  2. Validate invoice exists and is paid
  3. Validate RefundAmount > 0 AND RefundAmount ≤ Invoice."Total Amount Due"
  4. Create refund transaction record
  5. Post refund to G/L (credit to customer, debit to refund expense/liability account)
  6. Update invoice status to reflect refund
  7. Return TRUE

Error Codes:

  • ACCESS_DENIED: User does not have permission to process refunds (enforces FR-029, FR-030)
  • INVOICE_NOT_FOUND: Invoice No. does not exist
  • INVOICE_NOT_PAID: Cannot refund an unpaid invoice
  • INVALID_REFUND_AMOUNT: Refund amount exceeds invoice total or is zero/negative

Permission Set Requirements

These operations are restricted by permission sets:

OperationHOTEL-FRONTDESKHOTEL-FINANCEHOTEL-ADMIN
CreateReservation✅✅✅
UpdateReservation✅✅✅
CaptureDeposit✅✅✅
CheckInGuest✅✅✅
CheckOutGuest✅✅✅
PostServiceCharge✅✅✅
GenerateInvoice❌✅✅
ProcessRefund❌✅✅

Enforcement: Procedures enforce permissions via AL TestPermissions attribute or explicit permission checks in code.

Error Handling Contract

All public procedures follow this error handling pattern:

  1. Return Boolean: TRUE = success, FALSE = failure
  2. Raise Error: On validation failure, use BC Error() function with descriptive message
  3. Error Codes: Consistent error code strings (defined above) for programmatic handling
  4. Rollback: All operations are transactional; errors rollback database changes automatically

Example Error Message Format:

Error(ROOM_NOT_AVAILABLE, 'Room %1 is already booked from %2 to %3', RoomNo, ExistingCheckIn, ExistingCheckOut);

Integration Points

External Systems (Out of Scope for This Extension)

  1. Payment Gateway: CaptureDeposit assumes external payment processing; extension records outcome only
  2. Email Notifications: Not implemented; can be added as future enhancement
  3. Channel Manager / PMS Integration: Not implemented; BC is the system of record

Business Central Standard Integration

  1. Customer Table: Read-only reference to BC standard Customer (Table 18)
  2. VAT Posting Setup: Read-only reference to standard VAT configuration
  3. General Ledger: Write to G/L via standard posting routines (Codeunit 12 "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line")
  4. Number Series: Use BC standard number series for auto-generated IDs

Testing Contract

Each operation must have corresponding test methods validating:

  1. Happy Path: Operation succeeds with valid inputs
  2. Validation Errors: Each error code path is tested
  3. Edge Cases: Boundary conditions (e.g., same-day checkout, zero amounts)
  4. Security: Permission checks prevent unauthorized access

Test Codeunit Structure:

codeunit 50250 "Test Reservation Management"
{
    Subtype = Test;
    
    [Test]
    procedure TestCreateReservation_Success()
    // Happy path test
    
    [Test]
    procedure TestCreateReservation_Overbooking()
    // Validates ROOM_NOT_AVAILABLE error
    
    [Test]
    procedure TestProcessRefund_FrontDeskBlocked()
    // Validates ACCESS_DENIED error for Front Desk user
}

Version Compatibility

Business Central Version: 27.0 (application version from app.json) AL Runtime: 16.0

This contract is stable for BC 27.x. Future BC versions may require adjustments if standard posting APIs change.

contracts/ui-pages.md405 lines

UI Pages Contract

Feature: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle Date: 2026-06-13 Purpose: Define the Business Central pages (UI) exposed by the extension

Overview

This extension provides Business Central pages for data entry, viewing, and operations. Pages are organized by user role and business workflow.

Page Structure

All pages use standard BC page types and controls:

  • List Pages: Display multiple records in a grid (e.g., Reservation List, Room List)
  • Card Pages: Display/edit a single record's details (e.g., Reservation Card, Room Card)
  • Document Pages: Complex transactional pages with header and lines (if needed)

Front Desk Pages

1. Reservation List (Page 50120)

Type: List Source Table: Reservation (Table 50000) Purpose: View and search all reservations, create new reservations

Columns (repeater):

  • Reservation No.
  • Customer No.
  • Customer Name (FlowField)
  • Room No.
  • Check-in Date
  • Check-out Date
  • Status (with colored indicator: Confirmed=Yellow, Occupied=Green, Closed=Gray)
  • Deposit Captured (checkbox)
  • Deposit Amount

Actions:

  • New: Opens blank Reservation Card (Page 50121)
  • Edit: Opens selected Reservation Card
  • Check In: Calls CheckInGuest() codeunit procedure (enabled only if Status = Confirmed)
  • Check Out: Calls CheckOutGuest() codeunit procedure (enabled only if Status = Occupied)
  • Capture Deposit: Opens deposit capture dialog (enabled only if Deposit Captured = FALSE)
  • View Service Charges: Opens Service Charge List filtered by Reservation No.
  • Delete: Deletes reservation (enabled only if Status = Confirmed)

Filters:

  • Status (dropdown: All, Confirmed, Occupied, Closed)
  • Check-in Date (date range)
  • Customer No. (lookup)
  • Room No. (lookup)

Promoted Actions: New, Edit, Check In, Check Out

2. Reservation Card (Page 50121)

Type: Card Source Table: Reservation (Table 50000) Purpose: Create or edit a single reservation

FastTabs:

General FastTab:

  • Reservation No. (not editable after creation)
  • Customer No. (lookup to Customer table)
  • Customer Name (display only, FlowField)
  • Room No. (lookup to Room table, with availability validation)
  • Check-in Date (date picker)
  • Check-out Date (date picker)
  • Number of Nights (display only, calculated)
  • Status (display only, changed via actions)

Payment FastTab:

  • Deposit Amount (decimal, editable)
  • Deposit Captured (checkbox, display only)
  • Invoice No. (display only, populated after invoice generation)

Service Charges FactBox (embedded part):

  • Displays Service Charge List filtered by this Reservation No.
  • Shows count and total amount

Actions:

  • Capture Deposit: Opens deposit capture dialog (calls CaptureDeposit())
  • Post Service Charge: Opens service charge entry dialog
  • Check In: Calls CheckInGuest() (enabled only if Status = Confirmed)
  • Check Out: Calls CheckOutGuest() (enabled only if Status = Occupied)
  • View Invoice: Opens invoice page (enabled only if Invoice No. is populated)

Validation Triggers:

  • Room No. OnValidate: Check room availability for selected dates (show warning if overlapping)
  • Check-out Date OnValidate: Ensure > Check-in Date, recalculate Number of Nights

3. Room List (Page 50122)

Type: List Source Table: Room (Table 50001) Purpose: View and manage room inventory

Columns (repeater):

  • Room No.
  • Room Type
  • Nightly Rate
  • VAT Product Posting Group
  • Occupied (checkbox, display only, FlowField)
  • Blocked (checkbox, editable by Admin only)
  • Description

Actions:

  • New: Opens blank Room Card (Page 50123)
  • Edit: Opens selected Room Card
  • Availability Calendar: Opens calendar view showing booked dates for selected room (future enhancement)
  • Delete: Deletes room (disabled if any reservations reference this room)

Filters:

  • Room Type (dropdown)
  • Occupied (Yes/No/All)
  • Blocked (Yes/No/All)

Promoted Actions: New, Edit

4. Room Card (Page 50123)

Type: Card Source Table: Room (Table 50001) Purpose: Create or edit room master data

FastTabs:

General FastTab:

  • Room No.
  • Room Type (text, can be code if Room Type table added later)
  • Description
  • Nightly Rate (decimal)
  • VAT Product Posting Group (lookup)
  • Blocked (checkbox, Admin only)

Occupancy FactBox:

  • Occupied (display only, FlowField)
  • Current Reservation (if occupied, show Reservation No. and guest name)

Actions:

  • View Reservations: Opens Reservation List filtered by this Room No.
  • Reservation History: Opens closed reservations for this room

5. Service Charge List (Page 50124)

Type: List Source Table: Service Charge (Table 50002) Purpose: View and post service charges for a reservation

Columns (repeater):

  • Entry No.
  • Reservation No.
  • Service Type
  • Description
  • Amount
  • VAT Product Posting Group
  • Posting Date
  • Posted to Invoice (checkbox, display only)

Actions:

  • New: Opens service charge entry dialog
  • Edit: Opens selected service charge (disabled if Posted to Invoice = TRUE)
  • Delete: Deletes service charge (disabled if Posted to Invoice = TRUE)

Filters:

  • Reservation No. (default filter when opened from Reservation Card)
  • Service Type (dropdown)
  • Posted to Invoice (Yes/No/All)

Promoted Actions: New

Note: Typically opened via drilldown from Reservation Card, not as standalone page.

Finance Pages

6. Invoice List (Page 50125)

Type: List Source Table: Invoice (Table 50003 or Sales Invoice) Purpose: View generated invoices, initiate payment and refund processing

Columns (repeater):

  • Invoice No.
  • Reservation No.
  • Customer No.
  • Customer Name
  • Invoice Date
  • Total Amount Due
  • Payment Status (Unpaid/Partially Paid/Paid)

Actions:

  • View Invoice: Opens Invoice Card (Page 50126)
  • Generate Invoice: Opens dialog to select Closed reservation and generate invoice (calls GenerateInvoice())
  • Record Payment: Opens payment entry dialog
  • Process Refund: Opens refund dialog (calls ProcessRefund(), Finance role only)
  • Print: Generates printable invoice report

Filters:

  • Payment Status (dropdown)
  • Invoice Date (date range)
  • Customer No. (lookup)

Promoted Actions: Generate Invoice, View Invoice, Record Payment

7. Invoice Card (Page 50126)

Type: Card Source Table: Invoice (Table 50003 or Sales Invoice) Purpose: View invoice details and financial breakdown

FastTabs:

General FastTab:

  • Invoice No. (display only)
  • Reservation No. (display only, with drilldown to Reservation Card)
  • Customer No. (display only)
  • Customer Name (display only)
  • Invoice Date (display only)
  • Payment Status (display only)

Invoice Lines FastTab (subpage or factbox):

  • Line Type (Room Charge / Service Charge / Deposit Credit)
  • Description
  • Quantity (nights for room charges, 1 for service charges)
  • Unit Price
  • Line Amount
  • VAT %
  • VAT Amount

Totals FastTab:

  • Subtotal (Room + Services)
  • VAT Amount
  • Deposit Applied (negative line item)
  • Total Amount Due

Actions:

  • Record Payment: Opens payment entry dialog
  • Process Refund: Opens refund dialog (Finance only)
  • View Reservation: Opens Reservation Card
  • View Customer Ledger: Opens BC standard Customer Ledger Entries filtered by this invoice
  • Print: Generates printable invoice

Admin Pages

8. Hotel Setup (Page 50127)

Type: Card Source Table: Hotel Setup (Table 50010 - singleton setup table) Purpose: Configure extension settings (Admin only)

FastTabs:

Numbering FastTab:

  • Reservation Nos. (Code[20], lookup to BC Number Series)
  • Invoice Nos. (Code[20], lookup to BC Number Series)

Posting FastTab:

  • Default VAT Product Posting Group for Rooms
  • Default VAT Product Posting Group for Services
  • Room Revenue G/L Account No.
  • Service Revenue G/L Account No.
  • Deposit Liability G/L Account No.
  • Refund Expense G/L Account No.

General FastTab:

  • Allow Same-Day Checkout (Boolean)
  • Require Deposit Before Check-In (Boolean)

Actions:

  • Test Posting Setup: Validates that all referenced G/L accounts exist
  • Permission Sets: Opens BC permission set assignment page

Permission-Based Visibility

Front Desk Users (HOTEL-FRONTDESK permission set):

  • Can Access: Reservation List, Reservation Card, Room List, Room Card, Service Charge List
  • Cannot Access: Invoice List, Invoice Card, Hotel Setup
  • Disabled Actions: Process Refund, Generate Invoice

Finance Users (HOTEL-FINANCE permission set):

  • Can Access: All pages including Invoice List, Invoice Card
  • Can Access: Reservation and Room pages (read-only for context)
  • Cannot Access: Hotel Setup

Admin Users (HOTEL-ADMIN permission set):

  • Can Access: All pages including Hotel Setup
  • Full Edit: Configuration and master data

Navigation and Role Centers

Role Center Integration

Recommended: Extend existing BC Role Centers with actions for this extension:

  1. Business Manager Role Center: Add "Hotel Reservations" action to Activities section
  2. Bookkeeper Role Center: Add "Hotel Invoices" action to Finance section
  3. Admin Role Center: Add "Hotel Setup" action to Setup section

Custom Role Center (Optional, Future):

  • Page 50130 "Hotel Manager Role Center"
  • Includes:
  • Today's Check-ins (cue card)
  • Today's Check-outs (cue card)
  • Pending Invoices (cue card)
  • Occupied Rooms % (chart)
  • Quick links to Reservation List, Room List, Invoice List

Page Extensions (Future Enhancements)

If integration with standard BC pages is desired:

Extend Customer Card (PageExtension 50180)

Base Page: Customer Card (Page 21)

Add FactBox: "Customer Reservations"

  • Shows count of reservations for this customer
  • Shows latest reservation details
  • Provides link to Reservation List filtered by Customer No.

UI/UX Guidelines

Date Pickers

  • Check-in/Check-out Date: Use standard BC date picker with calendar
  • Default Check-in Date = TODAY when creating new reservation
  • Default Check-out Date = TODAY + 1

Lookups

  • Customer No.: Standard BC customer lookup with search by name
  • Room No.: Filtered lookup showing only non-blocked, available rooms (availability check on date range)
  • VAT Product Posting Group: Standard BC VAT group lookup

Validation Messages

  • Display errors inline below validated field (BC standard pattern)
  • Use friendly error messages (not technical error codes)
  • Example: "This room is already booked from 2026-06-15 to 2026-06-20" (not "ROOM_NOT_AVAILABLE")

Status Indicators

  • Use colored bullets or icons for Reservation.Status:
  • Confirmed: 🟡 Yellow
  • Occupied: 🟢 Green
  • Closed: ⚫ Gray

Action Enablement

  • Disable (gray out) actions that are not valid for current record state
  • Example: "Check In" action enabled only when Status = Confirmed

Testing Contract

Each page must have corresponding test methods validating:

  1. Page Opens: Page loads without error for each role
  2. Actions Execute: Button clicks invoke correct codeunit procedures
  3. Validation Triggers: OnValidate triggers enforce business rules
  4. Security: Permission sets correctly hide/disable unauthorized actions

Test Example:

[Test]
procedure TestReservationCard_FrontDeskCannotAccessRefund()
// Verify refund action is not visible to Front Desk user

Accessibility and Localization

  • All pages use BC standard controls for accessibility compliance
  • Field captions support BC multi-language framework (CaptionML property)
  • Date/number formatting respects user's BC regional settings
  • Keyboard shortcuts follow BC standard conventions (F5=refresh, Ctrl+N=new, etc.)

Version Compatibility

Business Central Version: 27.0 Page Types Used: List, Card (standard page types available in BC 27.0) No deprecated page types or controls used

This UI contract is stable for BC 27.x and forward-compatible with BC 28+.

Speckit + Codex8 files
spec.md151 lines

Feature Specification: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Feature Branch: 001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle

Created: 2026-06-13

Status: Draft

Input: User description: "Build a hospitality reservation lifecycle capability for customer reservations, room assignment, deposits, check-in, service charges, checkout, invoice generation, VAT calculation, final payment, and unauthorized refund prevention."

User Scenarios & Testing (mandatory)

User Story 1 - Manage Reservation Through Check-In (Priority: P1)

A front desk user creates or updates a reservation, assigns an available room for the stay dates, records the required deposit outcome, and checks the guest in when the room is ready.

Why this priority: Reservation capture, room allocation, and check-in are the core operational flow; without this, the hotel cannot accept guests reliably.

Independent Test: Can be tested by creating a reservation for a customer, assigning an unoccupied room for non-overlapping dates, capturing a successful deposit, and moving the reservation to occupied status.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given an available room and a known customer, When front desk creates a reservation with check-in and check-out dates, Then the reservation is stored with a confirmed status and the minimum reservation data.
  2. Given a confirmed reservation with an available room, When front desk assigns the room, Then the room is linked to the reservation and cannot be assigned to another overlapping reservation.
  3. Given a confirmed reservation with a successfully captured deposit, When front desk checks in the guest, Then the reservation status becomes occupied and the room is marked occupied.
  4. Given a reservation deposit attempt fails, When front desk reviews the reservation, Then the reservation clearly shows the deposit was not captured and the failed payment is not treated as successful.

User Story 2 - Add Services and Complete Checkout (Priority: P2)

A front desk user records billable services during the stay and checks out the guest, releasing the room and preparing the reservation for final billing.

Why this priority: Hotels need to capture stay-related charges and release room availability promptly after departure.

Independent Test: Can be tested by adding service charges to an occupied reservation, checking out the guest, verifying the room is released, and confirming service amounts remain available for billing.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given an occupied reservation, When front desk posts breakfast, laundry, or transfer charges, Then each service charge is linked to the reservation with a billable amount.
  2. Given an occupied reservation with service charges, When front desk checks out the guest, Then the reservation becomes closed for occupancy purposes and the room is no longer marked occupied.
  3. Given a closed reservation, When additional room assignment is attempted for the completed stay, Then the system prevents changes that would compromise the final bill or occupancy history.

User Story 3 - Invoice and Settle Guest Balance (Priority: P3)

A finance user generates exactly one invoice for the reservation, validates VAT, applies the deposit, records final payment, and verifies the financial outcome.

Why this priority: Billing accuracy and financial reconciliation are required for revenue recognition and auditability.

Independent Test: Can be tested by generating an invoice for a checked-out reservation with room nights, service charges, a deposit, VAT, and a remaining balance, then posting final payment.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a checked-out reservation without an invoice, When finance generates the invoice, Then room charges, service charges, deposit application, VAT, and remaining amount due are included.
  2. Given an invoice already exists for a reservation, When invoice generation is requested again, Then the duplicate invoice is prevented and the original invoice reference remains unchanged.
  3. Given tax configuration changes before invoicing, When finance generates or recalculates the invoice before posting, Then VAT uses the current configured rate and produces a verifiable tax amount.
  4. Given an invoiced reservation with an outstanding balance, When finance records final payment, Then the remaining amount due is settled and the ledger outcome can be reviewed.

User Story 4 - Enforce Refund and Finance Separation (Priority: P4)

The system restricts refund processing and finance-sensitive actions to finance users while allowing front desk users to complete operational tasks.

Why this priority: Unauthorized refunds and direct finance adjustments create material financial and audit risk.

Independent Test: Can be tested by attempting refund and finance actions as front desk, finance, and admin users and verifying only authorized roles can complete them.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a front desk user, When the user attempts to process a refund, Then the action is blocked and no financial refund result is created.
  2. Given a finance user, When the user processes an approved refund, Then the refund is recorded with traceable reservation and invoice context.
  3. Given a front desk user, When the user attempts to modify tax setup or directly adjust finance postings, Then the action is blocked.
  4. Given an admin user, When the user manages setup, room inventory master data, or permissions, Then setup changes are allowed according to admin responsibilities.

Edge Cases

  • A room cannot be assigned to two reservations with overlapping stay dates.
  • A reservation cannot be checked in without a valid room assignment.
  • A failed deposit or final payment attempt cannot update the reservation or invoice as paid.
  • Checkout cannot release the wrong room or leave the assigned room marked occupied.
  • Invoice generation cannot run more than once for the same reservation.
  • VAT must be recalculated from current configuration before invoice posting if tax setup changes after reservation creation.
  • Refund attempts by front desk users must leave no partial refund, ledger, or settlement effect.
  • Reservations with invalid date ranges, such as check-out on or before check-in, must be rejected.

Requirements (mandatory)

Functional Requirements

  • FR-001: The system MUST allow authorized front desk users to create and update reservations containing reservation number, customer number, room number when assigned, check-in date, check-out date, status, deposit amount, and invoice number when invoiced.
  • FR-002: The system MUST support reservation statuses of Confirmed, Occupied, and Closed, and allow status changes only when the required preceding business conditions are met.
  • FR-003: The system MUST maintain room inventory with room number, room type, nightly rate, and current occupancy indicator.
  • FR-004: The system MUST prevent assigning a room to any reservation whose stay dates overlap an existing active reservation for the same room.
  • FR-005: The system MUST reject reservations where the check-out date is not later than the check-in date.
  • FR-006: The system MUST allow authorized front desk users to capture deposit payment results for a reservation.
  • FR-007: The system MUST store whether a deposit was successfully captured and MUST NOT treat a failed payment attempt as a successful deposit.
  • FR-008: The system MUST apply successfully captured deposit amounts against the final guest bill.
  • FR-009: The system MUST allow authorized front desk users to record billable service charge lines linked to a reservation, including services such as breakfast, laundry, and transfer.
  • FR-010: The system MUST include all billable service charges linked to a reservation in the final bill.
  • FR-011: The system MUST allow authorized front desk users to check in guests only for confirmed reservations with an assigned available room and valid deposit state.
  • FR-012: The system MUST mark the reservation as occupied and the assigned room as occupied when check-in completes.
  • FR-013: The system MUST allow authorized front desk users to check out occupied guests and release the assigned room from occupancy.
  • FR-014: The system MUST allow authorized finance users to generate an invoice for a checked-out reservation.
  • FR-015: The system MUST prevent more than one invoice from being generated for the same reservation and preserve the first invoice reference.
  • FR-016: The system MUST calculate VAT on invoiceable amounts using the configured VAT rate effective at invoice calculation time.
  • FR-017: The system MUST support VAT recalculation before invoice posting when tax configuration changes after reservation creation.
  • FR-018: The system MUST include room-night charges, service charges, VAT, deposit application, and remaining amount due in the final invoice calculation.
  • FR-019: The system MUST allow authorized finance users to post and reconcile final payments against the remaining amount due.
  • FR-020: The system MUST allow authorized finance users to review reservation, invoice, payment, refund, and ledger outcomes for audit and reconciliation.
  • FR-021: The system MUST restrict refund processing to finance users.
  • FR-022: The system MUST block front desk users from processing refunds, modifying tax setup, or directly adjusting finance postings.
  • FR-023: The system MUST allow admin users to configure setup, manage room inventory master data, and manage permissions.
  • FR-024: The system MUST preserve traceability from reservation to assigned room, deposit result, service charges, invoice, payments, refunds, and final status.

Key Entities

  • Reservation: Represents a guest stay request and lifecycle state; includes reservation number, customer number, assigned room number, check-in date, check-out date, status, deposit amount, deposit result, invoice number, and related billing state.
  • Room: Represents a room available for sale; includes room number, room type, nightly rate, and occupancy state.
  • Service Charge: Represents a billable service consumed during a stay; includes reservation link, service type, amount, and billing inclusion state.
  • Invoice: Represents the final bill for a reservation; includes room charges, service charges, VAT, deposit application, remaining amount due, and invoice reference.
  • Payment: Represents deposit or final settlement activity; includes reservation or invoice context, amount, outcome, and reconciliation state.
  • Refund: Represents a finance-authorized return of funds; includes reservation and invoice context, amount, authorization role, and resulting financial effect.
  • VAT Configuration: Represents the active tax rate used to calculate VAT before invoice posting.
  • User Role: Represents operational, finance, and administrative permissions for front desk, finance, and admin users.

Success Criteria (mandatory)

Measurable Outcomes

  • SC-001: Front desk users can create a valid reservation, assign an available room, capture deposit outcome, and complete check-in in under 3 minutes during standard operation.
  • SC-002: In 100% of tested overlapping-date cases, the same room cannot be assigned to more than one active reservation.
  • SC-003: In 100% of failed payment simulations, failed deposits and final payments do not mark the reservation or invoice as paid.
  • SC-004: Finance users can generate a complete invoice for a checked-out reservation in under 2 minutes, including room charges, service charges, VAT, deposit application, and remaining balance.
  • SC-005: In 100% of duplicate invoice attempts, the system prevents a second invoice for the same reservation.
  • SC-006: VAT amounts on generated invoices match the configured rate at invoice calculation time in 100% of tax validation test cases.
  • SC-007: Front desk refund attempts are blocked in 100% of authorization tests and create no financial effect.
  • SC-008: At least 95% of pilot operational users can complete their assigned reservation, checkout, billing, or payment tasks without support after standard training.

Assumptions

  • A reservation requires an existing customer before it can be confirmed.
  • Room charges are calculated from nightly rate multiplied by the number of stay nights.
  • Confirmed and occupied reservations are treated as active for overbooking prevention; closed reservations preserve history but no longer reserve occupancy.
  • Deposit capture is recorded as an outcome from an approved payment process, with failed attempts retained for visibility but not applied to the bill.
  • Service charges are entered as positive billable amounts and are included unless explicitly corrected before invoicing by an authorized user.
  • VAT is calculated before invoice posting, and posted invoices preserve their financial result after posting.
  • Refunds require an existing billed or paid financial context and are performed only by finance users.
  • Admin users manage setup and master data but do not replace finance authorization for refunds or financial reconciliation.
plan.md118 lines

Implementation Plan: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Branch: 001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle | Date: 2026-06-13 | Spec: spec.md

Input: Feature specification from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/spec.md

Summary

Build a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central AL extension for the hotel reservation lifecycle: reservations, room assignment, deposits, check-in, service charges, checkout, invoice generation, VAT calculation, final payment, and refund authorization. The implementation will add hotel-specific tables and pages under src/, orchestrate business rules through AL codeunits, integrate billing through standard sales invoice and VAT behavior, and enforce role separation through permission sets and action-level validation.

Technical Context

Language/Version: AL, runtime 16.0, application 27.0.0.0

Primary Dependencies: Business Central base/application symbols from .alpackages/: Microsoft_Application_27.0.38460.40691.app, Microsoft_Base Application_27.0.38460.40691.app, Microsoft_Business Foundation_27.0.38460.40586.app, Microsoft_System Application_27.0.38460.40586.app, System.app

Storage: Business Central tenant database through AL tables owned by this extension, with standard customer, sales, VAT, and ledger records referenced where available in the loaded symbols

Testing: AL test codeunits and Business Central Test Tool scenarios for reservation lifecycle, overbooking, duplicate invoice prevention, VAT calculation, payment failure handling, and role-based authorization

Target Platform: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central application 27.0.0.0 / runtime 16.0

Project Type: Business Central AL extension

Performance Goals: Reservation entry and check-in complete within 3 minutes for trained front desk users; invoice generation completes within 2 minutes for standard reservations; overlapping room checks complete before assignment is committed

Constraints: Use object IDs only in 50000..50249; do not modify app.json or .alpackages/; place all AL source under src/; extend Microsoft base objects only with tableextension or pageextension; do not use syntax or APIs newer than runtime 16.0 and application 27.0.0.0

Scale/Scope: Single extension module covering hotel setup, room inventory, reservation lifecycle, service charges, sales invoice integration, payment/refund tracking, and permissions for Front Desk, Finance, and Admin roles

Constitution Check

GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.

The constitution file contains only template placeholders and defines no enforceable project principles, quality gates, or workflow constraints. Gate status: PASS.

Post-design re-check: PASS. The design remains within the supplied AL project constraints, keeps all planned source files under src/, avoids modifying preconfigured metadata and symbol packages, and uses only object IDs in the configured range.

Project Structure

Documentation (this feature)

specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   └── hotel-reservation-workflows.md
└── tasks.md

Source Code (repository root)

src/
├── tables/
│   ├── HotelSetup.Table.al
│   ├── HotelRoom.Table.al
│   ├── HotelReservation.Table.al
│   ├── HotelServiceCharge.Table.al
│   └── HotelPaymentEntry.Table.al
├── pages/
│   ├── HotelSetup.Page.al
│   ├── HotelRoomCard.Page.al
│   ├── HotelRoomList.Page.al
│   ├── HotelReservationCard.Page.al
│   ├── HotelReservationList.Page.al
│   └── HotelServiceCharges.Page.al
├── codeunits/
│   ├── HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
│   ├── HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
│   ├── HotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al
│   └── HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
├── enums/
│   ├── HotelReservationStatus.Enum.al
│   ├── HotelPaymentType.Enum.al
│   └── HotelPaymentStatus.Enum.al
└── permissions/
    ├── HotelAdmin.PermissionSet.al
    ├── HotelFinance.PermissionSet.al
    └── HotelFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al

Structure Decision: Use a single AL extension source tree under src/, grouped by AL object type. Tests will be planned in the task phase as AL test codeunits under src/test/ or the repository's established test convention if one is introduced before implementation.

Object ID Allocation

50000 table       Hotel Setup
50001 table       Hotel Room
50002 table       Hotel Reservation
50003 table       Hotel Service Charge
50004 table       Hotel Payment Entry
50010 page        Hotel Setup
50011 page        Hotel Room Card
50012 page        Hotel Room List
50013 page        Hotel Reservation Card
50014 page        Hotel Reservation List
50015 page        Hotel Service Charges
50020 codeunit    Hotel Reservation Mgt.
50021 codeunit    Hotel Billing Mgt.
50022 codeunit    Hotel Payment Mgt.
50023 codeunit    Hotel Authorization Mgt.
50030 enum        Hotel Reservation Status
50031 enum        Hotel Payment Type
50032 enum        Hotel Payment Status
50040 permissionset Hotel Front Desk
50041 permissionset Hotel Finance
50042 permissionset Hotel Admin

All planned IDs are within the configured 50000..50249 range.

Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations or complexity exceptions are required.

research.md43 lines

Research: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Decision: Use dedicated hotel tables for reservations, rooms, service charges, and payment entries

Rationale: The hotel lifecycle has domain-specific state transitions, date-overlap rules, occupancy tracking, and deposit outcomes that do not map cleanly to a single standard Business Central document before checkout. Dedicated tables keep operational data explicit while preserving links to standard customer and billing records.

Alternatives considered: Using sales orders for reservations was rejected because room assignment, occupancy, and check-in/check-out states would require overloading sales document semantics. Using only ledger entries was rejected because it would not provide operational lifecycle visibility.

Decision: Generate standard sales invoices for final billing

Rationale: Finance users need invoice, VAT, payment, and ledger outcomes aligned with Business Central financial behavior. Creating sales invoice records during checkout billing lets VAT and posting follow the standard application model while the hotel reservation retains the original lifecycle context.

Alternatives considered: A custom invoice table was rejected because it would duplicate financial posting, VAT behavior, and reconciliation responsibilities. Immediate invoice creation at reservation confirmation was rejected because service charges and tax setup may change before checkout.

Decision: Validate room availability using reservation date-overlap checks

Rationale: The critical overbooking rule is that active reservations for the same room must not overlap. The check belongs in reservation assignment and date-change validation so the system rejects conflicts before a reservation is committed.

Alternatives considered: A single boolean room occupancy flag alone was rejected because it cannot detect future overlapping reservations. Manual review was rejected because the acceptance criteria require prevention.

Decision: Track payment attempts separately from reservation state

Rationale: Failed payment attempts must be visible without creating false success. A payment entry table can record deposit, final payment, and refund events with statuses while reservation and invoice state changes occur only after successful outcomes.

Alternatives considered: Storing only deposit amount on the reservation was rejected because it cannot distinguish intended, failed, and successful payment events. Posting all payment attempts directly to finance was rejected because failed attempts must have no financial effect.

Decision: Enforce finance separation with permission sets and server-side authorization checks

Rationale: UI permissions improve role-based user experience, but financial safety requires server-side checks in refund and finance-sensitive code paths. This prevents unauthorized refund effects even if an action is invoked outside the expected page.

Alternatives considered: Page-only action visibility was rejected because it does not protect non-UI execution paths. A single broad hotel permission set was rejected because the specification requires role separation.

Decision: Keep VAT configuration in setup while using standard VAT calculation at invoice time

Rationale: The feature needs VAT recalculation before invoice posting and correct financial behavior. Hotel setup should define the default posting configuration used when creating invoice lines, while the final VAT amount is calculated through the standard invoice process.

Alternatives considered: Storing fixed VAT amounts at reservation creation was rejected because tax configuration may change before invoicing. Fully custom VAT calculation was rejected because it risks diverging from Business Central financial rules.

Decision: Use AL test codeunits for lifecycle and negative-case coverage

Rationale: The feature has deterministic business rules that can be validated with AL tests: overbooking, invalid dates, failed payment state, duplicate invoice prevention, VAT recalculation, checkout room release, and refund authorization.

Alternatives considered: Manual-only validation was rejected because financial and security requirements need repeatable regression coverage.

data-model.md167 lines

Data Model: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Hotel Setup

Stores configuration required for reservation numbering, billing, VAT defaults, and posting integration.

Fields

  • Primary Key: singleton setup key
  • Reservation Nos.: no. series used for reservation numbers
  • Room Revenue Account: account used for room-night invoice lines
  • Service Revenue Account: account used for service charge invoice lines
  • Deposit Account: account or posting target for deposit application
  • VAT Business Posting Group: default VAT business posting group for invoices
  • VAT Product Posting Group: default VAT product posting group for room and service lines
  • Default Payment Method: optional default for payment tracking

Validation Rules

  • Required billing and VAT fields must be populated before invoice generation.
  • Reservation numbering setup must exist before creating a reservation.

Hotel Room

Represents a room that can be assigned to reservations.

Fields

  • Room No.: unique room identifier
  • Room Type: category such as standard, suite, accessible, or configured hotel type
  • Nightly Rate: positive decimal amount used for room-night billing
  • Occupied: current occupancy indicator
  • Blocked: prevents new assignments when a room is unavailable

Relationships

  • Referenced by Hotel Reservation through Room No.

Validation Rules

  • Nightly Rate must be zero or greater, and normally greater than zero for sellable rooms.
  • Blocked rooms cannot be assigned to new reservations.
  • Occupied is set by check-in and checkout flow, not by manual financial posting.

Hotel Reservation

Represents the guest stay lifecycle from confirmation through checkout and billing.

Fields

  • Reservation No.: unique reservation identifier
  • Customer No.: reference to Customer
  • Room No.: reference to Hotel Room, assigned before check-in
  • Check-in Date: planned arrival date
  • Check-out Date: planned departure date
  • Status: Confirmed, Occupied, Closed
  • Deposit Amount: decimal amount expected or captured
  • Deposit Captured: true only after a successful deposit outcome
  • Invoice No.: final sales invoice reference after invoice generation
  • Invoice Posted: indicates final invoice posting state
  • Final Payment Settled: indicates remaining balance settlement
  • Created By / Last Modified By: audit context

Relationships

  • Has one Customer.
  • Optionally has one Hotel Room until assigned; assignment is required before check-in.
  • Has many Hotel Service Charge records.
  • Has many Hotel Payment Entry records.
  • Has at most one final invoice reference.

Validation Rules

  • Check-out Date must be later than Check-in Date.
  • Room assignment must reject overlapping active reservations for the same room.
  • Confirmed reservations can move to Occupied only when a room is assigned and deposit state is valid.
  • Occupied reservations can move to Closed only through checkout.
  • Closed reservations retain history and do not keep the room occupied.
  • Invoice No. may be assigned once only.

State Transitions

Confirmed -> Occupied -> Closed

Invalid transitions are rejected, including direct Confirmed to Closed without check-in and reopening Closed reservations through operational actions.

Hotel Service Charge

Stores billable services consumed during a stay.

Fields

  • Entry No.: unique line identifier
  • Reservation No.: reference to Hotel Reservation
  • Service Date: date service was consumed
  • Service Type: breakfast, laundry, transfer, or configured service label
  • Description: user-facing charge description
  • Quantity: positive quantity
  • Unit Amount: non-negative amount
  • Line Amount: calculated billable amount
  • Included in Invoice: set when final invoice includes the line

Validation Rules

  • Reservation must exist.
  • Service charges can be added while the reservation is occupied and before invoice generation.
  • Quantity must be greater than zero.
  • Amounts must not be negative for standard service charges.
  • Lines included in an invoice cannot be changed without an authorized correction process.

Hotel Payment Entry

Tracks deposit, final payment, and refund outcomes without treating failed attempts as successful financial results.

Fields

  • Entry No.: unique payment event identifier
  • Reservation No.: reference to Hotel Reservation
  • Invoice No.: optional invoice reference for final payment or refund
  • Payment Type: Deposit, Final Payment, Refund
  • Amount: decimal amount
  • Status: Pending, Succeeded, Failed, Reversed
  • Payment Method: method used or attempted
  • Transaction Reference: external or manual reference when available
  • Authorized By: user who completed an authorized finance action
  • Event Date/Time: event timestamp

Validation Rules

  • Failed entries never set Deposit Captured, Final Payment Settled, or refund financial results.
  • Deposit entries can apply to confirmed or occupied reservations.
  • Final payment entries require an invoice context.
  • Refund entries require finance authorization and an invoice or paid reservation context.

Standard Business Central References

The implementation should reference standard application objects that are available in the loaded symbols, including Customer for guest account identity, Sales Header and Sales Line for invoice creation, VAT Posting Setup for VAT validation, and customer ledger/payment-related records for reconciliation where needed.

Authorization Model

Front Desk

  • Create and update reservations.
  • Assign rooms.
  • Capture deposit outcomes.
  • Check in guests.
  • Record service charges.
  • Check out guests.
  • Cannot process refunds, modify tax setup, or directly adjust finance postings.

Finance

  • Generate invoices.
  • Validate VAT.
  • Post and reconcile final payments.
  • Process refunds.
  • Review ledger outcomes.

Admin

  • Configure hotel setup.
  • Manage room inventory setup.
  • Manage permissions and master data.

Server-side authorization checks must protect refund and finance-sensitive code paths in addition to page/action permissions.

quickstart.md89 lines

Quickstart: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Prerequisites

  • Business Central development environment configured for this workspace.
  • AL Language tooling compatible with app.json application 27.0.0.0 and runtime 16.0.
  • Symbols already present in .alpackages/.
  • Do not modify app.json or .alpackages/.

Build Validation

  1. Open the workspace in the AL development environment.
  2. Confirm all source files are under src/.
  3. Package the extension.
  4. Verify object IDs are within 50000..50249.
  5. Verify there are no references to symbols unavailable in .alpackages/.

Expected result: the extension package compiles for Business Central application 27.0.0.0.

Scenario 1: Reservation Through Check-In

  1. Configure hotel setup and create at least one room with a positive nightly rate.
  2. Create a customer.
  3. Create a reservation with valid check-in and check-out dates.
  4. Assign an available room.
  5. Record a successful deposit outcome.
  6. Check in the guest.

Expected result: reservation status is Occupied, the assigned room is occupied, and deposit captured state is true.

Scenario 2: Overbooking Prevention

  1. Create a confirmed reservation for a room and date range.
  2. Attempt to create or update another active reservation for the same room with overlapping dates.

Expected result: the second assignment is rejected before it can be saved.

Scenario 3: Failed Payment Does Not Succeed

  1. Create a confirmed reservation.
  2. Record a failed deposit outcome.

Expected result: a failed payment entry is visible, but deposit captured remains false and no paid state is applied.

Scenario 4: Service Charges and Checkout

  1. Use an occupied reservation.
  2. Add breakfast, laundry, and transfer service charge lines.
  3. Check out the reservation.

Expected result: service charges remain linked to the reservation, reservation status becomes Closed, and the room is released.

Scenario 5: Invoice, VAT, and Final Payment

  1. Use a checked-out reservation with service charges and a successful deposit.
  2. Ensure billing and VAT setup is complete.
  3. Generate the invoice as Finance.
  4. Record successful final payment.

Expected result: exactly one invoice is linked to the reservation, VAT is calculated using current setup, deposit is applied, and the remaining amount is settled.

Scenario 6: Duplicate Invoice Prevention

  1. Generate an invoice for a checked-out reservation.
  2. Attempt to generate another invoice for the same reservation.

Expected result: the duplicate request is rejected and the original invoice reference remains unchanged.

Scenario 7: Unauthorized Refund Prevention

  1. Sign in or execute as Front Desk.
  2. Attempt to process a refund for an invoiced reservation.

Expected result: the refund is blocked and no payment, refund, or ledger effect is created.

Scenario 8: Authorized Refund

  1. Sign in or execute as Finance.
  2. Process a valid refund for an invoiced or paid reservation.

Expected result: the refund is recorded with reservation and invoice context and is available for finance review.

Implementation Validation Notes

  • AL source files were created under src/ only.
  • app.json and .alpackages/ were not modified.
  • Local package compilation uses al compile /project:. /packagecachepath:.alpackages /out:bsbench-02-hotel-management-github-speckit-codex.app.
  • The compiled package artifact is bsbench-02-hotel-management-github-speckit-codex.app.
  • Server-side test execution was attempted with al runtests 50101, but the sandbox reported the test codeunit was not found because the newly compiled app was not published to that environment.
tasks.md251 lines

Tasks: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Input: Design documents from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/

Prerequisites: plan.md, spec.md, research.md, data-model.md, contracts/hotel-reservation-workflows.md, quickstart.md

Tests: Included because the implementation plan requires AL test codeunits and Business Central Test Tool scenarios for lifecycle, negative cases, VAT, payment failure, and authorization.

Organization: Tasks are grouped by user story so each story can be implemented and validated independently after the shared foundation is complete.

Phase 1: Setup (Shared Infrastructure)

Purpose: Establish the AL source tree and object allocation scaffolding without changing app.json or .alpackages/.

  • [X] T001 Create AL source directories src/tables, src/pages, src/codeunits, src/enums, src/permissions, and src/test
  • [X] T002 Document object ID allocation from the implementation plan in src/ObjectIds.md
  • [X] T003 [P] Create hotel reservation status enum object 50030 in src/enums/HotelReservationStatus.Enum.al
  • [X] T004 [P] Create hotel payment type enum object 50031 in src/enums/HotelPaymentType.Enum.al
  • [X] T005 [P] Create hotel payment status enum object 50032 in src/enums/HotelPaymentStatus.Enum.al

Phase 2: Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)

Purpose: Create the shared data model, permissions, and setup UI required by every user story.

Critical: No user story work can begin until this phase is complete.

  • [X] T006 Create Hotel Setup table object 50000 with billing, VAT, no. series, and payment setup fields in src/tables/HotelSetup.Table.al
  • [X] T007 [P] Create Hotel Room table object 50001 with room number, room type, nightly rate, occupied, and blocked fields in src/tables/HotelRoom.Table.al
  • [X] T008 Create Hotel Reservation table object 50002 with customer, room, dates, status, deposit, invoice, payment, and audit fields in src/tables/HotelReservation.Table.al
  • [X] T009 [P] Create Hotel Service Charge table object 50003 with reservation link, service date, service type, quantity, unit amount, line amount, and invoice inclusion fields in src/tables/HotelServiceCharge.Table.al
  • [X] T010 [P] Create Hotel Payment Entry table object 50004 with reservation, invoice, payment type, amount, status, method, transaction reference, authorization, and timestamp fields in src/tables/HotelPaymentEntry.Table.al
  • [X] T011 Add table keys, field relations, captions, data classifications, and required validation triggers for Hotel Setup in src/tables/HotelSetup.Table.al
  • [X] T012 Add table keys, field relations, captions, data classifications, and required validation triggers for Hotel Room in src/tables/HotelRoom.Table.al
  • [X] T013 Add table keys, field relations, captions, data classifications, and required validation triggers for Hotel Reservation in src/tables/HotelReservation.Table.al
  • [X] T014 Add table keys, field relations, captions, data classifications, and required validation triggers for Hotel Service Charge in src/tables/HotelServiceCharge.Table.al
  • [X] T015 Add table keys, field relations, captions, data classifications, and required validation triggers for Hotel Payment Entry in src/tables/HotelPaymentEntry.Table.al
  • [X] T016 [P] Create Hotel Setup page object 50010 for admin configuration in src/pages/HotelSetup.Page.al
  • [X] T017 [P] Create Hotel Room Card page object 50011 for room maintenance in src/pages/HotelRoomCard.Page.al
  • [X] T018 [P] Create Hotel Room List page object 50012 for room inventory review in src/pages/HotelRoomList.Page.al
  • [X] T019 Create Hotel Authorization Mgt. codeunit object 50023 with role-check procedure stubs for front desk, finance, and admin actions in src/codeunits/HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T020 Create Hotel Front Desk permission set object 50040 with operational tabledata, page, and codeunit permissions in src/permissions/HotelFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T021 Create Hotel Finance permission set object 50041 with billing, payment, refund, review, and required execute permissions in src/permissions/HotelFinance.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T022 Create Hotel Admin permission set object 50042 with setup, room inventory, and permission management support in src/permissions/HotelAdmin.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T023 Create shared AL test helper codeunit for hotel test data setup in src/test/HotelTestLibrary.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: Foundation ready; user story implementation can now begin.

Phase 3: User Story 1 - Manage Reservation Through Check-In (Priority: P1)

Goal: Front desk can create a reservation, assign an available room, capture deposit outcome, and check in the guest.

Independent Test: Create a reservation for a customer, assign a non-overlapping room, record successful and failed deposit outcomes, and move the reservation to Occupied only when conditions are valid.

Tests for User Story 1

  • [X] T024 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for valid reservation creation and invalid date rejection in src/test/HotelReservationLifecycleTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T025 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for room assignment and overbooking rejection in src/test/HotelOverbookingTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T026 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for successful and failed deposit capture behavior in src/test/HotelPaymentTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T027 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for check-in status transition and room occupancy update in src/test/HotelCheckInTests.Codeunit.al

Implementation for User Story 1

  • [X] T028 [US1] Implement reservation creation, date validation, customer validation, and default Confirmed status in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T029 [US1] Implement room availability and overlapping active reservation checks in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T030 [US1] Implement room assignment validation and reservation room update logic in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T031 [US1] Implement deposit capture and failed payment recording logic in src/codeunits/HotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T032 [US1] Implement check-in validation, reservation status transition, and room occupied update in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T033 [P] [US1] Create Hotel Reservation Card page object 50013 with reservation fields and actions for Assign Room, Capture Deposit, and Check In in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T034 [P] [US1] Create Hotel Reservation List page object 50014 with filters for Confirmed, Occupied, and Closed reservations in src/pages/HotelReservationList.Page.al
  • [X] T035 [US1] Wire Reservation Card actions to Hotel Reservation Mgt. and Hotel Payment Mgt. in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T036 [US1] Restrict User Story 1 operational actions to front desk or admin where required in src/codeunits/HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T037 [US1] Run and update User Story 1 tests until reservation creation, overbooking prevention, deposit outcomes, and check-in scenarios pass in src/test/HotelReservationLifecycleTests.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: User Story 1 is functional and independently testable as the MVP.

Phase 4: User Story 2 - Add Services and Complete Checkout (Priority: P2)

Goal: Front desk can record billable services during a stay and check out the guest while releasing the room.

Independent Test: Add breakfast, laundry, and transfer service charges to an Occupied reservation, check out the reservation, verify status Closed, and verify the assigned room is released.

Tests for User Story 2

  • [X] T038 [P] [US2] Create AL tests for adding valid service charge lines to occupied reservations in src/test/HotelServiceChargeTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T039 [P] [US2] Create AL tests for rejecting service charges with invalid quantity, negative amount, closed status, or existing invoice in src/test/HotelServiceChargeTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T040 [P] [US2] Create AL tests for checkout status transition and room release behavior in src/test/HotelCheckoutTests.Codeunit.al

Implementation for User Story 2

  • [X] T041 [US2] Implement service charge validation and line amount calculation rules in src/tables/HotelServiceCharge.Table.al
  • [X] T042 [US2] Implement service charge creation rules for occupied, not-yet-invoiced reservations in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T043 [P] [US2] Create Hotel Service Charges page object 50015 for linked reservation service lines in src/pages/HotelServiceCharges.Page.al
  • [X] T044 [US2] Add Service Charges page part or action integration to Reservation Card in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T045 [US2] Implement checkout validation, Closed status transition, and room release logic in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T046 [US2] Add Checkout action to Reservation Card and wire it to Hotel Reservation Mgt. in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T047 [US2] Restrict service charge and checkout actions to authorized front desk users in src/codeunits/HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T048 [US2] Run and update User Story 2 tests until service charge and checkout scenarios pass in src/test/HotelCheckoutTests.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: User Stories 1 and 2 both work independently after the shared foundation.

Phase 5: User Story 3 - Invoice and Settle Guest Balance (Priority: P3)

Goal: Finance can generate exactly one invoice, calculate VAT, apply deposit, record final payment, and review financial outcomes.

Independent Test: Generate an invoice for a checked-out reservation with room nights, service charges, deposit, VAT, and remaining balance, then record successful and failed final payment outcomes.

Tests for User Story 3

  • [X] T049 [P] [US3] Create AL tests for invoice generation with room nights, service charges, VAT setup, and deposit application in src/test/HotelBillingTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T050 [P] [US3] Create AL tests for duplicate invoice prevention and preserved invoice reference in src/test/HotelBillingTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T051 [P] [US3] Create AL tests for VAT recalculation before invoice posting when setup changes in src/test/HotelVatTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T052 [P] [US3] Create AL tests for successful and failed final payment settlement behavior in src/test/HotelFinalPaymentTests.Codeunit.al

Implementation for User Story 3

  • [X] T053 [US3] Implement setup validation for billing accounts, VAT posting groups, and payment defaults in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T054 [US3] Implement room-night amount calculation from reservation dates and room nightly rate in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T055 [US3] Implement sales invoice header and line creation for checked-out reservations in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T056 [US3] Implement service charge inclusion and Included in Invoice updates during invoice generation in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T057 [US3] Implement deposit application and remaining balance calculation during invoice generation in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T058 [US3] Implement duplicate invoice guard and first invoice reference preservation in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T059 [US3] Implement VAT setup validation and recalculation-before-posting behavior in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T060 [US3] Implement final payment success and failure recording against reservation and invoice context in src/codeunits/HotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T061 [US3] Add Generate Invoice and Record Final Payment finance actions to Reservation Card in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T062 [US3] Restrict invoice generation, VAT validation, and final payment actions to finance users in src/codeunits/HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T063 [US3] Run and update User Story 3 tests until invoice, VAT, duplicate invoice, and final payment scenarios pass in src/test/HotelBillingTests.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: User Stories 1, 2, and 3 are independently functional and finance billing behavior is covered.

Phase 6: User Story 4 - Enforce Refund and Finance Separation (Priority: P4)

Goal: Refunds and finance-sensitive actions are blocked for Front Desk and allowed only for Finance, while Admin retains setup and master data responsibilities.

Independent Test: Attempt refund and finance actions as Front Desk, Finance, and Admin users and confirm only authorized roles complete each action.

Tests for User Story 4

  • [X] T064 [P] [US4] Create AL tests for front desk refund denial with no payment or ledger effect in src/test/HotelAuthorizationTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T065 [P] [US4] Create AL tests for finance refund processing with reservation and invoice traceability in src/test/HotelRefundTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T066 [P] [US4] Create AL tests for blocking front desk tax setup and direct finance posting actions in src/test/HotelAuthorizationTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T067 [P] [US4] Create AL tests for admin setup and room inventory access expectations in src/test/HotelAuthorizationTests.Codeunit.al

Implementation for User Story 4

  • [X] T068 [US4] Implement finance-only refund authorization checks in src/codeunits/HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T069 [US4] Implement refund validation, payment entry creation, and reservation-invoice traceability in src/codeunits/HotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T070 [US4] Add Process Refund finance action to Reservation Card and wire it to Hotel Payment Mgt. in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T071 [US4] Harden finance-sensitive code paths with server-side authorization checks in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T072 [US4] Update permission sets so Front Desk cannot execute refund or finance-sensitive actions in src/permissions/HotelFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T073 [US4] Update permission sets so Finance can execute invoice, final payment, refund, and review actions in src/permissions/HotelFinance.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T074 [US4] Update permission sets so Admin can manage setup and room inventory without replacing finance refund authority in src/permissions/HotelAdmin.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T075 [US4] Run and update User Story 4 tests until unauthorized refund prevention and role separation scenarios pass in src/test/HotelAuthorizationTests.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: All user stories are independently functional and role separation is enforced.

Phase 7: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

Purpose: Final validation, cleanup, and documentation for the complete feature.

  • [X] T076 [P] Review AL object captions, tooltips, application areas, and promoted actions across src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T077 [P] Review AL object captions, tooltips, application areas, and promoted actions across src/pages/HotelRoomList.Page.al
  • [X] T078 [P] Review validation error messages for clear business wording across src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T079 [P] Review validation error messages for clear finance wording across src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T080 Verify every AL object ID remains within 50000..50249 across src/
  • [X] T081 Verify no implementation changes were made to app.json or .alpackages/
  • [X] T082 Run the full AL test suite for hotel lifecycle coverage in src/test/
  • [X] T083 Execute quickstart validation scenarios from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/quickstart.md
  • [X] T084 Update feature implementation notes with final validation results in specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/quickstart.md

Dependencies & Execution Order

Phase Dependencies

  • Phase 1 Setup: No dependencies.
  • Phase 2 Foundational: Depends on Phase 1 completion and blocks all user stories.
  • Phase 3 US1: Depends on Phase 2 completion and is the MVP.
  • Phase 4 US2: Depends on Phase 2 completion; uses occupied reservations from US1 for end-to-end validation but service and checkout logic can be developed independently.
  • Phase 5 US3: Depends on Phase 2 completion; full validation is strongest after US2 checkout exists.
  • Phase 6 US4: Depends on Phase 2 completion; refund validation is strongest after US3 billing exists.
  • Phase 7 Polish: Depends on all selected user stories being complete.

User Story Dependencies

  • US1 Manage Reservation Through Check-In: Start after foundational tasks; no dependency on other user stories.
  • US2 Add Services and Complete Checkout: Start after foundational tasks; integrates naturally after US1 but can be tested with prepared occupied reservations.
  • US3 Invoice and Settle Guest Balance: Start after foundational tasks; requires closed reservation data for full validation.
  • US4 Enforce Refund and Finance Separation: Start after foundational tasks; refund processing depends on invoice context for full validation.

Within Each User Story

  • Write story tests before implementation.
  • Implement or update tables before codeunits that depend on the fields.
  • Implement codeunit business logic before page action wiring.
  • Add authorization checks before considering a finance or refund action complete.
  • Run story-specific tests before moving to the next priority story.

Parallel Opportunities

  • Setup enum tasks T003, T004, and T005 can run in parallel.
  • Foundational table/page tasks T007, T009, T010, T016, T017, and T018 can run in parallel after T001.
  • Test tasks within each user story can run in parallel because they target separate test codeunits or distinct test procedures.
  • Page creation tasks can run in parallel with codeunit implementation when they use stable procedure signatures.
  • US2, US3, and US4 can be started by separate developers after Phase 2 if test data fixtures are created in src/test/HotelTestLibrary.Codeunit.al.

Parallel Example: User Story 1

Task: "T024 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for valid reservation creation and invalid date rejection in src/test/HotelReservationLifecycleTests.Codeunit.al"
Task: "T025 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for room assignment and overbooking rejection in src/test/HotelOverbookingTests.Codeunit.al"
Task: "T026 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for successful and failed deposit capture behavior in src/test/HotelPaymentTests.Codeunit.al"
Task: "T027 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for check-in status transition and room occupancy update in src/test/HotelCheckInTests.Codeunit.al"
Task: "T033 [P] [US1] Create Hotel Reservation Card page object 50013 with reservation fields and actions for Assign Room, Capture Deposit, and Check In in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al"
Task: "T034 [P] [US1] Create Hotel Reservation List page object 50014 with filters for Confirmed, Occupied, and Closed reservations in src/pages/HotelReservationList.Page.al"

Implementation Strategy

MVP First

  1. Complete Phase 1 and Phase 2.
  2. Complete Phase 3 for US1.
  3. Stop and validate reservation creation, room assignment, deposit outcome tracking, overbooking prevention, and check-in.

Incremental Delivery

  1. Deliver US1 as the operational MVP.
  2. Add US2 for in-stay service charges and checkout.
  3. Add US3 for finance invoicing, VAT, and final payment.
  4. Add US4 for refund control and role separation hardening.
  5. Run full quickstart validation after each increment.

Quality Gates

  • Every task must preserve the app.json constraints and keep AL source under src/.
  • Every user story must have passing story-specific AL tests before it is marked complete.
  • Every finance-sensitive action must have server-side authorization checks, not only page visibility.
  • Every generated invoice path must prevent duplicate invoice creation for the same reservation.
checklists/requirements.md34 lines
