contracts/hotel-reservation-workflows.md222 lines

Workflow Contracts: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

This feature exposes Business Central user workflows rather than an external API. These contracts define the expected user-visible actions, authorization boundaries, inputs, outputs, and failure behavior that implementation and tests must preserve.

Reservation Creation and Room Assignment

Authorized Role: Front Desk, Admin

Inputs

  • Customer No.
  • Check-in Date
  • Check-out Date
  • Optional Room No.
  • Deposit Amount

Preconditions

  • Customer exists.
  • Check-out Date is later than Check-in Date.
  • Assigned room exists, is not blocked, and has no overlapping active reservation.

Successful Outcome

  • Reservation is created or updated with status Confirmed.
  • Assigned room is linked to the reservation when provided.
  • No overlapping reservation exists for the same room and date range.

Failure Outcomes

  • Invalid date range is rejected.
  • Missing customer is rejected.
  • Overlapping room assignment is rejected.
  • Blocked room assignment is rejected.

Deposit Capture

Authorized Role: Front Desk, Finance

Inputs

  • Reservation No.
  • Deposit Amount
  • Payment Method
  • Payment outcome
  • Transaction Reference when available

Preconditions

  • Reservation exists.
  • Deposit amount is non-negative.

Successful Outcome

  • Successful payment outcome creates a succeeded payment entry.
  • Reservation deposit captured state is set only for succeeded deposit outcomes.

Failure Outcomes

  • Failed payment outcome creates a failed payment entry for visibility.
  • Reservation is not marked as paid or deposit-captured on failed outcome.

Check-In

Authorized Role: Front Desk

Inputs

  • Reservation No.

Preconditions

  • Reservation status is Confirmed.
  • Room is assigned.
  • Assigned room is available for the reservation date range.
  • Deposit state satisfies hotel policy.

Successful Outcome

  • Reservation status becomes Occupied.
  • Assigned room Occupied indicator becomes true.

Failure Outcomes

  • Missing room assignment is rejected.
  • Conflicting occupancy is rejected.
  • Invalid reservation status is rejected.

Service Charge Entry

Authorized Role: Front Desk

Inputs

  • Reservation No.
  • Service Date
  • Service Type
  • Description
  • Quantity
  • Unit Amount

Preconditions

  • Reservation exists.
  • Reservation is Occupied.
  • Invoice has not already been generated.

Successful Outcome

  • Billable service charge line is linked to the reservation.
  • Service line is available for final invoice generation.

Failure Outcomes

  • Closed or invoiced reservations reject new standard service charges.
  • Negative amount or non-positive quantity is rejected.

Checkout

Authorized Role: Front Desk

Inputs

  • Reservation No.

Preconditions

  • Reservation status is Occupied.
  • Assigned room exists.

Successful Outcome

  • Reservation status becomes Closed.
  • Assigned room Occupied indicator becomes false.
  • Reservation remains available for finance billing.

Failure Outcomes

  • Non-occupied reservations cannot be checked out.
  • Checkout cannot release a room different from the assigned room.

Invoice Generation

Authorized Role: Finance

Inputs

  • Reservation No.

Preconditions

  • Reservation status is Closed.
  • No invoice has already been generated for the reservation.
  • Hotel billing and VAT setup is complete.

Successful Outcome

  • One sales invoice is generated for the reservation.
  • Room-night charges, service charges, VAT configuration, deposit application, and remaining amount due are represented.
  • Reservation stores the invoice reference.
  • Service charge lines are marked as included in invoice.

Failure Outcomes

  • Duplicate invoice request is rejected and original invoice reference is preserved.
  • Missing setup is rejected before invoice creation.
  • VAT setup validation failure prevents posting.

Final Payment

Authorized Role: Finance

Inputs

  • Reservation No.
  • Invoice No.
  • Amount
  • Payment Method
  • Payment outcome

Preconditions

  • Invoice exists.
  • Amount does not exceed remaining amount due unless explicitly handled by finance correction policy.

Successful Outcome

  • Successful final payment settles the remaining amount due.
  • Payment entry is linked to reservation and invoice context.

Failure Outcomes

  • Failed final payment creates no settlement effect.
  • Missing invoice context is rejected.

Refund Processing

Authorized Role: Finance

Inputs

  • Reservation No.
  • Invoice No.
  • Refund Amount
  • Reason

Preconditions

  • User has finance authorization.
  • Reservation has a billed or paid financial context.
  • Refund amount is positive and within refundable bounds.

Successful Outcome

  • Refund entry is recorded with reservation and invoice context.
  • Ledger/payment outcome is available for finance review.

Failure Outcomes

  • Front Desk refund attempts are blocked with no financial effect.
  • Missing invoice or invalid amount is rejected.
  • Unauthorized invocation outside the page action is blocked by server-side validation.
Speckit + Gemini8 files
spec.md103 lines

Feature Specification: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Feature Branch: [001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle]

Created: 2026-06-13

Status: Draft

Input: User description: "Build a Business Central extension that supports the hotel guest lifecycle from reservation through billing."

User Scenarios & Testing (mandatory)

User Story 1 - Front Desk Manages Guest Stay (Priority: P1)

As a Front Desk user, I need to manage a guest's stay from initial reservation to checkout, so that I can handle daily hotel operations efficiently.

Why this priority: This is the core operational flow of the hotel and represents the primary value of the extension for daily users.

Independent Test: This can be tested by creating a new customer and reservation, assigning a room, posting a deposit, adding a service charge, and completing the checkout process. The successful completion of this journey delivers the main guest management functionality.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a new guest wants to book a room, When the Front Desk user creates a reservation and assigns an available room, Then the system confirms the reservation and marks the room as reserved for the specified dates.
  2. Given a guest with a confirmed reservation arrives, When the Front Desk user checks them in, Then the reservation status updates to 'Occupied' and the room is marked as occupied.
  3. Given an in-house guest requests a service (e.g., laundry), When the Front Desk user adds a service charge to their reservation, Then the charge is recorded and linked to the guest's final bill.
  4. Given a guest is ready to check out, When the Front Desk user initiates checkout, Then the system calculates the total bill, including room charges and services, and marks the reservation status as 'Closed'.

User Story 2 - Finance Processes Invoices and Payments (Priority: P2)

As a Finance user, I need to generate accurate invoices, handle payments, and process refunds securely, so that the hotel's financial records are correct and auditable.

Why this priority: This ensures financial integrity, which is critical for the business. It follows the operational flow to correctly handle the monetary transactions.

Independent Test: This can be tested by taking a 'Closed' reservation, generating an invoice, applying the deposit, posting the final payment, and verifying the financial postings. A refund scenario can also be tested independently.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a reservation is 'Closed' after checkout, When the Finance user generates an invoice, Then the system creates a single, non-duplicate invoice that includes all room and service charges, with VAT calculated correctly.
  2. Given an invoice has been generated, When the Finance user posts the final payment, Then the system applies the initial deposit, settles the remaining balance, and records the transaction in the financial ledgers.
  3. Given a valid business reason for a refund, When a Finance user processes a refund, Then the system correctly posts the refund transaction.
  4. Given a Front Desk user attempts to process a refund, When they try the action, Then the system blocks the action and presents an authorization error.

User Story 3 - Admin Configures Hotel System (Priority: P3)

As an Admin user, I need to configure the hotel's master data, such as rooms and tax rates, so that the system operates according to the hotel's specific parameters.

Why this priority: Setup and configuration are essential for the system to function, but they are performed less frequently than operational or financial tasks.

Independent Test: This can be tested by adding a new room to the inventory, setting its nightly rate, and verifying it becomes available for new reservations.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given the hotel is adding a new room, When the Admin user adds a new room with its type and rate, Then the system lists the new room in the available inventory.
  2. Given there is a change in tax legislation, When the Admin user updates the VAT rates in the system, Then all new invoices generated after the change must use the new rates for calculation.

Edge Cases

  • Overbooking: How does the system prevent a room from being assigned to two different reservations for the same dates?
  • Failed Payment: What happens if a guest's deposit or final payment transaction fails? The system should not mark the payment as successful.
  • Duplicate Invoice: How does the system prevent more than one final invoice from being generated for a single reservation?
  • Unauthorized Refund: How is it enforced that only Finance users can process refunds?

Requirements (mandatory)

Functional Requirements

  • FR-001: System MUST allow for the creation, update, and management of reservations with statuses (Confirmed, Occupied, Closed).
  • FR-002: System MUST maintain a room inventory and track occupancy to prevent overbooking and overlapping room use.
  • FR-003: System MUST release a room's occupancy status immediately upon guest checkout.
  • FR-004: System MUST capture and track guest deposits and apply them against the final invoice.
  • FR-005: System MUST prevent marking a payment as successful if the underlying transaction fails.
  • FR-006: System MUST allow billable service charges to be added to a reservation and included in the final bill.
  • FR-007: System MUST generate a single, unique invoice per reservation upon checkout.
  • FR-008: System MUST calculate VAT on invoices based on configured rates.
  • FR-009: System MUST post all financial results correctly to the appropriate ledgers.
  • FR-010: System MUST enforce role-based security, strictly preventing Front Desk users from processing refunds.

Key Entities

  • Reservation: Represents a booking made by a customer. Key attributes include Reservation No., Customer No., Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date, Status, Deposit Amount, and Invoice No.
  • Room: Represents a physical room in the hotel. Key attributes include Room No., Room Type, Nightly Rate, and an Occupied status.
  • Service Charge: Represents a billable service linked to a reservation. Key attributes include a description, amount, and a link to the parent reservation.

Success Criteria (mandatory)

Measurable Outcomes

  • SC-001: A reservation, from creation to check-in, can be completed by a Front Desk user in under 3 minutes.
  • SC-002: The system must prevent 100% of attempted overbookings for the same room on the same date.
  • SC-003: The time from checkout to having a customer-ready invoice generated must be less than 1 minute.
  • SC-004: 100% of refund actions attempted by non-Finance roles must be blocked.
  • SC-005: Financial reports must show a 100% match between charges posted and payments received for all closed reservations over a 30-day period.

Assumptions

  • The system will be built as an extension for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central and will leverage its standard customer and financial posting capabilities.
  • Users (Front Desk, Finance, Admin) are already defined within the Business Central roles and permissions framework.
  • A standard payment gateway integration is assumed to be available for handling credit card transactions, but the specific integration is out of scope for this feature. The focus is on tracking the success or failure of payments.
  • The definition and configuration of VAT and other taxes are handled through standard Business Central setup.
plan.md81 lines

Implementation Plan: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Branch: 001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle | Date: 2026-06-13 | Spec: spec.md

Input: Feature specification from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/spec.md

Summary

This plan outlines the technical implementation for a Business Central extension to manage the hotel guest lifecycle. The approach is to build a set of related but modular components for managing reservations, rooms, and billing, all within the AL language and Business Central environment.

Technical Context

Language/Version: AL for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central (latest version)

Primary Dependencies: Microsoft Base Application, Microsoft System Application

Storage: Business Central Database (SQL Server)

Testing: AL Test Toolkit

Target Platform: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central (Cloud)

Project Type: Business Central Extension

Performance Goals: UI actions (e.g., creating a reservation, check-in) should complete in under 2 seconds. Invoice generation for a standard reservation should take less than 5 seconds.

Constraints: Must adhere to Business Central extension development best practices to ensure upgradeability and maintainability. All financial transactions must use standard Business Central APIs and posting routines.

Scale/Scope: The initial version will support up to 200 rooms and handle approximately 50-100 reservations per day.

Constitution Check

GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.

  • All development will follow Test-First principles using the AL Test Toolkit.
  • The extension will be designed as a modular library.
  • Publicly accessible objects (Codeunits, Pages) will serve as the CLI/API, with clear interfaces.

Verdict: The plan aligns with the core principles of the constitution.

Project Structure

Documentation (this feature)

specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Not generated; no major unknowns
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output
└── tasks.md             # To be created by /speckit.tasks

Source Code (repository root)

src/
├── tables/
│   ├── HotelReservation.Table.al
│   └── HotelRoom.Table.al
├── pages/
│   ├── HotelReservation.Page.al
│   └── HotelRoomList.Page.al
├── codeunits/
│   ├── ReservationManager.Codeunit.al
│   └── BillingManager.Codeunit.al
└── enums/
    └── ReservationStatus.Enum.al

tests/
├── src/
│   ├── ReservationManager.Test.al
│   └── BillingManager.Test.al
└── app.json

Structure Decision: A single project structure is appropriate for this Business Central extension. The source code will be organized by object type (tables, pages, codeunits) in a flat src directory, which is standard for AL projects.

Complexity Tracking

No violations of the constitution are anticipated.

data-model.md55 lines

Data Model: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

This document defines the core data entities for the Hotel Reservation Lifecycle feature, based on the requirements in spec.md.

Entity: Reservation

Represents a customer's booking for a room.

AL Table: HotelReservation.Table.al

Field NameData TypeDescriptionValidation Rules
Reservation No.Code(20)Primary Key.Mandatory, Unique.
Customer No.Code(20)Foreign key to the Customer table.Must exist in Customer table.
Room No.Code(20)Foreign key to the Room table.Must exist in Room table.
Check-in DateDateThe date the guest is scheduled to arrive.Must be before Check-out Date.
Check-out DateDateThe date the guest is scheduled to depart.Must be after Check-in Date.
StatusEnumThe current status of the reservation.Must be one of: Confirmed, Occupied, Closed.
Deposit AmountDecimalThe amount of the deposit paid.Must be a positive value.
Invoice No.Code(20)The posted sales invoice number.Populated at checkout.

State Transitions

  • A Reservation starts with a status of Confirmed.
  • On check-in, the status changes to Occupied.
  • On check-out, the status changes to Closed.

Entity: Room

Represents a physical room in the hotel.

AL Table: HotelRoom.Table.al

Field NameData TypeDescriptionValidation Rules
Room No.Code(20)Primary Key.Mandatory, Unique.
Room TypeText(50)The type of room (e.g., Single, Double).
Nightly RateDecimalThe cost of the room per night.Must be a positive value.
OccupiedBooleanIndicates if the room is currently occupied.Managed by reservation check-in/out process.

Entity: Service Charge

Represents a billable service added to a reservation. This will be implemented using the standard Sales Line table with a reference to the reservation.

AL Table: Sales Line (Standard BC Table)

Field NameData TypeDescription
Document TypeEnumWill be 'Invoice'.
Document No.Code(20)The Reservation No.
TypeEnumThe type of charge (e.g., G/L Account, Item).
No.Code(20)The G/L Account or Item number.
DescriptionText(100)Description of the service.
Unit PriceDecimalThe price of the service.
QuantityDecimalThe quantity of the service.
quickstart.md43 lines

Quickstart: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

This guide provides steps to validate the core functionality of the Hotel Reservation Lifecycle extension.

Prerequisites

  • A running Business Central environment with the extension installed.
  • A test customer created in the system.
  • At least one room configured in the Room inventory.

Validation Scenarios

Scenario 1: Full Guest Lifecycle

This scenario validates the primary user journey from reservation to billing.

  1. Create a Reservation:
  • Action: Using the ReservationManager codeunit, call the CreateReservation function with a valid Customer No., Room No., and dates.
  • Expected Outcome: A new reservation is created with the status 'Confirmed'. The reservation number is returned.
  1. Check-In Guest:
  • Action: Call the CheckIn function with the reservation number from the previous step.
  • Expected Outcome: The reservation status changes to 'Occupied'.
  1. Add a Service Charge:
  • Action: Call the AddServiceCharge function in the BillingManager to add a charge for 'Laundry' to the reservation.
  • Expected Outcome: A new line is added to the reservation's billing details.
  1. Check-Out Guest:
  • Action: Call the CheckOut function with the reservation number.
  • Expected Outcome: The reservation status changes to 'Closed'.
  1. Generate Invoice:
  • Action: Call the GenerateInvoice function in the BillingManager with the reservation number.
  • Expected Outcome: A new, posted sales invoice is created, and the invoice number is returned. The invoice should contain lines for the room charges and the laundry service.

Scenario 2: Security - Unauthorized Refund

This scenario validates the security constraint preventing the Front Desk role from processing refunds.

  1. Attempt Refund:
  • Action: As a user with the 'Front Desk' permission set, attempt to run the standard 'Create Credit Memo' action on the invoice generated in Scenario 1.
  • Expected Outcome: The system should display a permission error, blocking the action.
tasks.md158 lines

Tasks: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Input: Design documents from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/

Prerequisites: plan.md, spec.md, data-model.md, contracts/

Tests: Test tasks are included as per the project's Test-First constitution.

Organization: Tasks are grouped by user story to enable independent implementation and testing.

Format: [ID] [P?] [Story] Description

  • [P]: Can run in parallel (different files, no dependencies)
  • [Story]: Which user story this task belongs to (e.g., US1, US2, US3)
  • Include exact file paths in descriptions

Path Conventions

  • Paths are relative to the project root.

Phase 1: Setup (Shared Infrastructure)

Purpose: Configure the AL project and base structure.

  • [ ] T001 Verify and configure the main app.json for the extension.
  • [ ] T002 Configure debugger settings in .vscode/launch.json.

Phase 2: Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)

Purpose: Core data structures that MUST be complete before ANY user story can be implemented.

  • [ ] T003 Create the ReservationStatus.Enum.al in src/enums/.
  • [X] T004 [P] Create the HotelRoom.Table.al definition in src/tables/.
  • [ ] T005 [P] Create the HotelReservation.Table.al definition in src/tables/.

Checkpoint: Foundation ready - user story implementation can now begin.

Phase 3: User Story 1 - Front Desk Manages Guest Stay (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

Goal: Implement the core operational flow for managing a guest's stay, from reservation to checkout.

Independent Test: Create a reservation, check a guest in, add a service charge, and check the guest out. Verify the status changes and financial entries are correct.

Tests for User Story 1 ⚠️

NOTE: Write these tests FIRST, ensure they FAIL before implementation
  • [ ] T006 [P] [US1] Create test file ReservationManager.Test.al in tests/src/ with failing tests for the CreateReservation, CheckIn, and CheckOut functions.
  • [ ] T007 [P] [US1] Create test file BillingManager.Test.al in tests/src/ with a failing test for the AddServiceCharge function.

Implementation for User Story 1

  • [ ] T008 [US1] Implement ReservationManager.Codeunit.al in src/codeunits/ with the CreateReservation, CheckIn, and CheckOut functions to pass tests from T006.
  • [ ] T009 [US1] Implement BillingManager.Codeunit.al in src/codeunits/ with the AddServiceCharge function to pass the test from T007.
  • [ ] T010 [P] [US1] Create the HotelRoomList.Page.al in src/pages/ to display the list of hotel rooms.
  • [X] T011 [US1] Create the HotelReservation.Page.al in src/pages/ to create and manage individual reservations.

Checkpoint: At this point, User Story 1 should be fully functional and testable independently.

Phase 4: User Story 2 - Finance Processes Invoices and Payments (Priority: P2)

Goal: Implement the financial processing part of the lifecycle, including invoicing and security.

Independent Test: Take a reservation with a 'Closed' status, generate an invoice, and verify its correctness. As a 'Front Desk' user, attempt to issue a refund and confirm it is blocked.

Tests for User Story 2 ⚠️

  • [ ] T012 [US2] Add a failing test to BillingManager.Test.al in tests/src/ for the GenerateInvoice function.
  • [ ] T013 [US2] Add a failing security test to BillingManager.Test.al in tests/src/ to verify that a user without 'Finance' permissions cannot process a refund/credit memo.

Implementation for User Story 2

  • [ ] T014 [US2] Extend the BillingManager.Codeunit.al in src/codeunits/ to implement the GenerateInvoice function, ensuring it passes the test from T012.
  • [X] T015 [US2] Implement the necessary permission checks in the system to block unauthorized refund attempts, ensuring the security test from T013 passes.

Checkpoint: At this point, User Stories 1 AND 2 should both work independently.

Phase 5: User Story 3 - Admin Configures Hotel System (Priority: P3)

Goal: Allow an administrator to configure master data for the hotel.

Independent Test: Create a new room via the UI, then verify it is available for a new reservation.

Tests for User Story 3 ⚠️

  • [ ] T016 [US3] Add a test to tests/src/ that programmatically creates a new room and then verifies it can be assigned to a new reservation.

Implementation for User Story 3

  • [X] T017 [US3] Ensure the HotelRoomList.Page.al in src/pages/ has actions to create, edit, and delete rooms, and that the underlying HotelRoom.Table.al supports these operations.

Checkpoint: All user stories should now be independently functional.

Phase 6: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

Purpose: Final improvements and documentation.

  • [ ] T018 [P] Review and add comments to all new AL files (.al) for clarity.
  • [ ] T019 [P] Perform a final review of all code against AL best practices.
  • [X] T020 Run all validation scenarios listed in specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/quickstart.md to ensure end-to-end functionality.

Dependencies & Execution Order

Phase Dependencies

  • Setup (Phase 1): Must be completed first.
  • Foundational (Phase 2): Depends on Setup. Blocks all user story phases.
  • User Stories (Phase 3-5): Depend on Foundational.
  • US2 depends on US1 because invoicing requires a completed reservation.
  • US3 is independent of US1 and US2 and could be done after the Foundational phase.
  • Polish (Phase 6): Depends on all user stories being complete.

Recommended Execution Flow

  1. Phase 1 (Setup)
  2. Phase 2 (Foundational)
  3. Phase 3 (User Story 1)
  4. Phase 4 (User Story 2)
  5. Phase 5 (User Story 3)
  6. Phase 6 (Polish)

Parallel Opportunities

  • Within Phase 2, T004 and T005 can be done in parallel.
  • Within Phase 3 (US1), T006 and T007 (test creation) can be done in parallel. After tests are written, T008 and T009 (codeunit implementation) can be worked on in parallel. UI tasks T010 and T011 can also be done in parallel with the codeunit work, as long as the underlying tables exist.
  • Once the foundational phase is done, User Story 3 can be developed in parallel with User Story 1 and 2.

Implementation Strategy

MVP First (User Story 1 Only)

  1. Complete Phase 1: Setup
  2. Complete Phase 2: Foundational
  3. Complete Phase 3: User Story 1
  4. STOP and VALIDATE: Test the full Front Desk flow independently. This provides the core value of the extension.

Incremental Delivery

  1. Deliver MVP (US1).
  2. Add User Story 2 for financial processing.
  3. Add User Story 3 for administrative configuration.
  4. Complete the Polish phase.

contracts/billing.md28 lines

Contract: Billing Management

AL Codeunit: BillingManager.Codeunit.al

This codeunit handles the financial aspects of a reservation, including invoicing and payments.

Public Functions

GenerateInvoice(ReservationNo: Code[20]): Code[20]

  • Description: Generates a sales invoice for a 'Closed' reservation. It compiles all room charges and service charges.
  • Parameters:
  • ReservationNo: The reservation to generate an invoice for.
  • Returns: The Invoice No. of the newly created and posted sales invoice.
  • Events:
  • OnAfterGenerateInvoice(ReservationNo: Code[20]; InvoiceNo: Code[20])

AddServiceCharge(ReservationNo: Code[20]; ServiceType: Enum; ServiceNo: Code[20]; Description: Text; Amount: Decimal)

  • Description: Adds a billable service to a reservation.
  • Parameters:
  • ReservationNo: The reservation to add the charge to.
  • ServiceType: The type of service (e.g., G/L Account).
  • ServiceNo: The number of the service (e.g., G/L Account No.).
  • Description: A description of the service.
  • Amount: The cost of the service.
  • Events:
  • OnAfterAddServiceCharge(ReservationNo: Code[20])
contracts/reservations.md35 lines

Contract: Reservation Management

AL Codeunit: ReservationManager.Codeunit.al

This codeunit exposes the primary business logic for managing reservations.

Public Functions

CreateReservation(CustomerNo: Code[20]; RoomNo: Code[20]; CheckinDate: Date; CheckoutDate: Date): Code[20]

  • Description: Creates a new reservation with a 'Confirmed' status.
  • Parameters:
  • CustomerNo: The customer who is making the reservation.
  • RoomNo: The room being reserved.
  • CheckinDate: The check-in date.
  • CheckoutDate: The check-out date.
  • Returns: The Reservation No. of the newly created reservation.
  • Events:
  • OnAfterCreateReservation(ReservationNo: Code[20])

CheckIn(ReservationNo: Code[20])

  • Description: Checks in a guest for a confirmed reservation, changing the status to 'Occupied'.
  • Parameters:
  • ReservationNo: The reservation to check in.
  • Events:
  • OnAfterCheckIn(ReservationNo: Code[20])

CheckOut(ReservationNo: Code[20])

  • Description: Checks out a guest, changing the reservation status to 'Closed'. This will trigger the billing process.
  • Parameters:
  • ReservationNo: The reservation to check out.
  • Events:
  • OnAfterCheckOut(ReservationNo: Code[20])

Vendor Rebate Engine 04-vendor-management

Enterprise vendor rebate engine: 13 FRs, approval workflow, retroactive recalculation, job queue, integration events.

Speckit + Codex9 files
spec.md212 lines

Feature Specification: Global Rebate Engine

Feature Branch: 001-global-rebate-engine

Created: 2026-06-15

Status: Draft

Input: User description: "Global Enterprise Trade Promotion & Vendor Rebate Engine"

User Scenarios & Testing (mandatory)

User Story 1 - Manage and Activate Rebate Agreements (Priority: P1)

Finance, procurement, and sales operations users need to define vendor rebate and customer incentive agreements with clear eligibility rules, validity periods, parties, currencies, dimensions, approval status, and posting setup so approved agreements can be used for calculation with full auditability.

Why this priority: No accrual, settlement, or reconciliation process can be trusted until agreement setup, approval, activation, and version control are reliable.

Independent Test: Can be tested by creating a vendor rebate agreement, adding eligibility criteria and rules, submitting it for approval, approving it, activating it, then verifying that the active version is locked and usable for calculation.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a user has agreement management permission, When they create an agreement with required parties, dates, eligibility criteria, rule details, and posting setup, Then the agreement can be submitted for approval.
  2. Given an agreement is missing required posting setup or contains conflicting active rules, When the user attempts activation, Then activation is blocked with specific validation messages.
  3. Given an agreement is active, When a manager changes business terms, Then a new version is created and prior versions remain readable, locked, and linked to prior calculations.
  4. Given an auditor reviews an agreement, When they compare two versions, Then they can see changed fields, effective dates, user, timestamp, and reason.

User Story 2 - Calculate Rebate Accruals for Eligible Transactions (Priority: P1)

Rebate analysts need to run manual or scheduled calculations that evaluate sales and purchase transactions against active agreement rules, record eligibility outcomes, calculate accrual amounts, and produce detailed entries that can be traced back to source transactions.

Why this priority: Automated, repeatable calculation is the core business value and replaces manual spreadsheets and fragmented legacy processes.

Independent Test: Can be tested by preparing approved agreements and source transactions, running a calculation for a date range and company scope, then verifying calculated amounts, rejected-line reasons, currency details, and request status.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given active agreements and unprocessed eligible source lines exist, When a calculation request is run, Then the system creates calculation ledger entries with agreement version, rule, source transaction, company, currency, exchange rate, and processing request references.
  2. Given a source line does not meet eligibility criteria, When the calculation request evaluates it, Then the system records a clear rejection reason without creating an accrual amount.
  3. Given a calculation request for the same processing scope has already completed, When the same request is run again without recalculation mode, Then duplicate calculation and duplicate accrual results are prevented.
  4. Given a high-volume calculation fails partway through, When the user retries the failed portion, Then completed chunks are preserved and only failed or pending chunks are resumed.

User Story 3 - Post, Reverse, and Reconcile Accruals (Priority: P1)

Controllers and rebate posters need calculated accruals to become auditable financial postings, with posting preview, balanced entries, dimensions, reversal support, duplicate-posting prevention, and reconciliation between accrual balances and financial results.

Why this priority: Financial correctness and audit traceability are required before the feature can support enterprise accounting processes.

Independent Test: Can be tested by calculating accruals, previewing postings, posting approved accruals, reversing them, and reconciling accrual entries to financial postings for a period.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given calculated accruals are ready for posting, When a permitted user previews posting, Then the preview shows balanced debit and credit impact with dimensions and posting groups.
  2. Given accrual entries are approved for posting, When a permitted user posts them, Then balanced financial entries are created and each accrual entry is marked with posting references.
  3. Given an accrual entry has already been posted, When any user attempts to post it again, Then duplicate posting is blocked.
  4. Given a posted accrual must be corrected, When a permitted user reverses it with a reason code, Then the system creates traceable reversal entries rather than editing posted records.

User Story 4 - Settle Approved Rebate Balances (Priority: P2)

Finance users need to create settlement proposals from open accrual balances, review and adjust proposed lines, route settlements for approval, post final settlements, and close selected accrual entries.

Why this priority: Settlement completes the commercial lifecycle by turning accrual balances into claims, credits, invoices, or financial adjustments.

Independent Test: Can be tested by generating a settlement proposal for a vendor or customer, approving it, posting it, and confirming selected accrual entries are closed.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given open approved accrual entries exist for a party and period, When a user creates a settlement proposal, Then eligible entries are grouped by party, agreement, company, currency, and settlement method.
  2. Given a settlement proposal is under review, When a user adjusts settlement amounts within permitted limits and provides a reason, Then the adjustment is retained in the settlement audit history.
  3. Given a settlement is rejected, When a user attempts posting, Then posting is blocked until the settlement is corrected and approved.
  4. Given a settlement is posted, When users view included accrual entries, Then those entries show closed status and settlement references.

User Story 5 - Recalculate Retroactive Changes and Post Deltas (Priority: P2)

Rebate managers and controllers need a controlled recalculation process when agreement terms change retroactively, so impacted historical transactions are recomputed and only delta adjustments are posted.

Why this priority: Enterprise rebate programs often change after transactions occur; corrections must be auditable and financially controlled.

Independent Test: Can be tested by changing agreement terms with a retroactive effective date, running recalculation for the affected period, and verifying old amounts, new amounts, deltas, and linked adjustment postings.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given an approved retroactive agreement version exists, When recalculation runs for the impacted scope, Then the system identifies historical transactions that used the prior version.
  2. Given impacted transactions are recalculated, When old and new rebate amounts differ, Then the system creates delta entries linked to original calculation entries.
  3. Given a recalculation produces no difference, When results are reviewed, Then the system records the no-change outcome without creating financial adjustments.
  4. Given a recalculation requires approval, When it is not approved, Then delta postings are blocked.

User Story 6 - Monitor Background Jobs, Integrations, and Audit Evidence (Priority: P3)

System administrators, integration support teams, and auditors need visibility into long-running processing, external messages, retries, failures, approvals, data changes, and audit exports across companies and regions.

Why this priority: Enterprise operations require transparency, retry control, and audit evidence once core agreement, calculation, posting, and settlement flows are established.

Independent Test: Can be tested by running background calculations, importing an external agreement reference, forcing retryable failures, reviewing logs, and exporting audit data for a selected period.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a long-running job is active, When a user opens the job monitor, Then they can see scope, progress, completed chunks, failed chunks, retry eligibility, and current status.
  2. Given an external message has a unique external reference, When it is processed successfully, Then repeat delivery of the same reference does not create duplicate business records.
  3. Given an integration request fails with a retryable error, When an authorized user retries it, Then the retry is logged and linked to the original request.
  4. Given an auditor exports audit evidence, When they filter by period, company, user, agreement, or source document, Then exported records include user, timestamp, action, old value, new value, reason, and related record.

Edge Cases

  • Agreements with overlapping validity, party, item, location, country, or dimension criteria must be detected and either blocked or clearly prioritized before activation.
  • Source lines in currencies different from the agreement or company reporting currency must preserve the exchange rate and currency basis used for calculation.
  • Transactions that are reversed, corrected, settled, or already processed must not be silently recalculated unless the user runs an authorized recalculation process.
  • Posting failure after partial preparation must leave a traceable recovery path and must not create unbalanced financial results.
  • Suspended, expired, rejected, or draft agreements must not be used for new calculations.
  • Failed calculation chunks must be retryable without duplicating completed ledger entries.
  • Settlement proposals must handle mixed currencies, closed entries, reversed entries, and entries already selected by another open proposal.
  • Integration messages with duplicate external reference IDs must be idempotent across retries.
  • Users without the required role must be blocked from approval, posting, setup changes, audit export, and sensitive integration maintenance.
  • High-volume processing must avoid blocking normal users from posting everyday business transactions.

Requirements (mandatory)

Functional Requirements

  • FR-001: System MUST allow authorized users to create, view, modify, submit, approve, activate, suspend, expire, and archive vendor rebate and customer incentive agreements.
  • FR-002: System MUST support agreement attributes for agreement type, parties, party groups, items, item categories, locations, countries or regions, dimensions, validity dates, currencies, settlement method, thresholds, and posting behavior.
  • FR-003: System MUST validate that required agreement setup, approval status, dates, rules, thresholds, and posting configuration are complete before activation.
  • FR-004: System MUST create immutable agreement versions when active agreement terms change, and MUST link each calculation to the agreement version used.
  • FR-005: System MUST allow auditors to compare agreement versions and view changed fields, user, timestamp, reason, and effective period.
  • FR-006: System MUST allow authorized users to configure standard rule bases including sales amount, purchase amount, quantity, margin, payment date, invoice date, and shipment or receipt date.
  • FR-007: System MUST allow standard calculation methods including fixed amount, percentage, tiered percentage, slab amount, formula reference, growth-based calculation, and inclusion or exclusion criteria.
  • FR-008: System MUST detect conflicting, overlapping, or incomplete rules before activation and present actionable validation messages.
  • FR-009: System MUST identify eligible sales and purchase source lines for a requested company, party, date range, and agreement scope.
  • FR-010: System MUST record whether each evaluated source line was eligible or rejected, including the reason and rule context.
  • FR-011: System MUST prevent already processed source lines from being processed again unless an authorized recalculation mode is used.
  • FR-012: System MUST produce detailed calculation ledger entries containing source transaction, agreement version, rule, company, currency, exchange rate, calculation basis, calculated amount, status, and processing request.
  • FR-013: System MUST calculate accrual balances from calculation ledger entries and support manual, scheduled, and resumable processing.
  • FR-014: System MUST make repeated execution for the same completed processing scope idempotent.
  • FR-015: System MUST support posting preview for accruals, reversals, settlements, and adjustments before final posting.
  • FR-016: System MUST create balanced financial postings for accruals, reversals, settlements, and adjustments, with dimensions and posting groups carried from configured and source data.
  • FR-017: System MUST prevent duplicate financial posting for the same accrual, settlement, reversal, or adjustment.
  • FR-018: System MUST keep posted calculation entries, posted accrual entries, posted settlements, and financial references immutable; corrections MUST be represented by reversal or delta entries.
  • FR-019: System MUST allow authorized users to initiate recalculation by agreement, company, party, date range, or source scope.
  • FR-020: System MUST identify impacted historical transactions during recalculation and compute previous amount, revised amount, delta amount, and link to original entries.
  • FR-021: System MUST post only approved delta adjustments or reversals for recalculation outcomes.
  • FR-022: System MUST allow users to create settlement proposals from open approved accrual balances and group entries by party, agreement, company, currency, period, and settlement method.
  • FR-023: System MUST allow authorized users to review, adjust with reason, approve, reject, and post settlement proposals.
  • FR-024: System MUST close selected accrual entries when settlement is posted and retain references between settlement, accrual, calculation, source transaction, and financial posting.
  • FR-025: System MUST support approval workflows for agreements, recalculations, settlements, and material adjustments.
  • FR-026: System MUST route approvals using amount, region, company, agreement type, department, and role-based criteria.
  • FR-027: System MUST record approval history including submitter, approver, action, timestamp, comments, delegation, rejection, and requested changes.
  • FR-028: System MUST enforce separation of duties for setup, agreement management, approval, calculation, posting, administration, and audit export.
  • FR-029: System MUST maintain audit records for business-critical changes and postings, including user, timestamp, action, old value, new value, reason, and related record.
  • FR-030: System MUST prevent users from editing audit records through normal user interfaces.
  • FR-031: System MUST process high-volume calculation and recalculation work in monitored chunks with progress, failure status, retry eligibility, and recovery details.
  • FR-032: System MUST classify errors as validation, configuration, posting, integration, concurrency, or unexpected system errors.
  • FR-033: System MUST store user-facing message, technical details, related record, retry eligibility, timestamp, and user or job context for each processing error.
  • FR-034: System MUST expose controlled integration capabilities for agreement import, calculation status, settlement export, and audit data exchange.
  • FR-035: System MUST log integration requests and responses, enforce unique external reference IDs, and support retry of failed external messages.
  • FR-036: System MUST protect sensitive integration values and commercial agreement data from unauthorized viewing or modification.
  • FR-037: System MUST provide operational and financial reporting views for accrual balances, settlement status, calculation results, exceptions, audit history, and reconciliation.
  • FR-038: System MUST allow reports to be filtered by company, period, party, agreement, currency, status, and source document.
  • FR-039: System MUST support multi-company, multi-currency, and multi-country operation without hardcoded company, currency, region, posting, or tax assumptions.
  • FR-040: System MUST allow future country-specific eligibility, posting, tax, and settlement behavior to be added without changing existing agreement history or posted records.

Key Entities (include if feature involves data)

  • Rebate Agreement: Commercial arrangement defining agreement type, parties, eligibility scope, validity, rules, settlement method, approval state, and posting behavior.
  • Agreement Version: Immutable snapshot of approved agreement terms used for calculation and audit comparison.
  • Rebate Rule: Business condition and calculation definition that determines eligibility and rebate amount.
  • Threshold: Volume, amount, quantity, margin, growth, or period boundary used by tiered, slab, or growth-based rules.
  • Calculation Request: User- or schedule-created processing scope with company, period, party, agreement, status, progress, and retry details.
  • Calculation Ledger Entry: Detailed result of evaluating a source transaction line against an agreement version and rule.
  • Accrual Entry: Financial estimate created from calculation results before final settlement, including open, posted, reversed, or closed status.
  • Settlement Proposal: Group of eligible open accrual entries prepared for review, approval, adjustment, and posting.
  • Settlement Line: Individual accrual or adjustment included in a settlement proposal with amount, currency, status, and source references.
  • Recalculation Result: Comparison of original and revised calculation amounts, including delta, reason, impacted source, and link to original entries.
  • Audit Entry: Immutable record of business-critical changes, approvals, postings, reversals, exports, and administrative actions.
  • Integration Message: External request or response with external reference ID, status, payload summary, retry state, and error details.
  • Processing Error: Classified error record with user-facing message, technical details, related record, retry eligibility, and context.
  • Posting Setup: Company-specific configuration that determines how accruals, settlements, reversals, and adjustments are financially classified.

Success Criteria (mandatory)

Measurable Outcomes

  • SC-001: Finance users can configure, approve, activate, and audit a complete vendor rebate agreement in one controlled workflow without spreadsheet tracking.
  • SC-002: The system can evaluate at least 1,000,000 source lines in a batch window while preserving retryable progress and without blocking normal business posting activity.
  • SC-003: Re-running a completed calculation for the same scope without recalculation mode creates zero duplicate calculation entries and zero duplicate financial postings.
  • SC-004: 100% of posted accruals, settlements, reversals, and delta adjustments can be traced to source transaction, agreement version, rule, processing request, user action, and financial posting reference.
  • SC-005: Recalculation for retroactive agreement changes reports previous amount, revised amount, and delta for every impacted transaction, and posts only approved deltas.
  • SC-006: Settlement users can create, review, approve, and post a settlement proposal from open accrual balances, and all selected entries are closed after posting.
  • SC-007: Auditors can export complete audit evidence for a selected company and period, including agreement changes, approvals, postings, reversals, integration events, and user actions.
  • SC-008: Integration retries using the same external reference ID do not create duplicate agreements, settlement exports, audit exports, or status records.
  • SC-009: Role-based access prevents unauthorized users from completing restricted setup, approval, posting, audit export, and sensitive integration activities in all tested scenarios.
  • SC-010: Reconciliation users can identify unmatched, failed, reversed, pending, posted, and settled accruals by company, period, party, agreement, and currency.

Assumptions

  • The first releasable scope includes vendor rebate and customer incentive agreements in a shared agreement model with agreement type distinguishing behavior.
  • Standard settlement outputs include vendor credit memo, customer credit memo, journal adjustment, or custom settlement ledger entry; the specific default can be selected during planning.
  • Phase-one source documents include posted sales and purchase invoice lines, with other shipments, receipts, and historical sources added through later enhancements if required.
  • Native approval and controlled workflow behavior are required in the core product experience; external workflow tools may be integrated later.
  • Local tax and posting variations are expected, but the core specification treats them as country-specific extensions rather than hardcoded phase-one behavior.
  • Sensitive integration credentials and commercially sensitive agreement data are available only to authorized roles.
  • Historical agreement versions, posted entries, audit records, and external reference records are retained according to enterprise financial record retention policy.
  • Out-of-scope MVP items are AI-assisted forecasting, vendor self-service portal, advanced analytics semantic model, real-time data lake streaming, full marketplace packaging, and automated legal contract generation.
plan.md153 lines

Implementation Plan: Global Rebate Engine

Branch: "" | Date: 2026-06-15 | Spec: spec.md

Input: Feature specification from /specs/001-global-rebate-engine/spec.md

Summary

Implement an enterprise rebate, incentive, accrual, settlement, recalculation, audit, and reconciliation capability as a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central AL extension. The MVP uses a shared agreement model for vendor rebates and customer incentives, immutable versioning and posted entries, chunked calculation requests, approval-gated settlement and recalculation flows, and integration-facing API pages/logs. Source code will be placed under src/, use only object IDs in 50000..50249, compile against application 27.0.0.0 and runtime 16.0, and reference only symbols provided in .alpackages/.

Technical Context

Language/Version: AL, runtime 16.0, Business Central application 27.0.0.0, platform 1.0.0.0

Primary Dependencies: Microsoft Application, Base Application, Business Foundation, System Application, and System symbols from .alpackages/; no external app dependencies declared in app.json

Storage: Business Central extension tables and ledger-style append-only records; company-scoped setup and entries unless explicitly modeled as cross-company request metadata

Testing: AL test codeunits and Business Central test runner/test toolkit conventions; validation through publish/sync/test in a Business Central sandbox configured with the supplied symbols

Target Platform: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central SaaS or compatible sandbox matching application 27.0

Project Type: Business Central AL extension

Performance Goals: Evaluate at least 1,000,000 source lines per batch window using chunked requests, resumable progress, indexed lookups, SetLoadFields, and temporary calculation buffers where appropriate

Constraints: Do not modify app.json or .alpackages/; do not change app id, name, publisher, or version; all AL source files must be under src/; use only object IDs 50000..50249; extend Microsoft base objects with extensions rather than modifying base objects; avoid APIs or syntax newer than runtime 16.0 / application 27.0

Scale/Scope: Multi-company, multi-currency, multi-country rebate processing for posted sales and purchase invoice lines in MVP, with extensibility points for additional source documents and country-specific behavior

Constitution Check

GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.

The current constitution file contains placeholder principles only and defines no enforceable project-specific gates. The plan therefore applies the explicit prompt constraints and Spec Kit quality expectations as the active gates:

  • PASS: AL project constraints are captured, including application/runtime version and object ID range.
  • PASS: Existing app.json and .alpackages/ are treated as read-only inputs.
  • PASS: Source layout confines AL files to src/.
  • PASS: Financial correctness is protected through immutable posted entries, reversals, deltas, approval gates, and audit records.
  • PASS: Performance and reliability are addressed through chunked background processing, retry-safe requests, idempotency keys, and recovery logs.

Post-design re-check:

  • PASS: Research decisions resolve technical unknowns without adding dependencies outside app.json.
  • PASS: Data model uses table/page/codeunit responsibilities that fit the allowed object range.
  • PASS: Contracts document external API page behavior and idempotency requirements without requiring unavailable symbols.
  • PASS: Quickstart validates agreement, calculation, posting, settlement, recalculation, audit, and integration flows.

Project Structure

├── contracts/
